built with pdoc

vertai

VertAI - A local-first lightweight AI agent SDK for vertical-domain rapid development.

NOTE: Early development. API is unstable and not production-ready. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for the target architecture and docs/ROADMAP.md for the roadmap.

  1"""VertAI - A local-first lightweight AI agent SDK for vertical-domain rapid development.
  2
  3NOTE: Early development. API is unstable and not production-ready.
  4See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for the target architecture and docs/ROADMAP.md for the roadmap.
  5"""
  6
  7__version__ = "0.2.0"
  8

class Agent: 
 82class Agent:
 83    """A minimal tool-calling agent.
 84
 85    Args:
 86        provider: The :class:`LLMProvider` to drive the loop. Its
 87            ``generate`` / ``agenerate`` must accept ``tools=``.
 88        tools: Tools the agent can call. May be empty / ``None`` for a plain
 89            chat agent (the loop terminates after one generation).
 90        system_prompt: Optional system message prepended to every run.
 91        max_iterations: Hard upper bound on provider generations. Defaults to
 92            ``10``. Must be ``>= 1``.
 93        callbacks: Optional observability hooks (see :class:`Callback`).
 94        memory: Optional :class:`SessionMemory`. When provided, the agent
 95            seeds the conversation with prior history and writes each new
 96            message back. Full memory reliability is S9; here we only consume
 97            the stable API.
 98
 99    The agent is single-turn with respect to ``input``: each
100    :meth:`run` / :meth:`arun` call processes one user input through the
101    tool-calling loop and returns an :class:`AgentResult`. Multi-turn state
102    is carried via ``memory``.
103    """
104
105    def __init__(
106        self,
107        provider: LLMProvider,
108        tools: list[Tool] | None = None,
109        *,
110        system_prompt: str | None = None,
111        max_iterations: int = 10,
112        callbacks: list[Callback] | None = None,
113        memory: SessionMemory | None = None,
114    ) -> None:
115        if max_iterations < 1:
116            raise ValueError(
117                f"max_iterations must be >= 1, got {max_iterations}"
118            )
119        self._provider = provider
120        self._registry = ToolRegistry(tools) if tools else ToolRegistry()
121        self._system_prompt = system_prompt
122        self._max_iterations = max_iterations
123        self._callbacks = list(callbacks) if callbacks else []
124        self._memory = memory
125
126    # -- introspection ----------------------------------------------------
127
128    @property
129    def provider(self) -> LLMProvider:
130        """The backing LLM provider."""
131        return self._provider
132
133    @property
134    def registry(self) -> ToolRegistry:
135        """The agent's :class:`ToolRegistry` (mutable; callers may register
136        more tools before a run)."""
137        return self._registry
138
139    @property
140    def max_iterations(self) -> int:
141        return self._max_iterations
142
143    @property
144    def memory(self) -> SessionMemory | None:
145        return self._memory
146
147    # -- message construction --------------------------------------------
148
149    def _seed_messages(self, input: str) -> list[ChatMessage]:
150        """Build the initial message list for a run.
151
152        Order: system prompt -> memory history -> user input. Memory history
153        is replayed verbatim; we exclude ``system`` rows already stored in
154        memory (the agent's own system prompt takes precedence to avoid
155        prompt drift across runs).
156        """
157        messages: list[ChatMessage] = []
158        if self._system_prompt:
159            messages.append(
160                ChatMessage(role="system", content=self._system_prompt)
161            )
162        if self._memory is not None:
163            for prior in self._memory.get_history():
164                if prior.role == "system":
165                    continue
166                messages.append(
167                    ChatMessage(role=prior.role, content=prior.content)
168                )
169        messages.append(ChatMessage(role="user", content=input))
170        return messages
171
172    def _assistant_message(self, result: GenerateResult) -> ChatMessage:
173        """Render a GenerateResult's assistant turn as a ChatMessage.
174
175        The model's text goes into ``content``; any tool calls are summarised
176        into a compact descriptor so a subsequent generation can see that the
177        assistant asked for tools (and which ones). Provider-specific
178        representations are normalised here so the loop is provider-agnostic.
179        """
180        parts: list[str] = []
181        if result.content:
182            parts.append(result.content)
183        if result.tool_calls:
184            calls = [
185                {"id": tc.id, "name": tc.name, "arguments": tc.arguments}
186                for tc in result.tool_calls
187            ]
188            parts.append(
189                "[tool_calls] " + json.dumps(calls, ensure_ascii=False)
190            )
191        return ChatMessage(role="assistant", content="\n".join(parts))
192
193    def _record_to_memory(self, message: ChatMessage) -> None:
194        if self._memory is None:
195            return
196        # SessionMemory.add_message accepts only system/user/assistant; tool
197        # results are folded into the assistant turn text instead of stored
198        # as a separate role.
199        if message.role in ("system", "user", "assistant"):
200            self._memory.add_message(message.role, message.content)
201
202    # -- tool execution --------------------------------------------------
203
204    def _execute_tool_calls_sync(
205        self,
206        iteration: int,
207        tool_calls: list[ToolCall],
208    ) -> tuple[list[ChatMessage], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
209        """Run every tool call synchronously, returning the tool-result
210        messages and a per-call history record."""
211        new_messages: list[ChatMessage] = []
212        history: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
213        for tc in tool_calls:
214            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_tool_start", tc.name, tc.arguments)
215            # ToolRegistry.call routes through Tool.execute, which already
216            # applies the per-tool failure_error_function. A failed tool
217            # therefore yields a friendly string, not an exception -- the
218            # agent loop never crashes on a tool error.
219            result = self._registry.call(tc.name, dict(tc.arguments))
220            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_tool_end", tc.name, result)
221            record = {
222                "iteration": iteration,
223                "tool_call": {
224                    "id": tc.id,
225                    "name": tc.name,
226                    "arguments": dict(tc.arguments),
227                },
228                "result": result,
229            }
230            history.append(record)
231            msg = ChatMessage(role="tool", content=result)
232            new_messages.append(msg)
233            # Memory stores tool results as assistant text (no 'tool' role).
234            if self._memory is not None:
235                self._memory.add_message("assistant", f"[tool:{tc.name}] {result}")
236        return new_messages, history
237
238    async def _execute_tool_calls_async(
239        self,
240        iteration: int,
241        tool_calls: list[ToolCall],
242    ) -> tuple[list[ChatMessage], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
243        """Asynchronous counterpart of
244        :meth:`_execute_tool_calls_sync`."""
245        new_messages: list[ChatMessage] = []
246        history: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
247        for tc in tool_calls:
248            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_tool_start", tc.name, tc.arguments)
249            result = await self._registry.acall(tc.name, dict(tc.arguments))
250            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_tool_end", tc.name, result)
251            record = {
252                "iteration": iteration,
253                "tool_call": {
254                    "id": tc.id,
255                    "name": tc.name,
256                    "arguments": dict(tc.arguments),
257                },
258                "result": result,
259            }
260            history.append(record)
261            new_messages.append(ChatMessage(role="tool", content=result))
262            if self._memory is not None:
263                self._memory.add_message("assistant", f"[tool:{tc.name}] {result}")
264        return new_messages, history
265
266    # -- public API -------------------------------------------------------
267
268    def run(self, input: str) -> AgentResult:
269        """Run the synchronous tool-calling loop and return the result.
270
271        The loop:
272        1. Build messages (system + history + input).
273        2. Call :meth:`LLMProvider.generate` with the tool specs.
274        3. If the model returned tool calls, execute them and append the
275           results, then loop again.
276        4. Stop when the model returns no tool calls, or after
277           ``max_iterations`` generations (``truncated=True``).
278        """
279        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_start", input)
280        start = time.monotonic()
281        messages = self._seed_messages(input)
282        self._record_to_memory(ChatMessage(role="user", content=input))
283
284        tool_specs = self._registry.to_specs()
285        total_tokens = 0
286        history: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
287        iterations = 0
288        truncated = False
289        final_output = ""
290        finish_reason = "stop"
291
292        for iteration in range(1, self._max_iterations + 1):
293            iterations = iteration
294            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_start", list(messages))
295            result = self._provider.generate(messages, tools=tool_specs)
296            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_end", result)
297            total_tokens += result.total_tokens or (
298                result.prompt_tokens + result.completion_tokens
299            )
300            finish_reason = result.finish_reason
301            final_output = result.content
302
303            assistant_msg = self._assistant_message(result)
304            messages.append(assistant_msg)
305            self._record_to_memory(assistant_msg)
306
307            if not result.tool_calls:
308                # Model is done: no further tool calls requested.
309                break
310
311            tool_msgs, call_records = self._execute_tool_calls_sync(
312                iteration, result.tool_calls
313            )
314            messages.extend(tool_msgs)
315            history.extend(call_records)
316        else:
317            # The for-loop ran to exhaustion: the model kept requesting
318            # tools through max_iterations. Mark truncated.
319            truncated = True
320
321        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
322        agent_result = AgentResult(
323            final_output=final_output,
324            tool_calls_history=history,
325            iterations=iterations,
326            total_tokens=total_tokens,
327            elapsed_seconds=elapsed,
328            truncated=truncated,
329            finish_reason=finish_reason,
330        )
331        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_end", agent_result)
332        return agent_result
333
334    async def arun(self, input: str) -> AgentResult:
335        """Asynchronous variant of :meth:`run`.
336
337        Uses :meth:`LLMProvider.agenerate` and :meth:`ToolRegistry.acall`.
338        The loop semantics (termination, truncation, token accounting,
339        callbacks) are identical to the synchronous version.
340        """
341        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_start", input)
342        start = time.monotonic()
343        messages = self._seed_messages(input)
344        self._record_to_memory(ChatMessage(role="user", content=input))
345
346        tool_specs = self._registry.to_specs()
347        total_tokens = 0
348        history: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
349        iterations = 0
350        truncated = False
351        final_output = ""
352        finish_reason = "stop"
353
354        for iteration in range(1, self._max_iterations + 1):
355            iterations = iteration
356            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_start", list(messages))
357            result = await self._provider.agenerate(messages, tools=tool_specs)
358            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_end", result)
359            total_tokens += result.total_tokens or (
360                result.prompt_tokens + result.completion_tokens
361            )
362            finish_reason = result.finish_reason
363            final_output = result.content
364
365            assistant_msg = self._assistant_message(result)
366            messages.append(assistant_msg)
367            self._record_to_memory(assistant_msg)
368
369            if not result.tool_calls:
370                break
371
372            tool_msgs, call_records = await self._execute_tool_calls_async(
373                iteration, result.tool_calls
374            )
375            messages.extend(tool_msgs)
376            history.extend(call_records)
377        else:
378            truncated = True
379
380        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
381        agent_result = AgentResult(
382            final_output=final_output,
383            tool_calls_history=history,
384            iterations=iterations,
385            total_tokens=total_tokens,
386            elapsed_seconds=elapsed,
387            truncated=truncated,
388            finish_reason=finish_reason,
389        )
390        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_end", agent_result)
391        return agent_result

A minimal tool-calling agent.

Args: provider: The LLMProvider to drive the loop. Its generate / agenerate must accept tools=. tools: Tools the agent can call. May be empty / None for a plain chat agent (the loop terminates after one generation). system_prompt: Optional system message prepended to every run. max_iterations: Hard upper bound on provider generations. Defaults to 10. Must be >= 1. callbacks: Optional observability hooks (see Callback). memory: Optional SessionMemory. When provided, the agent seeds the conversation with prior history and writes each new message back. Full memory reliability is S9; here we only consume the stable API.

The agent is single-turn with respect to input: each run() / arun() call processes one user input through the tool-calling loop and returns an AgentResult. Multi-turn state is carried via memory.

Agent( provider: LLMProvider, tools: list[Tool] | None = None, *, system_prompt: str | None = None, max_iterations: int = 10, callbacks: list[Callback] | None = None, memory: SessionMemory | None = None) 
105    def __init__(
106        self,
107        provider: LLMProvider,
108        tools: list[Tool] | None = None,
109        *,
110        system_prompt: str | None = None,
111        max_iterations: int = 10,
112        callbacks: list[Callback] | None = None,
113        memory: SessionMemory | None = None,
114    ) -> None:
115        if max_iterations < 1:
116            raise ValueError(
117                f"max_iterations must be >= 1, got {max_iterations}"
118            )
119        self._provider = provider
120        self._registry = ToolRegistry(tools) if tools else ToolRegistry()
121        self._system_prompt = system_prompt
122        self._max_iterations = max_iterations
123        self._callbacks = list(callbacks) if callbacks else []
124        self._memory = memory
provider: LLMProvider 
128    @property
129    def provider(self) -> LLMProvider:
130        """The backing LLM provider."""
131        return self._provider

The backing LLM provider.

registry: ToolRegistry 
133    @property
134    def registry(self) -> ToolRegistry:
135        """The agent's :class:`ToolRegistry` (mutable; callers may register
136        more tools before a run)."""
137        return self._registry

The agent's ToolRegistry (mutable; callers may register more tools before a run).

max_iterations: int 
139    @property
140    def max_iterations(self) -> int:
141        return self._max_iterations
memory: SessionMemory | None 
143    @property
144    def memory(self) -> SessionMemory | None:
145        return self._memory
def run(self, input: str) -> AgentResult: 
268    def run(self, input: str) -> AgentResult:
269        """Run the synchronous tool-calling loop and return the result.
270
271        The loop:
272        1. Build messages (system + history + input).
273        2. Call :meth:`LLMProvider.generate` with the tool specs.
274        3. If the model returned tool calls, execute them and append the
275           results, then loop again.
276        4. Stop when the model returns no tool calls, or after
277           ``max_iterations`` generations (``truncated=True``).
278        """
279        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_start", input)
280        start = time.monotonic()
281        messages = self._seed_messages(input)
282        self._record_to_memory(ChatMessage(role="user", content=input))
283
284        tool_specs = self._registry.to_specs()
285        total_tokens = 0
286        history: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
287        iterations = 0
288        truncated = False
289        final_output = ""
290        finish_reason = "stop"
291
292        for iteration in range(1, self._max_iterations + 1):
293            iterations = iteration
294            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_start", list(messages))
295            result = self._provider.generate(messages, tools=tool_specs)
296            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_end", result)
297            total_tokens += result.total_tokens or (
298                result.prompt_tokens + result.completion_tokens
299            )
300            finish_reason = result.finish_reason
301            final_output = result.content
302
303            assistant_msg = self._assistant_message(result)
304            messages.append(assistant_msg)
305            self._record_to_memory(assistant_msg)
306
307            if not result.tool_calls:
308                # Model is done: no further tool calls requested.
309                break
310
311            tool_msgs, call_records = self._execute_tool_calls_sync(
312                iteration, result.tool_calls
313            )
314            messages.extend(tool_msgs)
315            history.extend(call_records)
316        else:
317            # The for-loop ran to exhaustion: the model kept requesting
318            # tools through max_iterations. Mark truncated.
319            truncated = True
320
321        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
322        agent_result = AgentResult(
323            final_output=final_output,
324            tool_calls_history=history,
325            iterations=iterations,
326            total_tokens=total_tokens,
327            elapsed_seconds=elapsed,
328            truncated=truncated,
329            finish_reason=finish_reason,
330        )
331        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_end", agent_result)
332        return agent_result

Run the synchronous tool-calling loop and return the result.

The loop:

  1. Build messages (system + history + input).
  2. Call LLMProvider.generate() with the tool specs.
  3. If the model returned tool calls, execute them and append the results, then loop again.
  4. Stop when the model returns no tool calls, or after max_iterations generations (truncated=True).
async def arun(self, input: str) -> AgentResult: 
334    async def arun(self, input: str) -> AgentResult:
335        """Asynchronous variant of :meth:`run`.
336
337        Uses :meth:`LLMProvider.agenerate` and :meth:`ToolRegistry.acall`.
338        The loop semantics (termination, truncation, token accounting,
339        callbacks) are identical to the synchronous version.
340        """
341        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_start", input)
342        start = time.monotonic()
343        messages = self._seed_messages(input)
344        self._record_to_memory(ChatMessage(role="user", content=input))
345
346        tool_specs = self._registry.to_specs()
347        total_tokens = 0
348        history: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
349        iterations = 0
350        truncated = False
351        final_output = ""
352        finish_reason = "stop"
353
354        for iteration in range(1, self._max_iterations + 1):
355            iterations = iteration
356            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_start", list(messages))
357            result = await self._provider.agenerate(messages, tools=tool_specs)
358            dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_llm_end", result)
359            total_tokens += result.total_tokens or (
360                result.prompt_tokens + result.completion_tokens
361            )
362            finish_reason = result.finish_reason
363            final_output = result.content
364
365            assistant_msg = self._assistant_message(result)
366            messages.append(assistant_msg)
367            self._record_to_memory(assistant_msg)
368
369            if not result.tool_calls:
370                break
371
372            tool_msgs, call_records = await self._execute_tool_calls_async(
373                iteration, result.tool_calls
374            )
375            messages.extend(tool_msgs)
376            history.extend(call_records)
377        else:
378            truncated = True
379
380        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
381        agent_result = AgentResult(
382            final_output=final_output,
383            tool_calls_history=history,
384            iterations=iterations,
385            total_tokens=total_tokens,
386            elapsed_seconds=elapsed,
387            truncated=truncated,
388            finish_reason=finish_reason,
389        )
390        dispatch(self._callbacks, "on_agent_end", agent_result)
391        return agent_result

Asynchronous variant of run().

Uses LLMProvider.agenerate() and ToolRegistry.acall(). The loop semantics (termination, truncation, token accounting, callbacks) are identical to the synchronous version.

@dataclass
class AgentResult: 
54@dataclass
55class AgentResult:
56    """Outcome of an :class:`Agent` run.
57
58    Attributes:
59        final_output: The model's final text answer (last ``content``).
60        tool_calls_history: Per-call records
61            ``{iteration, tool_call, result}`` so callers can audit the
62            sequence of tool invocations and their outputs.
63        iterations: Number of provider generations executed (capped by
64            ``max_iterations``).
65        total_tokens: Sum of ``GenerateResult.total_tokens`` across
66            iterations.
67        elapsed_seconds: Wall-clock seconds from start to finish.
68        truncated: ``True`` if the loop stopped because it hit
69            ``max_iterations`` (the model kept requesting tool calls).
70        finish_reason: The last observed ``GenerateResult.finish_reason``.
71    """
72
73    final_output: str
74    tool_calls_history: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
75    iterations: int = 0
76    total_tokens: int = 0
77    elapsed_seconds: float = 0.0
78    truncated: bool = False
79    finish_reason: str = "stop"

Outcome of an Agent run.

Attributes: final_output: The model's final text answer (last content). tool_calls_history: Per-call records {iteration, tool_call, result} so callers can audit the sequence of tool invocations and their outputs. iterations: Number of provider generations executed (capped by max_iterations). total_tokens: Sum of GenerateResult.total_tokens across iterations. elapsed_seconds: Wall-clock seconds from start to finish. truncated: True if the loop stopped because it hit max_iterations (the model kept requesting tool calls). finish_reason: The last observed GenerateResult.finish_reason.

AgentResult( final_output: str, tool_calls_history: list[dict[str, typing.Any]] = <factory>, iterations: int = 0, total_tokens: int = 0, elapsed_seconds: float = 0.0, truncated: bool = False, finish_reason: str = 'stop')
final_output: str
tool_calls_history: list[dict[str, typing.Any]]
iterations: int = 0
total_tokens: int = 0
elapsed_seconds: float = 0.0
truncated: bool = False
finish_reason: str = 'stop'
@runtime_checkable
class Callback(typing.Protocol): 
42@runtime_checkable
43class Callback(Protocol):
44    """Observability hook protocol.
45
46    Implementations may provide any subset of the hooks below. The default
47    method bodies are empty so a concrete class that subclasses this Protocol
48    (or simply duck-types it) is not forced to override every hook. Agent
49    dispatches each hook defensively (it checks the attribute is callable), so
50    objects that only implement e.g. ``on_agent_end`` work correctly.
51
52    Note on ``isinstance``: ``@runtime_checkable`` protocols only return
53    ``True`` from ``isinstance`` when *every* member is present on the
54    instance. A partial implementation therefore fails the isinstance check.
55    This is by design and matches the contract: the agent dispatches hooks
56    via ``getattr`` (see :func:`dispatch`), not via ``isinstance``, so partial
57    callbacks are fully functional at runtime regardless of the isinstance
58    result.
59    """
60
61    def on_agent_start(self, input: str) -> None:
62        """Called once at the start of :meth:`Agent.run` / :meth:`Agent.arun`."""
63
64    def on_llm_start(self, messages: list[Any]) -> None:
65        """Called before each provider ``generate`` / ``agenerate`` call."""
66
67    def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None:
68        """Called after each provider ``generate`` / ``agenerate`` call."""
69
70    def on_tool_start(self, tool_name: str, args: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
71        """Called before each tool invocation."""
72
73    def on_tool_end(self, tool_name: str, result: Any) -> None:
74        """Called after each tool invocation (result is a string from Tool)."""
75
76    def on_agent_end(self, result: Any) -> None:
77        """Called once at the end of the run with the final :class:`AgentResult`."""

Observability hook protocol.

Implementations may provide any subset of the hooks below. The default method bodies are empty so a concrete class that subclasses this Protocol (or simply duck-types it) is not forced to override every hook. Agent dispatches each hook defensively (it checks the attribute is callable), so objects that only implement e.g. on_agent_end work correctly.

Note on isinstance: @runtime_checkable protocols only return True from isinstance when every member is present on the instance. A partial implementation therefore fails the isinstance check. This is by design and matches the contract: the agent dispatches hooks via getattr (see dispatch()), not via isinstance, so partial callbacks are fully functional at runtime regardless of the isinstance result.

Callback(*args, **kwargs) 
1960def _no_init_or_replace_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
1961    cls = type(self)
1962
1963    if cls._is_protocol:
1964        raise TypeError('Protocols cannot be instantiated')
1965
1966    # Already using a custom `__init__`. No need to calculate correct
1967    # `__init__` to call. This can lead to RecursionError. See bpo-45121.
1968    if cls.__init__ is not _no_init_or_replace_init:
1969        return
1970
1971    # Initially, `__init__` of a protocol subclass is set to `_no_init_or_replace_init`.
1972    # The first instantiation of the subclass will call `_no_init_or_replace_init` which
1973    # searches for a proper new `__init__` in the MRO. The new `__init__`
1974    # replaces the subclass' old `__init__` (ie `_no_init_or_replace_init`). Subsequent
1975    # instantiation of the protocol subclass will thus use the new
1976    # `__init__` and no longer call `_no_init_or_replace_init`.
1977    for base in cls.__mro__:
1978        init = base.__dict__.get('__init__', _no_init_or_replace_init)
1979        if init is not _no_init_or_replace_init:
1980            cls.__init__ = init
1981            break
1982    else:
1983        # should not happen
1984        cls.__init__ = object.__init__
1985
1986    cls.__init__(self, *args, **kwargs)
def on_agent_start(self, input: str) -> None: 
61    def on_agent_start(self, input: str) -> None:
62        """Called once at the start of :meth:`Agent.run` / :meth:`Agent.arun`."""

Called once at the start of Agent.run() / Agent.arun().

def on_llm_start(self, messages: list[typing.Any]) -> None: 
64    def on_llm_start(self, messages: list[Any]) -> None:
65        """Called before each provider ``generate`` / ``agenerate`` call."""

Called before each provider generate / agenerate call.

def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None: 
67    def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None:
68        """Called after each provider ``generate`` / ``agenerate`` call."""

Called after each provider generate / agenerate call.

def on_tool_start(self, tool_name: str, args: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> None: 
70    def on_tool_start(self, tool_name: str, args: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
71        """Called before each tool invocation."""

Called before each tool invocation.

def on_tool_end(self, tool_name: str, result: Any) -> None: 
73    def on_tool_end(self, tool_name: str, result: Any) -> None:
74        """Called after each tool invocation (result is a string from Tool)."""

Called after each tool invocation (result is a string from Tool).

def on_agent_end(self, result: Any) -> None: 
76    def on_agent_end(self, result: Any) -> None:
77        """Called once at the end of the run with the final :class:`AgentResult`."""

Called once at the end of the run with the final AgentResult.

class LoggingCallback: 
118class LoggingCallback:
119    """A :class:`Callback` that records every event as a log record.
120
121    By default it logs at ``INFO`` level to the ``vertai.agent`` logger. A
122    custom ``logger`` can be injected (e.g. for tests capturing records). The
123    emitted records are also appended to :attr:`events` as
124    ``(hook, payload_repr)`` tuples, which is handy for assertions without
125    inspecting log capture machinery.
126    """
127
128    def __init__(self, logger: logging.Logger | None = None) -> None:
129        self._logger = logger or logging.getLogger("vertai.agent")
130        self.events: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
131
132    def _emit(self, hook: str, payload: Any) -> None:
133        self.events.append((hook, repr(payload)))
134        self._logger.info("%s: %r", hook, payload)
135
136    def on_agent_start(self, input: str) -> None:
137        self._emit("on_agent_start", input)
138
139    def on_llm_start(self, messages: list[Any]) -> None:
140        self._emit("on_llm_start", f"{len(messages)} messages")
141
142    def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None:
143        self._emit(
144            "on_llm_end",
145            f"tokens={result.total_tokens} tool_calls={len(result.tool_calls)}",
146        )
147
148    def on_tool_start(self, tool_name: str, args: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
149        self._emit("on_tool_start", f"{tool_name}({args})")
150
151    def on_tool_end(self, tool_name: str, result: Any) -> None:
152        self._emit("on_tool_end", f"{tool_name} -> {result!r}")
153
154    def on_agent_end(self, result: Any) -> None:
155        # Avoid importing AgentResult at module load (circular: agent imports
156        # callbacks). A duck-typed repr is sufficient for the log line.
157        self._emit("on_agent_end", result)

A Callback that records every event as a log record.

By default it logs at INFO level to the vertai.agent logger. A custom logger can be injected (e.g. for tests capturing records). The emitted records are also appended to events as (hook, payload_repr) tuples, which is handy for assertions without inspecting log capture machinery.

LoggingCallback(logger: logging.Logger | None = None) 
128    def __init__(self, logger: logging.Logger | None = None) -> None:
129        self._logger = logger or logging.getLogger("vertai.agent")
130        self.events: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
events: list[tuple[str, str]]
def on_agent_start(self, input: str) -> None: 
136    def on_agent_start(self, input: str) -> None:
137        self._emit("on_agent_start", input)
def on_llm_start(self, messages: list[typing.Any]) -> None: 
139    def on_llm_start(self, messages: list[Any]) -> None:
140        self._emit("on_llm_start", f"{len(messages)} messages")
def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None: 
142    def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None:
143        self._emit(
144            "on_llm_end",
145            f"tokens={result.total_tokens} tool_calls={len(result.tool_calls)}",
146        )
def on_tool_start(self, tool_name: str, args: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> None: 
148    def on_tool_start(self, tool_name: str, args: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
149        self._emit("on_tool_start", f"{tool_name}({args})")
def on_tool_end(self, tool_name: str, result: Any) -> None: 
151    def on_tool_end(self, tool_name: str, result: Any) -> None:
152        self._emit("on_tool_end", f"{tool_name} -> {result!r}")
def on_agent_end(self, result: Any) -> None: 
154    def on_agent_end(self, result: Any) -> None:
155        # Avoid importing AgentResult at module load (circular: agent imports
156        # callbacks). A duck-typed repr is sufficient for the log line.
157        self._emit("on_agent_end", result)
class TokenCountCallback: 
160class TokenCountCallback:
161    """A :class:`Callback` that accumulates token usage across iterations.
162
163    Sums :attr:`GenerateResult.total_tokens` (falling back to
164    ``prompt_tokens + completion_tokens`` when ``total_tokens`` is unset) from
165    every :meth:`on_llm_end` call. Exposes the running total via
166    :attr:`total_tokens` and per-iteration counts via :attr:`per_iteration`.
167
168    This lets an application report cost/usage without inspecting the
169    :class:`AgentResult` directly (e.g. when streaming through a callback
170    pipeline).
171    """
172
173    def __init__(self) -> None:
174        self.total_tokens: int = 0
175        self.prompt_tokens: int = 0
176        self.completion_tokens: int = 0
177        self.per_iteration: list[int] = []
178
179    def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None:
180        total = result.total_tokens or (
181            result.prompt_tokens + result.completion_tokens
182        )
183        self.total_tokens += total
184        self.prompt_tokens += result.prompt_tokens
185        self.completion_tokens += result.completion_tokens
186        self.per_iteration.append(total)
187
188    def reset(self) -> Self:
189        """Reset accumulated counters. Returns ``self`` for chaining."""
190        self.total_tokens = 0
191        self.prompt_tokens = 0
192        self.completion_tokens = 0
193        self.per_iteration = []
194        return self

A Callback that accumulates token usage across iterations.

Sums GenerateResult.total_tokens (falling back to prompt_tokens + completion_tokens when total_tokens is unset) from every on_llm_end() call. Exposes the running total via total_tokens and per-iteration counts via per_iteration.

This lets an application report cost/usage without inspecting the AgentResult directly (e.g. when streaming through a callback pipeline).

total_tokens: int
prompt_tokens: int
completion_tokens: int
per_iteration: list[int]
def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None: 
179    def on_llm_end(self, result: GenerateResult) -> None:
180        total = result.total_tokens or (
181            result.prompt_tokens + result.completion_tokens
182        )
183        self.total_tokens += total
184        self.prompt_tokens += result.prompt_tokens
185        self.completion_tokens += result.completion_tokens
186        self.per_iteration.append(total)
def reset(self) -> Self: 
188    def reset(self) -> Self:
189        """Reset accumulated counters. Returns ``self`` for chaining."""
190        self.total_tokens = 0
191        self.prompt_tokens = 0
192        self.completion_tokens = 0
193        self.per_iteration = []
194        return self

Reset accumulated counters. Returns self for chaining.

ANTHROPIC_API_VERSION = '2023-06-01'
class AnthropicProvider(vertai.LLMProvider): 
647class AnthropicProvider(LLMProvider):
648    """Adapter for the Anthropic Messages API (``/v1/messages``).
649
650    DeepSeek's Anthropic-compatible endpoint reuses this adapter via
651    :class:`DeepSeekProvider`.
652    """
653
654    def _headers(self) -> dict[str, str]:
655        return {
656            "Content-Type": "application/json",
657            "x-api-key": self.config.api_key or "",
658            "anthropic-version": ANTHROPIC_API_VERSION,
659        }
660
661    def _endpoint(self) -> str:
662        return f"{self.config.base_url.rstrip('/')}/v1/messages"
663
664    def _build_payload(
665        self,
666        messages: list[ChatMessage],
667        *,
668        stream: bool,
669        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None,
670        system_override: str | None,
671        **kwargs: Any,
672    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
673        config = self.config
674        system, rest = self._split_system(messages)
675        if system_override is not None:
676            system = system_override
677        elif system is None and config.system_prompt is not None:
678            system = config.system_prompt
679
680        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
681            "model": config.model,
682            "max_tokens": int(kwargs.get("max_tokens", config.max_tokens)),
683            "messages": [{"role": m.role, "content": m.content} for m in rest],
684            "stream": stream,
685        }
686        if system:
687            payload["system"] = system
688
689        temperature = kwargs.get("temperature", config.temperature)
690        if temperature is not None:
691            payload["temperature"] = float(temperature)
692        top_p = kwargs.get("top_p", config.top_p)
693        if top_p is not None:
694            payload["top_p"] = float(top_p)
695        top_k = kwargs.get("top_k", config.top_k)
696        if top_k is not None:
697            payload["top_k"] = int(top_k)
698
699        if tools:
700            payload["tools"] = [
701                {
702                    "name": t.name,
703                    "description": t.description,
704                    "input_schema": t.input_schema,
705                }
706                for t in tools
707            ]
708        return payload
709
710    def _ensure_api_key(self) -> None:
711        if not self.config.api_key:
712            raise RuntimeError(
713                "Anthropic-compatible API requires an API key. Provide it via "
714                "the VERTAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable or "
715                "LLMConfig(api_key=...)."
716            )
717
718    # -- sync -------------------------------------------------------------
719
720    def generate(
721        self,
722        messages: list[ChatMessage],
723        *,
724        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
725        **kwargs: Any,
726    ) -> GenerateResult:
727        self._ensure_api_key()
728        coerced = self._coerce_messages(messages)
729        payload = self._build_payload(
730            coerced, stream=False, tools=tools, system_override=None, **kwargs
731        )
732        try:
733            response = self._get_sync_client().post(
734                self._endpoint(), json=payload, headers=self._headers()
735            )
736            response.raise_for_status()
737        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
738            raise RuntimeError(
739                f"Cannot connect to API service ({self.config.base_url}). "
740                "Check the network and base_url configuration."
741            ) from e
742        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
743            raise RuntimeError(
744                f"Anthropic API request failed: {e.response.status_code}\n"
745                f"Response: {e.response.text}"
746            ) from e
747        return _parse_anthropic_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
748
749    def stream(
750        self,
751        messages: list[ChatMessage],
752        *,
753        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
754        **kwargs: Any,
755    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
756        self._ensure_api_key()
757        coerced = self._coerce_messages(messages)
758        payload = self._build_payload(
759            coerced, stream=True, tools=tools, system_override=None, **kwargs
760        )
761        try:
762            with self._get_sync_client().stream(
763                "POST",
764                self._endpoint(),
765                json=payload,
766                headers=self._headers(),
767            ) as response:
768                yield from self._iter_anthropic_stream(response)
769        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
770            raise RuntimeError(
771                f"Cannot connect to API service ({self.config.base_url}). "
772                "Check the network and base_url configuration."
773            ) from e
774
775    @staticmethod
776    def _iter_anthropic_stream(
777        response: httpx.Response,
778    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
779        """Parse an Anthropic SSE stream into StreamEvent values."""
780        # Accumulate tool-use blocks (id/name set on start, args accumulate).
781        tool_blocks: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {}
782        for data in _iter_sse_lines_sync(response):
783            event_type = str(data.get("type", ""))
784            if event_type == "content_block_start":
785                block = data.get("content_block", {})
786                if block.get("type") == "tool_use":
787                    index = int(data.get("index", 0))
788                    tool_blocks[index] = {
789                        "id": str(block.get("id", "")),
790                        "name": str(block.get("name", "")),
791                        "args": "",
792                    }
793            elif event_type == "content_block_delta":
794                delta = data.get("delta", {})
795                delta_type = delta.get("type", "")
796                if delta_type == "text_delta":
797                    yield TextDeltaEvent(text=str(delta.get("text", "")))
798                elif delta_type == "input_json_delta":
799                    index = int(data.get("index", 0))
800                    if index in tool_blocks:
801                        tool_blocks[index]["args"] += str(
802                            delta.get("partial_json", "")
803                        )
804            elif event_type == "content_block_stop":
805                index = int(data.get("index", 0))
806                block = tool_blocks.pop(index, None)
807                if block is not None:
808                    yield ToolUseEvent(
809                        id=block["id"],
810                        name=block["name"],
811                        arguments=block["args"],
812                    )
813            elif event_type == "message_stop":
814                yield DoneEvent(finish_reason="stop")
815                break
816            elif event_type == "message_delta":
817                delta = data.get("delta", {})
818                if delta.get("stop_reason"):
819                    # message_stop follows; record reason for terminal event.
820                    pass
821
822    # -- async ------------------------------------------------------------
823
824    async def agenerate(
825        self,
826        messages: list[ChatMessage],
827        *,
828        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
829        **kwargs: Any,
830    ) -> GenerateResult:
831        self._ensure_api_key()
832        coerced = self._coerce_messages(messages)
833        payload = self._build_payload(
834            coerced, stream=False, tools=tools, system_override=None, **kwargs
835        )
836        client = await self._get_async_client()
837        try:
838            response = await client.post(
839                self._endpoint(), json=payload, headers=self._headers()
840            )
841            response.raise_for_status()
842        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
843            raise RuntimeError(
844                f"Cannot connect to API service ({self.config.base_url}). "
845                "Check the network and base_url configuration."
846            ) from e
847        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
848            raise RuntimeError(
849                f"Anthropic API request failed: {e.response.status_code}\n"
850                f"Response: {e.response.text}"
851            ) from e
852        return _parse_anthropic_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
853
854    async def astream(
855        self,
856        messages: list[ChatMessage],
857        *,
858        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
859        **kwargs: Any,
860    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
861        self._ensure_api_key()
862        coerced = self._coerce_messages(messages)
863        payload = self._build_payload(
864            coerced, stream=True, tools=tools, system_override=None, **kwargs
865        )
866        client = await self._get_async_client()
867        try:
868            async with client.stream(
869                "POST",
870                self._endpoint(),
871                json=payload,
872                headers=self._headers(),
873            ) as response:
874                async for event in self._iter_anthropic_stream_async(response):
875                    yield event
876        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
877            raise RuntimeError(
878                f"Cannot connect to API service ({self.config.base_url}). "
879                "Check the network and base_url configuration."
880            ) from e
881
882    @staticmethod
883    async def _iter_anthropic_stream_async(
884        response: httpx.Response,
885    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
886        tool_blocks: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {}
887        async for data in _iter_sse_lines_async(response):
888            event_type = str(data.get("type", ""))
889            if event_type == "content_block_start":
890                block = data.get("content_block", {})
891                if block.get("type") == "tool_use":
892                    index = int(data.get("index", 0))
893                    tool_blocks[index] = {
894                        "id": str(block.get("id", "")),
895                        "name": str(block.get("name", "")),
896                        "args": "",
897                    }
898            elif event_type == "content_block_delta":
899                delta = data.get("delta", {})
900                delta_type = delta.get("type", "")
901                if delta_type == "text_delta":
902                    yield TextDeltaEvent(text=str(delta.get("text", "")))
903                elif delta_type == "input_json_delta":
904                    index = int(data.get("index", 0))
905                    if index in tool_blocks:
906                        tool_blocks[index]["args"] += str(
907                            delta.get("partial_json", "")
908                        )
909            elif event_type == "content_block_stop":
910                index = int(data.get("index", 0))
911                block = tool_blocks.pop(index, None)
912                if block is not None:
913                    yield ToolUseEvent(
914                        id=block["id"],
915                        name=block["name"],
916                        arguments=block["args"],
917                    )
918            elif event_type == "message_stop":
919                yield DoneEvent(finish_reason="stop")
920                break

Adapter for the Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages).

DeepSeek's Anthropic-compatible endpoint reuses this adapter via DeepSeekProvider.

def generate( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult: 
720    def generate(
721        self,
722        messages: list[ChatMessage],
723        *,
724        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
725        **kwargs: Any,
726    ) -> GenerateResult:
727        self._ensure_api_key()
728        coerced = self._coerce_messages(messages)
729        payload = self._build_payload(
730            coerced, stream=False, tools=tools, system_override=None, **kwargs
731        )
732        try:
733            response = self._get_sync_client().post(
734                self._endpoint(), json=payload, headers=self._headers()
735            )
736            response.raise_for_status()
737        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
738            raise RuntimeError(
739                f"Cannot connect to API service ({self.config.base_url}). "
740                "Check the network and base_url configuration."
741            ) from e
742        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
743            raise RuntimeError(
744                f"Anthropic API request failed: {e.response.status_code}\n"
745                f"Response: {e.response.text}"
746            ) from e
747        return _parse_anthropic_response(response.json(), self.config.model)

752        *,

754        **kwargs: Any,
755    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
756        self._ensure_api_key()
757        coerced = self._coerce_messages(messages)
758        payload = self._build_payload(
759            coerced, stream=True, tools=tools, system_override=None, **kwargs
760        )
761        try:
762            with self._get_sync_client().stream(
763                "POST",
764                self._endpoint(),
765                json=payload,
766                headers=self._headers(),
767            ) as response:
768                yield from self._iter_anthropic_stream(response)
769        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
770            raise RuntimeError(
771                f"Cannot connect to API service ({self.config.base_url}). "
772                "Check the network and base_url configuration."
773            ) from e

830    ) -> GenerateResult:

833        payload = self._build_payload(

835        )
836        client = await self._get_async_client()

async def astream( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> AsyncIterator[Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]]: 
854    async def astream(
855        self,
856        messages: list[ChatMessage],
857        *,
858        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
859        **kwargs: Any,
860    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
861        self._ensure_api_key()

864            coerced, stream=True, tools=tools, system_override=None, **kwargs
865        )

868            async with client.stream(

870                self._endpoint(),
871                json=payload,
872                headers=self._headers(),
873            ) as response:

875                    yield event
876        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
877            raise RuntimeError(
878                f"Cannot connect to API service ({self.config.base_url}). "
879                "Check the network and base_url configuration."
880            ) from e

Async streaming generation.

Concrete adapters implement this as async def (an async generator); the ABC declares it without async so the override is type-compatible (see mypy's async-iterator override rules).

class ChatMessage(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
 82class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
 83    """A single chat message.
 84
 85    ``role`` is one of ``system``/``user``/``assistant``/``tool``. Content is
 86    plain text; assistant messages that contained tool calls are represented
 87    via the separate :class:`ToolCall` list on :class:`GenerateResult`.
 88    """
 89
 90    role: str = Field(description="Message role: system/user/assistant/tool")
 91    content: str = Field(description="Message content")
 92
 93    model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)
 94
 95    @classmethod
 96    def coerce(cls, message: ChatMessage | dict[str, Any]) -> ChatMessage:
 97        """Coerce a dict ``{"role", "content"}`` or a ``ChatMessage`` into a
 98        ``ChatMessage``. Raises ``TypeError`` for unsupported types so that
 99        ``chat()`` never silently misinterprets input.
100        """
101        if isinstance(message, ChatMessage):
102            return message
103        if isinstance(message, dict):
104            return cls(
105                role=str(message["role"]),
106                content=str(message["content"]),
107            )
108        raise TypeError(
109            f"Unsupported message type {type(message).__name__}; "
110            "expected ChatMessage or dict with 'role' and 'content'."
111        )

A single chat message.

role is one of system/user/assistant/tool. Content is plain text; assistant messages that contained tool calls are represented via the separate ToolCall list on GenerateResult.

role: str = PydanticUndefined

Message role: system/user/assistant/tool

content: str = PydanticUndefined

Message content

@classmethod
def coerce( cls, message: ChatMessage | dict[str, typing.Any]) -> ChatMessage: 
 95    @classmethod
 96    def coerce(cls, message: ChatMessage | dict[str, Any]) -> ChatMessage:
 97        """Coerce a dict ``{"role", "content"}`` or a ``ChatMessage`` into a
 98        ``ChatMessage``. Raises ``TypeError`` for unsupported types so that
 99        ``chat()`` never silently misinterprets input.
100        """
101        if isinstance(message, ChatMessage):
102            return message
103        if isinstance(message, dict):
104            return cls(
105                role=str(message["role"]),
106                content=str(message["content"]),
107            )
108        raise TypeError(
109            f"Unsupported message type {type(message).__name__}; "
110            "expected ChatMessage or dict with 'role' and 'content'."
111        )

Coerce a dict {"role", "content"} or a ChatMessage into a ChatMessage. Raises TypeError for unsupported types so that chat() never silently misinterprets input.

class DeepSeekProvider(vertai.AnthropicProvider): 
923class DeepSeekProvider(AnthropicProvider):
924    """DeepSeek adapter. DeepSeek exposes an Anthropic-compatible Messages API,
925    so this is a thin specialization of :class:`AnthropicProvider`. The
926    reasoning ("thinking") content blocks are preserved in
927    ``GenerateResult.metadata['thinking']``.
928    """

DeepSeek adapter. DeepSeek exposes an Anthropic-compatible Messages API, so this is a thin specialization of AnthropicProvider. The reasoning ("thinking") content blocks are preserved in GenerateResult.metadata['thinking'].

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DoneEvent: 
191@dataclass(frozen=True)
192class DoneEvent:
193    """Signals stream completion.
194
195    ``tool_calls`` carries fully-parsed tool calls when the stream contained
196    tool-use blocks (so non-incremental consumers can read them from the
197    terminal event).
198    """
199
200    finish_reason: str
201    tool_calls: list[ToolCall] = field(default_factory=list)

Signals stream completion.

tool_calls carries fully-parsed tool calls when the stream contained tool-use blocks (so non-incremental consumers can read them from the terminal event).

DoneEvent( finish_reason: str, tool_calls: list[ToolCall] = <factory>)
finish_reason: str
tool_calls: list[ToolCall]
class GenerateResult(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
146class GenerateResult(BaseModel):
147    """Result of a non-streaming generation."""
148
149    content: str = Field(description="Generated text content")
150    model: str = Field(description="Model name that produced the result")
151    prompt_tokens: int = Field(default=0, description="Input token count")
152    completion_tokens: int = Field(default=0, description="Output token count")
153    total_tokens: int = Field(default=0, description="Total token count")
154    finish_reason: str = Field(default="stop", description="Stop reason")
155    tool_calls: list[ToolCall] = Field(
156        default_factory=list, description="Tool calls requested by the model"
157    )
158    metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(
159        default_factory=dict, description="Extra provider-specific metadata"
160    )
161
162    model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)

Result of a non-streaming generation.

content: str = PydanticUndefined

Generated text content

model: str = PydanticUndefined

Model name that produced the result

prompt_tokens: int = 0

Input token count

completion_tokens: int = 0

Output token count

total_tokens: int = 0

Total token count

finish_reason: str = 'stop'

Stop reason

tool_calls: list[ToolCall] = PydanticUndefined

Tool calls requested by the model

metadata: dict[str, typing.Any] = PydanticUndefined

Extra provider-specific metadata

class LLMConfig(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
213class LLMConfig(BaseModel):
214    """Configuration for an LLM provider.
215
216    API key is injected from the ``VERTAI_API_KEY`` / ``ANTHROPIC_API_KEY``
217    environment variables via ``model_validator(mode="before")`` (not by
218    overriding ``__init__``).
219
220    Sentinel sampling parameters (``temperature``/``top_p``/``top_k``) default
221    to ``None``: providers only include them in the payload when explicitly
222    set, rather than guessing with hardcoded ``0.7``/``0.9``/``40``.
223
224    Example:
225        # Default local model (Ollama)
226        config = LLMConfig()
227
228        # Anthropic-compatible API (e.g. DeepSeek)
229        config = LLMConfig(
230            provider=ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
231            base_url="https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
232            api_key="sk-xxx",
233            model="deepseek-chat",
234        )
235
236        # Real OpenAI
237        config = LLMConfig(
238            provider=ModelProvider.OPENAI,
239            base_url="https://api.openai.com/v1",
240            api_key="sk-xxx",
241            model="gpt-4o-mini",
242        )
243    """
244
245    model: str = Field(default="llama3.2", description="Model name")
246    provider: ModelProvider = Field(
247        default=ModelProvider.OLLAMA, description="Model provider"
248    )
249    base_url: str = Field(
250        default="http://localhost:11434", description="API base URL"
251    )
252    api_key: str | None = Field(default=None, description="API key")
253    temperature: float | None = Field(
254        default=None, ge=0.0, le=2.0, description="Sampling temperature"
255    )
256    max_tokens: int = Field(default=4096, ge=1, description="Max output tokens")
257    top_p: float | None = Field(
258        default=None, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Top-p sampling"
259    )
260    top_k: int | None = Field(
261        default=None, ge=0, description="Top-k sampling"
262    )
263    repeat_penalty: float | None = Field(
264        default=None, ge=1.0, description="Repeat penalty (Ollama)"
265    )
266    seed: int | None = Field(default=None, description="Random seed")
267    system_prompt: str | None = Field(default=None, description="System prompt")
268    timeout: float = Field(
269        default=120.0, ge=1.0, description="Request timeout in seconds"
270    )
271
272    model_config = ConfigDict(
273        extra="forbid",
274        validate_default=True,
275    )
276
277    @model_validator(mode="before")
278    @classmethod
279    def _inject_env_api_key(cls, data: Any) -> Any:
280        """Inject API key from environment if not explicitly provided.
281
282        Priority: explicit ``api_key`` arg > ``VERTAI_API_KEY`` >
283        ``ANTHROPIC_API_KEY``.
284        """
285        if not isinstance(data, dict):
286            return data
287        if data.get("api_key") is None:
288            env_key = os.environ.get("VERTAI_API_KEY") or os.environ.get(
289                "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"
290            )
291            if env_key:
292                data["api_key"] = env_key
293        return data
294
295    @model_validator(mode="after")
296    def _validate_model_name(self) -> LLMConfig:
297        """Reject model names with characters that could enable injection."""
298        if not re.match(_MODEL_NAME_PATTERN, self.model):
299            raise ValueError(
300                "Model name contains illegal characters. "
301                "Only letters, digits, dot (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-) "
302                "are allowed."
303            )
304        return self
305
306    def is_anthropic_compatible(self) -> bool:
307        """Whether the provider speaks the Anthropic Messages API."""
308        return self.provider in (
309            ModelProvider.ANTHROPIC,
310            ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
311        )
312
313    def is_openai_compatible(self) -> bool:
314        """Whether the provider speaks the OpenAI Chat Completions API.
315
316        Note: ``CUSTOM`` is treated as Anthropic-compatible by default; callers
317        who want OpenAI semantics must set ``provider=ModelProvider.OPENAI``.
318        """
319        return self.provider is ModelProvider.OPENAI

Configuration for an LLM provider.

API key is injected from the VERTAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variables via model_validator(mode="before") (not by overriding __init__).

Sentinel sampling parameters (temperature/top_p/top_k) default to None: providers only include them in the payload when explicitly set, rather than guessing with hardcoded 0.7/0.9/40.

Example: # Default local model (Ollama) config = LLMConfig()

# Anthropic-compatible API (e.g. DeepSeek)
config = LLMConfig(
    provider=ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
    base_url="https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
    api_key="sk-xxx",
    model="deepseek-chat",
)

# Real OpenAI
config = LLMConfig(
    provider=ModelProvider.OPENAI,
    base_url="https://api.openai.com/v1",
    api_key="sk-xxx",
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
)
model: str = 'llama3.2'

Model name

provider: ModelProvider = <ModelProvider.OLLAMA: 'ollama'>

Model provider

base_url: str = 'http://localhost:11434'

API base URL

api_key: str | None = None

API key

temperature: float | None = None

Sampling temperature

max_tokens: int = 4096

Max output tokens

top_p: float | None = None

Top-p sampling

top_k: int | None = None

Top-k sampling

repeat_penalty: float | None = None

Repeat penalty (Ollama)

seed: int | None = None

Random seed

system_prompt: str | None = None

System prompt

timeout: float = 120.0

Request timeout in seconds

def is_anthropic_compatible(self) -> bool: 
306    def is_anthropic_compatible(self) -> bool:
307        """Whether the provider speaks the Anthropic Messages API."""
308        return self.provider in (
309            ModelProvider.ANTHROPIC,
310            ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
311        )

Whether the provider speaks the Anthropic Messages API.

def is_openai_compatible(self) -> bool: 
313    def is_openai_compatible(self) -> bool:
314        """Whether the provider speaks the OpenAI Chat Completions API.
315
316        Note: ``CUSTOM`` is treated as Anthropic-compatible by default; callers
317        who want OpenAI semantics must set ``provider=ModelProvider.OPENAI``.
318        """
319        return self.provider is ModelProvider.OPENAI

Whether the provider speaks the OpenAI Chat Completions API.

Note: CUSTOM is treated as Anthropic-compatible by default; callers who want OpenAI semantics must set provider=ModelProvider.OPENAI.

class LLMEngine: 
178class LLMEngine:
179    """Backward-compatible LLM engine facade.
180
181    Delegates to a :class:`LLMProvider` selected via
182    :func:`create_provider`. The legacy single-prompt API (``generate(prompt,
183    system_prompt)``) and the ``chat``/``stream``/``chat_stream`` methods are
184    preserved; they coerce to :class:`ChatMessage` internally.
185
186    Example:
187        # Default local model (Ollama)
188        engine = LLMEngine()
189        result = engine.generate("Hello!")
190        print(result.content)
191
192        # DeepSeek (Anthropic-compatible)
193        config = LLMConfig(
194            provider=ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
195            base_url="https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
196            api_key="sk-xxx",
197            model="deepseek-chat",
198        )
199        engine = LLMEngine(config)
200
201        # Multi-turn chat (dict or ChatMessage both accepted)
202        messages = [
203            {"role": "user", "content": "Hi"},
204            ChatMessage(role="assistant", content="Hello!"),
205            ChatMessage(role="user", "content": "Who are you?"),
206        ]
207        result = engine.chat(messages)
208    """
209
210    _MODEL_NAME_PATTERN = r"^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$"
211
212    def __init__(self, config: LLMConfig | None = None) -> None:
213        self.config = config or LLMConfig()
214        self._detector = OllamaDetector(self.config.base_url)
215        self._provider = create_provider(self.config)
216
217    # -- provider access --------------------------------------------------
218
219    @property
220    def provider(self) -> LLMProvider:
221        """The underlying :class:`LLMProvider` instance."""
222        return self._provider
223
224    @property
225    def current_model(self) -> str:
226        """The configured model name."""
227        return self.config.model
228
229    @property
230    def is_available(self) -> bool:
231        """Whether the service is available.
232
233        For Anthropic-compatible/OpenAI providers, availability means an API
234        key is configured; for Ollama it means the service is running.
235        """
236        if self.config.provider is ModelProvider.OLLAMA:
237            return self._detector.is_running()
238        return self.config.api_key is not None
239
240    # -- model management (Ollama convenience) ----------------------------
241
242    def list_models(self) -> list[LLMModelInfo]:
243        """List available Ollama models."""
244        return self._detector.list_models()
245
246    def switch_model(self, model_name: str) -> bool:
247        """Switch the active model (Ollama only).
248
249        Raises ``RuntimeError`` if the Ollama service is not running and
250        ``ValueError`` if the model is not found or the name is invalid.
251        """
252        if not re.match(self._MODEL_NAME_PATTERN, model_name):
253            raise ValueError(
254                "Model name contains illegal characters. "
255                "Only letters, digits, dot (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-) "
256                "are allowed."
257            )
258        if not self._detector.is_running():
259            raise RuntimeError(
260                "Ollama service is not running. Start it with:\n"
261                "  1. Install: https://ollama.ai\n"
262                "  2. Run: ollama serve\n"
263                "  3. Pull a model: ollama pull <model>"
264            )
265        model_info = self._detector.get_model_info(model_name)
266        if model_info is None:
267            available = [m.name for m in self.list_models()]
268            if available:
269                raise ValueError(
270                    "Requested model not found.\n"
271                    f"Available models: {', '.join(available)}\n"
272                    "Use 'ollama pull' to fetch new models."
273                )
274            raise ValueError(
275                "Requested model not found and no models are available locally.\n"
276                "Use 'ollama pull' to fetch a model."
277            )
278        # Mutate config in place so the provider picks up the new model.
279        self.config = self.config.model_copy(update={"model": model_name})
280        self._provider = create_provider(self.config)
281        return True
282
283    # -- generation (legacy single-prompt API) ----------------------------
284
285    def generate(
286        self,
287        prompt: str,
288        system_prompt: str | None = None,
289        **kwargs: Any,
290    ) -> GenerateResult:
291        """Generate text from a single prompt (legacy convenience API).
292
293        Args:
294            prompt: User prompt.
295            system_prompt: Optional system prompt (overrides config).
296            **kwargs: Extra generation parameters forwarded to the provider.
297        """
298        self._ensure_available()
299        messages: list[ChatMessage] = []
300        sys_text = system_prompt or self.config.system_prompt
301        if sys_text:
302            messages.append(ChatMessage(role="system", content=sys_text))
303        messages.append(ChatMessage(role="user", content=prompt))
304        return self._provider.generate(messages, **kwargs)
305
306    def stream(
307        self,
308        prompt: str,
309        system_prompt: str | None = None,
310        **kwargs: Any,
311    ) -> Iterator[str]:
312        """Stream text from a single prompt (yields ``str`` for compat).
313
314        For typed ``StreamEvent`` values, use ``self.provider.stream(...)``
315        directly.
316        """
317        self._ensure_available()
318        messages: list[ChatMessage] = []
319        sys_text = system_prompt or self.config.system_prompt
320        if sys_text:
321            messages.append(ChatMessage(role="system", content=sys_text))
322        messages.append(ChatMessage(role="user", content=prompt))
323        for event in self._provider.stream(messages, **kwargs):
324            if isinstance(event, TextDeltaEvent):
325                yield event.text
326
327    # -- chat -------------------------------------------------------------
328
329    def chat(
330        self,
331        messages: Union[list[ChatMessage], list[dict[str, Any]]],
332        **kwargs: Any,
333    ) -> GenerateResult:
334        """Multi-turn chat.
335
336        ``messages`` accepts ``list[ChatMessage]`` or ``list[dict]`` (each dict
337        must have ``role`` and ``content``); both are coerced uniformly via
338        :meth:`ChatMessage.coerce`.
339        """
340        self._ensure_available()
341        coerced = [ChatMessage.coerce(m) for m in messages]
342        return self._provider.generate(coerced, **kwargs)
343
344    def chat_stream(
345        self,
346        messages: Union[list[ChatMessage], list[dict[str, Any]]],
347        **kwargs: Any,
348    ) -> Iterator[str]:
349        """Stream a multi-turn chat (yields ``str`` for compat)."""
350        self._ensure_available()
351        coerced = [ChatMessage.coerce(m) for m in messages]
352        for event in self._provider.stream(coerced, **kwargs):
353            if isinstance(event, TextDeltaEvent):
354                yield event.text
355
356    # -- embeddings (Ollama / OpenAI-style; S3 will refactor) -------------
357
358    def embeddings(self, text: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
359        """Get embeddings.
360
361        For Ollama: uses ``/api/embeddings``. For Anthropic-compatible/OpenAI
362        providers: uses ``/v1/embeddings`` (OpenAI format). S3 introduces a
363        dedicated :class:`EmbeddingProvider` abstraction; this method is kept
364        only for backward compatibility.
365        """
366        self._ensure_available()
367        texts = [text] if isinstance(text, str) else text
368        if self.config.provider is ModelProvider.OLLAMA:
369            return self._embeddings_ollama(texts)
370        return self._embeddings_cloud(texts)
371
372    def _embeddings_ollama(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
373        results: list[list[float]] = []
374        client = self._get_sync_client()
375        for t in texts:
376            response = client.post(
377                f"{self.config.base_url.rstrip('/')}/api/embeddings",
378                json={"model": self.config.model, "prompt": t},
379            )
380            response.raise_for_status()
381            data = response.json()
382            results.append(list(data.get("embedding", [])))
383        return results
384
385    def _embeddings_cloud(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
386        headers: dict[str, str]
387        if self.config.is_openai_compatible():
388            headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.config.api_key or ''}"}
389        else:
390            headers = {
391                "x-api-key": self.config.api_key or "",
392                "anthropic-version": ANTHROPIC_API_VERSION,
393            }
394        results: list[list[float]] = []
395        client = self._get_sync_client()
396        for t in texts:
397            response = client.post(
398                f"{self.config.base_url.rstrip('/')}/v1/embeddings",
399                json={"input": t, "model": self.config.model},
400                headers=headers,
401            )
402            response.raise_for_status()
403            data = response.json()
404            embedding_data = data.get("data", [])
405            if embedding_data:
406                results.append(list(embedding_data[0].get("embedding", [])))
407            else:
408                results.append([])
409        return results
410
411    # -- internals --------------------------------------------------------
412
413    def _get_sync_client(self) -> httpx.Client:
414        # Reuse the provider's sync client for embeddings (shared lifecycle).
415        return self._provider._get_sync_client()
416
417    def _ensure_available(self) -> None:
418        """Ensure the configured service is usable."""
419        if self.config.provider is ModelProvider.OLLAMA:
420            if not self._detector.is_running():
421                raise RuntimeError(
422                    "Ollama service is not running.\n\n"
423                    "Steps:\n"
424                    "  1. Install Ollama: https://ollama.ai\n"
425                    "  2. Start the service: ollama serve\n"
426                    "  3. Pull a model: ollama pull <model>\n"
427                    "Check the base_url configuration."
428                )
429            return
430        if not self.config.api_key:
431            raise RuntimeError(
432                "API requires an API key. Provide it via the "
433                "VERTAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable or "
434                "LLMConfig(api_key=...)."
435            )
436
437    def close(self) -> None:
438        """Close the engine and underlying clients."""
439        self._provider.close()
440        self._detector.close()
441
442    def __enter__(self) -> LLMEngine:
443        return self
444
445    def __exit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
446        self.close()

Backward-compatible LLM engine facade.

Delegates to a LLMProvider selected via create_provider(). The legacy single-prompt API (generate(prompt, system_prompt)) and the chat/stream/chat_stream methods are preserved; they coerce to ChatMessage internally.

Example: # Default local model (Ollama) engine = LLMEngine() result = engine.generate("Hello!") print(result.content)

# DeepSeek (Anthropic-compatible)
config = LLMConfig(
    provider=ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
    base_url="https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic",
    api_key="sk-xxx",
    model="deepseek-chat",
)
engine = LLMEngine(config)

# Multi-turn chat (dict or ChatMessage both accepted)
messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "Hi"},
    ChatMessage(role="assistant", content="Hello!"),
    ChatMessage(role="user", "content": "Who are you?"),
]
result = engine.chat(messages)
LLMEngine(config: LLMConfig | None = None) 
212    def __init__(self, config: LLMConfig | None = None) -> None:
213        self.config = config or LLMConfig()
214        self._detector = OllamaDetector(self.config.base_url)
215        self._provider = create_provider(self.config)
config
provider: LLMProvider 
219    @property
220    def provider(self) -> LLMProvider:
221        """The underlying :class:`LLMProvider` instance."""
222        return self._provider

The underlying LLMProvider instance.

current_model: str 
224    @property
225    def current_model(self) -> str:
226        """The configured model name."""
227        return self.config.model

The configured model name.

is_available: bool 
229    @property
230    def is_available(self) -> bool:
231        """Whether the service is available.
232
233        For Anthropic-compatible/OpenAI providers, availability means an API
234        key is configured; for Ollama it means the service is running.
235        """
236        if self.config.provider is ModelProvider.OLLAMA:
237            return self._detector.is_running()
238        return self.config.api_key is not None

Whether the service is available.

For Anthropic-compatible/OpenAI providers, availability means an API key is configured; for Ollama it means the service is running.

def list_models(self) -> list[LLMModelInfo]: 
242    def list_models(self) -> list[LLMModelInfo]:
243        """List available Ollama models."""
244        return self._detector.list_models()

List available Ollama models.

def switch_model(self, model_name: str) -> bool: 
246    def switch_model(self, model_name: str) -> bool:
247        """Switch the active model (Ollama only).
248
249        Raises ``RuntimeError`` if the Ollama service is not running and
250        ``ValueError`` if the model is not found or the name is invalid.
251        """
252        if not re.match(self._MODEL_NAME_PATTERN, model_name):
253            raise ValueError(
254                "Model name contains illegal characters. "
255                "Only letters, digits, dot (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-) "
256                "are allowed."
257            )
258        if not self._detector.is_running():
259            raise RuntimeError(
260                "Ollama service is not running. Start it with:\n"
261                "  1. Install: https://ollama.ai\n"
262                "  2. Run: ollama serve\n"
263                "  3. Pull a model: ollama pull <model>"
264            )
265        model_info = self._detector.get_model_info(model_name)
266        if model_info is None:
267            available = [m.name for m in self.list_models()]
268            if available:
269                raise ValueError(
270                    "Requested model not found.\n"
271                    f"Available models: {', '.join(available)}\n"
272                    "Use 'ollama pull' to fetch new models."
273                )
274            raise ValueError(
275                "Requested model not found and no models are available locally.\n"
276                "Use 'ollama pull' to fetch a model."
277            )
278        # Mutate config in place so the provider picks up the new model.
279        self.config = self.config.model_copy(update={"model": model_name})
280        self._provider = create_provider(self.config)
281        return True

Switch the active model (Ollama only).

Raises RuntimeError if the Ollama service is not running and ValueError if the model is not found or the name is invalid.

def generate( self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult:

286        self,

292

297        """
298        self._ensure_available()
299        messages: list[ChatMessage] = []
300        sys_text = system_prompt or self.config.system_prompt
301        if sys_text:
302            messages.append(ChatMessage(role="system", content=sys_text))
303        messages.append(ChatMessage(role="user", content=prompt))
304        return self._provider.generate(messages, **kwargs)
