def embeddings(self, text: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]: 
358    def embeddings(self, text: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
359        """Get embeddings.
360
361        For Ollama: uses ``/api/embeddings``. For Anthropic-compatible/OpenAI
362        providers: uses ``/v1/embeddings`` (OpenAI format). S3 introduces a
363        dedicated :class:`EmbeddingProvider` abstraction; this method is kept
364        only for backward compatibility.
365        """

368        if self.config.provider is ModelProvider.OLLAMA:
369            return self._embeddings_ollama(texts)
370        return self._embeddings_cloud(texts)

Get embeddings.

For Ollama: uses /api/embeddings. For Anthropic-compatible/OpenAI providers: uses /v1/embeddings (OpenAI format). S3 introduces a dedicated EmbeddingProvider abstraction; this method is kept only for backward compatibility.

def close(self) -> None:

438        """Close the engine and underlying clients."""
439        self._provider.close()
440        self._detector.close()

Close the engine and underlying clients.

@dataclass
class LLMModelInfo: 
62@dataclass
63class LLMModelInfo:
64    """Metadata for a model (renamed from ``ModelInfo`` to avoid clashing with
65    ``vertai.local.ModelInfo``; the local side is reconciled in S7).
66    """
67
68    name: str
69    provider: ModelProvider
70    status: ModelStatus = ModelStatus.AVAILABLE
71    size: str | None = None
72    parameters: str | None = None
73    quantization: str | None = None
74    modified_at: str | None = None

Metadata for a model (renamed from ModelInfo to avoid clashing with vertai.local.ModelInfo; the local side is reconciled in S7).

LLMModelInfo( name: str, provider: ModelProvider, status: ModelStatus = <ModelStatus.AVAILABLE: 'available'>, size: str | None = None, parameters: str | None = None, quantization: str | None = None, modified_at: str | None = None)
name: str
provider: ModelProvider
status: ModelStatus = <ModelStatus.AVAILABLE: 'available'>
size: str | None = None
parameters: str | None = None
quantization: str | None = None
modified_at: str | None = None
class LLMProvider(abc.ABC): 
327class LLMProvider(ABC):
328    """LLM provider abstraction.
329
330    Each concrete provider (Ollama/Anthropic/DeepSeek/OpenAI) implements sync
331    and async generation + streaming with optional tool calling. ``messages``
332    are always :class:`ChatMessage`; ``tools`` is a list of :class:`ToolSpec`.
333    """
334
335    def __init__(self, config: LLMConfig) -> None:
336        self.config = config
337        self._sync_client: httpx.Client | None = None
338        self._async_client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None
339
340    # -- client lifecycle -------------------------------------------------
341
342    def _get_sync_client(self) -> httpx.Client:
343        if self._sync_client is None:
344            self._sync_client = httpx.Client(timeout=self.config.timeout)
345        return self._sync_client
346
347    async def _get_async_client(self) -> httpx.AsyncClient:
348        if self._async_client is None:
349            self._async_client = httpx.AsyncClient(
350                timeout=self.config.timeout
351            )
352        return self._async_client
353
354    def close(self) -> None:
355        """Close the underlying sync HTTP client."""
356        if self._sync_client is not None:
357            self._sync_client.close()
358            self._sync_client = None
359
360    async def aclose(self) -> None:
361        """Close the underlying async HTTP client."""
362        if self._async_client is not None:
363            await self._async_client.aclose()
364            self._async_client = None
365
366    def __enter__(self) -> LLMProvider:
367        return self
368
369    def __exit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
370        self.close()
371
372    # -- message helpers --------------------------------------------------
373
374    @staticmethod
375    def _coerce_messages(
376        messages: list[ChatMessage] | list[dict[str, Any]],
377    ) -> list[ChatMessage]:
378        """Coerce a list of ChatMessage or dict into list[ChatMessage]."""
379        return [ChatMessage.coerce(m) for m in messages]
380
381    @staticmethod
382    def _split_system(
383        messages: list[ChatMessage],
384    ) -> tuple[str | None, list[ChatMessage]]:
385        """Pull leading/system messages into a system string (Anthropic style).
386
387        Returns ``(system_or_None, non_system_messages)``.
388        """
389        system_parts: list[str] = []
390        rest: list[ChatMessage] = []
391        for m in messages:
392            if m.role == "system":
393                system_parts.append(m.content)
394            else:
395                rest.append(m)
396        system = "\n\n".join(system_parts) if system_parts else None
397        return system, rest
398
399    # -- abstract API -----------------------------------------------------
400
401    @abstractmethod
402    def generate(
403        self,
404        messages: list[ChatMessage],
405        *,
406        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
407        **kwargs: Any,
408    ) -> GenerateResult: ...
409
410    @abstractmethod
411    def stream(
412        self,
413        messages: list[ChatMessage],
414        *,
415        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
416        **kwargs: Any,
417    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]: ...
418
419    @abstractmethod
420    async def agenerate(
421        self,
422        messages: list[ChatMessage],
423        *,
424        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
425        **kwargs: Any,
426    ) -> GenerateResult: ...
427
428    @abstractmethod
429    def astream(
430        self,
431        messages: list[ChatMessage],
432        *,
433        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
434        **kwargs: Any,
435    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
436        """Async streaming generation.
437
438        Concrete adapters implement this as ``async def`` (an async generator);
439        the ABC declares it without ``async`` so the override is type-compatible
440        (see mypy's async-iterator override rules).
441        """
442        ...

LLM provider abstraction.

Each concrete provider (Ollama/Anthropic/DeepSeek/OpenAI) implements sync and async generation + streaming with optional tool calling. messages are always ChatMessage; tools is a list of ToolSpec.

config
def close(self) -> None: 
354    def close(self) -> None:
355        """Close the underlying sync HTTP client."""
356        if self._sync_client is not None:
357            self._sync_client.close()
358            self._sync_client = None

Close the underlying sync HTTP client.

async def aclose(self) -> None: 
360    async def aclose(self) -> None:
361        """Close the underlying async HTTP client."""
362        if self._async_client is not None:
363            await self._async_client.aclose()
364            self._async_client = None

Close the underlying async HTTP client.

@abstractmethod
def generate( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult: 
401    @abstractmethod
402    def generate(
403        self,
404        messages: list[ChatMessage],
405        *,
406        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
407        **kwargs: Any,
408    ) -> GenerateResult: ...
@abstractmethod
def stream( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]]: 
410    @abstractmethod
411    def stream(
412        self,
413        messages: list[ChatMessage],
414        *,
415        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
416        **kwargs: Any,
417    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]: ...
@abstractmethod
async def agenerate( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult: 
419    @abstractmethod
420    async def agenerate(
421        self,
422        messages: list[ChatMessage],
423        *,
424        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
425        **kwargs: Any,
426    ) -> GenerateResult: ...
@abstractmethod
def astream( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> AsyncIterator[Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]]: 
428    @abstractmethod
429    def astream(
430        self,
431        messages: list[ChatMessage],
432        *,
433        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
434        **kwargs: Any,
435    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
436        """Async streaming generation.
437
438        Concrete adapters implement this as ``async def`` (an async generator);
439        the ABC declares it without ``async`` so the override is type-compatible
440        (see mypy's async-iterator override rules).
441        """
442        ...

Async streaming generation.

Concrete adapters implement this as async def (an async generator); the ABC declares it without async so the override is type-compatible (see mypy's async-iterator override rules).

class ModelProvider(builtins.str, enum.Enum): 
39class ModelProvider(str, Enum):
40    """Model provider enum.
41
42    ``CUSTOM`` is kept for backward compatibility; ``create_provider`` falls
43    back to the Anthropic-compatible path for it (configurable via base_url).
44    """
45
46    OLLAMA = "ollama"
47    OPENAI = "openai"
48    ANTHROPIC = "anthropic"
49    DEEPSEEK = "deepseek"
50    CUSTOM = "custom"

Model provider enum.

CUSTOM is kept for backward compatibility; create_provider falls back to the Anthropic-compatible path for it (configurable via base_url).

OLLAMA = <ModelProvider.OLLAMA: 'ollama'>
OPENAI = <ModelProvider.OPENAI: 'openai'>
ANTHROPIC = <ModelProvider.ANTHROPIC: 'anthropic'>
DEEPSEEK = <ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK: 'deepseek'>
CUSTOM = <ModelProvider.CUSTOM: 'custom'>
class ModelStatus(builtins.str, enum.Enum): 
53class ModelStatus(str, Enum):
54    """Model availability status (used by :class:`LLMModelInfo`)."""
55
56    AVAILABLE = "available"
57    UNAVAILABLE = "unavailable"
58    LOADING = "loading"
59    ERROR = "error"

Model availability status (used by LLMModelInfo).

AVAILABLE = <ModelStatus.AVAILABLE: 'available'>
UNAVAILABLE = <ModelStatus.UNAVAILABLE: 'unavailable'>
LOADING = <ModelStatus.LOADING: 'loading'>
ERROR = <ModelStatus.ERROR: 'error'>
class OllamaProvider(vertai.LLMProvider): 
1234class OllamaProvider(LLMProvider):
1235    """Adapter for the Ollama ``/api/chat`` API."""
1236
1237    def _endpoint(self) -> str:
1238        return f"{self.config.base_url.rstrip('/')}/api/chat"
1239
1240    def _build_payload(
1241        self,
1242        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1243        *,
1244        stream: bool,
1245        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None,
1246        **kwargs: Any,
1247    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1248        config = self.config
1249        system, rest = self._split_system(messages)
1250        if system is None and config.system_prompt is not None:
1251            system = config.system_prompt
1252        msg_list: list[dict[str, str]] = [
1253            {"role": m.role, "content": m.content} for m in rest
1254        ]
1255        if system:
1256            msg_list.insert(0, {"role": "system", "content": system})
1257
1258        options: dict[str, Any] = {
1259            "num_predict": int(kwargs.get("max_tokens", config.max_tokens)),
1260        }
1261        temperature = kwargs.get("temperature", config.temperature)
1262        if temperature is not None:
1263            options["temperature"] = float(temperature)
1264        top_p = kwargs.get("top_p", config.top_p)
1265        if top_p is not None:
1266            options["top_p"] = float(top_p)
1267        top_k = kwargs.get("top_k", config.top_k)
1268        if top_k is not None:
1269            options["top_k"] = int(top_k)
1270        repeat_penalty = kwargs.get("repeat_penalty", config.repeat_penalty)
1271        if repeat_penalty is not None:
1272            options["repeat_penalty"] = float(repeat_penalty)
1273        seed = kwargs.get("seed", config.seed)
1274        if seed is not None:
1275            options["seed"] = int(seed)
1276
1277        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
1278            "model": config.model,
1279            "messages": msg_list,
1280            "stream": stream,
1281            "options": options,
1282        }
1283        if tools:
1284            payload["tools"] = [
1285                {
1286                    "type": "function",
1287                    "function": {
1288                        "name": t.name,
1289                        "description": t.description,
1290                        "parameters": t.input_schema,
1291                    },
1292                }
1293                for t in tools
1294            ]
1295        return payload
1296
1297    def _ensure_available(self, running: bool) -> None:
1298        if not running:
1299            raise RuntimeError(
1300                "Ollama service is not running.\n\n"
1301                "Steps:\n"
1302                "  1. Install Ollama: https://ollama.ai\n"
1303                "  2. Start the service: ollama serve\n"
1304                "  3. Pull a model: ollama pull <model>\n"
1305                "Check the base_url configuration."
1306            )
1307
1308    def _is_running(self) -> bool:
1309        try:
1310            response = self._get_sync_client().get(
1311                f"{self.config.base_url.rstrip('/')}/api/tags"
1312            )
1313            return response.status_code == 200
1314        except (httpx.ConnectError, httpx.TimeoutException):
1315            return False
1316
1317    # -- sync -------------------------------------------------------------
1318
1319    def generate(
1320        self,
1321        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1322        *,
1323        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1324        **kwargs: Any,
1325    ) -> GenerateResult:
1326        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1327        payload = self._build_payload(
1328            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1329        )
1330        try:
1331            response = self._get_sync_client().post(self._endpoint(), json=payload)
1332            response.raise_for_status()
1333        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1334            raise RuntimeError(
1335                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1336                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1337            ) from e
1338        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1339            raise RuntimeError(
1340                f"Ollama request failed: {e.response.status_code} - "
1341                f"{e.response.text}"
1342            ) from e
1343        return _parse_ollama_chat_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
1344
1345    def stream(
1346        self,
1347        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1348        *,
1349        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1350        **kwargs: Any,
1351    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
1352        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1353        payload = self._build_payload(
1354            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1355        )
1356        try:
1357            with self._get_sync_client().stream(
1358                "POST", self._endpoint(), json=payload
1359            ) as response:
1360                yield from self._iter_ollama_stream(response)
1361        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1362            raise RuntimeError(
1363                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1364                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1365            ) from e
1366
1367    @staticmethod
1368    def _iter_ollama_stream(
1369        response: httpx.Response,
1370    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
1371        for line in response.iter_lines():
1372            if not line:
1373                continue
1374            try:
1375                data = json.loads(line)
1376            except json.JSONDecodeError:
1377                continue
1378            message = data.get("message", {})
1379            if isinstance(message.get("content"), str) and message["content"]:
1380                yield TextDeltaEvent(text=message["content"])
1381            if data.get("done"):
1382                yield DoneEvent(
1383                    finish_reason="stop" if data.get("done") else "length"
1384                )
1385                break
1386
1387    # -- async ------------------------------------------------------------
1388
1389    async def agenerate(
1390        self,
1391        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1392        *,
1393        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1394        **kwargs: Any,
1395    ) -> GenerateResult:
1396        # Reuse a sync availability probe (cheap GET); async path still does
1397        # the real generation via AsyncClient.
1398        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1399        payload = self._build_payload(
1400            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1401        )
1402        client = await self._get_async_client()
1403        try:
1404            response = await client.post(self._endpoint(), json=payload)
1405            response.raise_for_status()
1406        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1407            raise RuntimeError(
1408                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1409                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1410            ) from e
1411        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1412            raise RuntimeError(
1413                f"Ollama request failed: {e.response.status_code} - "
1414                f"{e.response.text}"
1415            ) from e
1416        return _parse_ollama_chat_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
1417
1418    async def astream(
1419        self,
1420        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1421        *,
1422        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1423        **kwargs: Any,
1424    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
1425        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1426        payload = self._build_payload(
1427            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1428        )
1429        client = await self._get_async_client()
1430        try:
1431            async with client.stream("POST", self._endpoint(), json=payload) as response:
1432                async for event in self._iter_ollama_stream_async(response):
1433                    yield event
1434        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1435            raise RuntimeError(
1436                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1437                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1438            ) from e
1439
1440    @staticmethod
1441    async def _iter_ollama_stream_async(
1442        response: httpx.Response,
1443    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
1444        async for line in response.aiter_lines():
1445            if not line:
1446                continue
1447            try:
1448                data = json.loads(line)
1449            except json.JSONDecodeError:
1450                continue
1451            message = data.get("message", {})
1452            if isinstance(message.get("content"), str) and message["content"]:
1453                yield TextDeltaEvent(text=message["content"])
1454            if data.get("done"):
1455                yield DoneEvent(
1456                    finish_reason="stop" if data.get("done") else "length"
1457                )
1458                break

Adapter for the Ollama /api/chat API.

def generate( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult: 
1319    def generate(
1320        self,
1321        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1322        *,
1323        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1324        **kwargs: Any,
1325    ) -> GenerateResult:
1326        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1327        payload = self._build_payload(
1328            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1329        )
1330        try:
1331            response = self._get_sync_client().post(self._endpoint(), json=payload)
1332            response.raise_for_status()
1333        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1334            raise RuntimeError(
1335                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1336                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1337            ) from e
1338        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1339            raise RuntimeError(
1340                f"Ollama request failed: {e.response.status_code} - "
1341                f"{e.response.text}"
1342            ) from e
1343        return _parse_ollama_chat_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
def stream( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]]: 
1345    def stream(
1346        self,
1347        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1348        *,
1349        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1350        **kwargs: Any,
1351    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
1352        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1353        payload = self._build_payload(
1354            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1355        )
1356        try:
1357            with self._get_sync_client().stream(
1358                "POST", self._endpoint(), json=payload
1359            ) as response:
1360                yield from self._iter_ollama_stream(response)
1361        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1362            raise RuntimeError(
1363                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1364                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1365            ) from e
async def agenerate( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult: 
1389    async def agenerate(
1390        self,
1391        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1392        *,
1393        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1394        **kwargs: Any,
1395    ) -> GenerateResult:
1396        # Reuse a sync availability probe (cheap GET); async path still does
1397        # the real generation via AsyncClient.
1398        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1399        payload = self._build_payload(
1400            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1401        )
1402        client = await self._get_async_client()
1403        try:
1404            response = await client.post(self._endpoint(), json=payload)
1405            response.raise_for_status()
1406        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1407            raise RuntimeError(
1408                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1409                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1410            ) from e
1411        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1412            raise RuntimeError(
1413                f"Ollama request failed: {e.response.status_code} - "
1414                f"{e.response.text}"
1415            ) from e
1416        return _parse_ollama_chat_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
async def astream( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> AsyncIterator[Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]]: 
1418    async def astream(
1419        self,
1420        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1421        *,
1422        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1423        **kwargs: Any,
1424    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
1425        self._ensure_available(self._is_running())
1426        payload = self._build_payload(
1427            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1428        )
1429        client = await self._get_async_client()
1430        try:
1431            async with client.stream("POST", self._endpoint(), json=payload) as response:
1432                async for event in self._iter_ollama_stream_async(response):
1433                    yield event
1434        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1435            raise RuntimeError(
1436                f"Cannot connect to Ollama ({self.config.base_url}). "
1437                "Ensure Ollama is running: ollama serve"
1438            ) from e

Async streaming generation.

Concrete adapters implement this as async def (an async generator); the ABC declares it without async so the override is type-compatible (see mypy's async-iterator override rules).

class OpenAIProvider(vertai.LLMProvider): 
 936class OpenAIProvider(LLMProvider):
 937    """Adapter for the real OpenAI Chat Completions API.
 938
 939    Uses ``/chat/completions`` under the configured ``base_url`` and sends the
 940    API key as a ``Authorization: Bearer ...`` header (the previous code routed
 941    OpenAI through the Anthropic path with ``x-api-key``, which is incorrect).
 942    """
 943
 944    def _headers(self) -> dict[str, str]:
 945        return {
 946            "Content-Type": "application/json",
 947            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.config.api_key or ''}",
 948        }
 949
 950    def _endpoint(self) -> str:
 951        # base_url is expected to end with /v1; append /chat/completions.
 952        return f"{self.config.base_url.rstrip('/')}/chat/completions"
 953
 954    def _build_payload(
 955        self,
 956        messages: list[ChatMessage],
 957        *,
 958        stream: bool,
 959        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None,
 960        **kwargs: Any,
 961    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 962        config = self.config
 963        # OpenAI uses a single messages list; system messages stay inline.
 964        msg_list: list[dict[str, str]] = []
 965        system_text = config.system_prompt
 966        for m in self._coerce_messages(messages):
 967            if m.role == "system":
 968                # Merge any inline system messages with the configured one.
 969                system_text = (
 970                    f"{system_text}\n\n{m.content}"
 971                    if system_text
 972                    else m.content
 973                )
 974            else:
 975                msg_list.append({"role": m.role, "content": m.content})
 976        if system_text:
 977            msg_list.insert(0, {"role": "system", "content": system_text})
 978
 979        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
 980            "model": config.model,
 981            "messages": msg_list,
 982            "stream": stream,
 983            "max_tokens": int(kwargs.get("max_tokens", config.max_tokens)),
 984        }
 985        temperature = kwargs.get("temperature", config.temperature)
 986        if temperature is not None:
 987            payload["temperature"] = float(temperature)
 988        top_p = kwargs.get("top_p", config.top_p)
 989        if top_p is not None:
 990            payload["top_p"] = float(top_p)
 991        seed = kwargs.get("seed", config.seed)
 992        if seed is not None:
 993            payload["seed"] = int(seed)
 994
 995        if tools:
 996            payload["tools"] = [
 997                {
 998                    "type": "function",
 999                    "function": {
1000                        "name": t.name,
1001                        "description": t.description,
1002                        "parameters": t.input_schema,
1003                    },
1004                }
1005                for t in tools
1006            ]
1007        return payload
1008
1009    def _ensure_api_key(self) -> None:
1010        if not self.config.api_key:
1011            raise RuntimeError(
1012                "OpenAI API requires an API key. Provide it via the "
1013                "VERTAI_API_KEY environment variable or LLMConfig(api_key=...)."
1014            )
1015
1016    # -- sync -------------------------------------------------------------
1017
1018    def generate(
1019        self,
1020        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1021        *,
1022        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1023        **kwargs: Any,
1024    ) -> GenerateResult:
1025        self._ensure_api_key()
1026        payload = self._build_payload(
1027            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1028        )
1029        try:
1030            response = self._get_sync_client().post(
1031                self._endpoint(), json=payload, headers=self._headers()
1032            )
1033            response.raise_for_status()
1034        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1035            raise RuntimeError(
1036                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1037            ) from e
1038        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1039            raise RuntimeError(
1040                f"OpenAI API request failed: {e.response.status_code}\n"
1041                f"Response: {e.response.text}"
1042            ) from e
1043        return _parse_openai_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
1044
1045    def stream(
1046        self,
1047        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1048        *,
1049        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1050        **kwargs: Any,
1051    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
1052        self._ensure_api_key()
1053        payload = self._build_payload(
1054            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1055        )
1056        try:
1057            with self._get_sync_client().stream(
1058                "POST",
1059                self._endpoint(),
1060                json=payload,
1061                headers=self._headers(),
1062            ) as response:
1063                yield from self._iter_openai_stream(response)
1064        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1065            raise RuntimeError(
1066                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1067            ) from e
1068
1069    @staticmethod
1070    def _iter_openai_stream(
1071        response: httpx.Response,
1072    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
1073        """Parse an OpenAI SSE stream into StreamEvent values."""
1074        tool_acc: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {}
1075        tool_order: list[int] = []
1076        finish_reason = "stop"
1077        for data in _iter_sse_lines_sync(response):
1078            if data.get("[DONE]"):  # not standard but defensive
1079                break
1080            choices = data.get("choices", [])
1081            if not choices:
1082                continue
1083            choice = choices[0]
1084            delta = choice.get("delta", {})
1085            if isinstance(delta.get("content"), str) and delta["content"]:
1086                yield TextDeltaEvent(text=delta["content"])
1087            for tc in delta.get("tool_calls", []) or []:
1088                idx = int(tc.get("index", 0))
1089                if idx not in tool_acc:
1090                    tool_acc[idx] = {
1091                        "id": str(tc.get("id", "")),
1092                        "name": "",
1093                        "args": "",
1094                    }
1095                    tool_order.append(idx)
1096                if tc.get("id"):
1097                    tool_acc[idx]["id"] = str(tc["id"])
1098                fn = tc.get("function", {})
1099                if fn.get("name"):
1100                    tool_acc[idx]["name"] = str(fn["name"])
1101                if fn.get("arguments"):
1102                    tool_acc[idx]["args"] += str(fn["arguments"])
1103            if choice.get("finish_reason"):
1104                finish_reason = str(choice["finish_reason"])
1105        for idx in tool_order:
1106            block = tool_acc[idx]
1107            yield ToolUseEvent(
1108                id=block["id"], name=block["name"], arguments=block["args"]
1109            )
1110        yield DoneEvent(
1111            finish_reason=finish_reason,
1112            tool_calls=[
1113                ToolCall(
1114                    id=tool_acc[idx]["id"],
1115                    name=tool_acc[idx]["name"],
1116                    arguments=_parse_json_object(tool_acc[idx]["args"]),
1117                )
1118                for idx in tool_order
1119            ],
1120        )
1121
1122    # -- async ------------------------------------------------------------
1123
1124    async def agenerate(
1125        self,
1126        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1127        *,
1128        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1129        **kwargs: Any,
1130    ) -> GenerateResult:
1131        self._ensure_api_key()
1132        payload = self._build_payload(
1133            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1134        )
1135        client = await self._get_async_client()
1136        try:
1137            response = await client.post(
1138                self._endpoint(), json=payload, headers=self._headers()
1139            )
1140            response.raise_for_status()
1141        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1142            raise RuntimeError(
1143                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1144            ) from e
1145        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1146            raise RuntimeError(
1147                f"OpenAI API request failed: {e.response.status_code}\n"
1148                f"Response: {e.response.text}"
1149            ) from e
1150        return _parse_openai_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
1151
1152    async def astream(
1153        self,
1154        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1155        *,
1156        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1157        **kwargs: Any,
1158    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
1159        self._ensure_api_key()
1160        payload = self._build_payload(
1161            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1162        )
1163        client = await self._get_async_client()
1164        try:
1165            async with client.stream(
1166                "POST",
1167                self._endpoint(),
1168                json=payload,
1169                headers=self._headers(),
1170            ) as response:
1171                async for event in self._iter_openai_stream_async(response):
1172                    yield event
1173        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1174            raise RuntimeError(
1175                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1176            ) from e
1177
1178    @staticmethod
1179    async def _iter_openai_stream_async(
1180        response: httpx.Response,
1181    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
1182        tool_acc: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {}
1183        tool_order: list[int] = []
1184        finish_reason = "stop"
1185        async for data in _iter_sse_lines_async(response):
1186            choices = data.get("choices", [])
1187            if not choices:
1188                continue
1189            choice = choices[0]
1190            delta = choice.get("delta", {})
1191            if isinstance(delta.get("content"), str) and delta["content"]:
1192                yield TextDeltaEvent(text=delta["content"])
1193            for tc in delta.get("tool_calls", []) or []:
1194                idx = int(tc.get("index", 0))
1195                if idx not in tool_acc:
1196                    tool_acc[idx] = {
1197                        "id": str(tc.get("id", "")),
1198                        "name": "",
1199                        "args": "",
1200                    }
1201                    tool_order.append(idx)
1202                if tc.get("id"):
1203                    tool_acc[idx]["id"] = str(tc["id"])
1204                fn = tc.get("function", {})
1205                if fn.get("name"):
1206                    tool_acc[idx]["name"] = str(fn["name"])
1207                if fn.get("arguments"):
1208                    tool_acc[idx]["args"] += str(fn["arguments"])
1209            if choice.get("finish_reason"):
1210                finish_reason = str(choice["finish_reason"])
1211        for idx in tool_order:
1212            block = tool_acc[idx]
1213            yield ToolUseEvent(
1214                id=block["id"], name=block["name"], arguments=block["args"]
1215            )
1216        yield DoneEvent(
1217            finish_reason=finish_reason,
1218            tool_calls=[
1219                ToolCall(
1220                    id=tool_acc[idx]["id"],
1221                    name=tool_acc[idx]["name"],
1222                    arguments=_parse_json_object(tool_acc[idx]["args"]),
1223                )
1224                for idx in tool_order
1225            ],
1226        )

Adapter for the real OpenAI Chat Completions API.

Uses /chat/completions under the configured base_url and sends the API key as a Authorization: Bearer ... header (the previous code routed OpenAI through the Anthropic path with x-api-key, which is incorrect).

def generate( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult: 
1018    def generate(
1019        self,
1020        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1021        *,
1022        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1023        **kwargs: Any,
1024    ) -> GenerateResult:
1025        self._ensure_api_key()
1026        payload = self._build_payload(
1027            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1028        )
1029        try:
1030            response = self._get_sync_client().post(
1031                self._endpoint(), json=payload, headers=self._headers()
1032            )
1033            response.raise_for_status()
1034        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1035            raise RuntimeError(
1036                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1037            ) from e
1038        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1039            raise RuntimeError(
1040                f"OpenAI API request failed: {e.response.status_code}\n"
1041                f"Response: {e.response.text}"
1042            ) from e
1043        return _parse_openai_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
def stream( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]]: 
1045    def stream(
1046        self,
1047        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1048        *,
1049        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1050        **kwargs: Any,
1051    ) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
1052        self._ensure_api_key()
1053        payload = self._build_payload(
1054            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1055        )
1056        try:
1057            with self._get_sync_client().stream(
1058                "POST",
1059                self._endpoint(),
1060                json=payload,
1061                headers=self._headers(),
1062            ) as response:
1063                yield from self._iter_openai_stream(response)
1064        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1065            raise RuntimeError(
1066                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1067            ) from e
async def agenerate( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> GenerateResult: 
1124    async def agenerate(
1125        self,
1126        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1127        *,
1128        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1129        **kwargs: Any,
1130    ) -> GenerateResult:
1131        self._ensure_api_key()
1132        payload = self._build_payload(
1133            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=False, tools=tools, **kwargs
1134        )
1135        client = await self._get_async_client()
1136        try:
1137            response = await client.post(
1138                self._endpoint(), json=payload, headers=self._headers()
1139            )
1140            response.raise_for_status()
1141        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1142            raise RuntimeError(
1143                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1144            ) from e
1145        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
1146            raise RuntimeError(
1147                f"OpenAI API request failed: {e.response.status_code}\n"
1148                f"Response: {e.response.text}"
1149            ) from e
1150        return _parse_openai_response(response.json(), self.config.model)
async def astream( self, messages: list[ChatMessage], *, tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> AsyncIterator[Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]]: 
1152    async def astream(
1153        self,
1154        messages: list[ChatMessage],
1155        *,
1156        tools: list[ToolSpec] | None = None,
1157        **kwargs: Any,
1158    ) -> AsyncIterator[StreamEvent]:
1159        self._ensure_api_key()
1160        payload = self._build_payload(
1161            self._coerce_messages(messages), stream=True, tools=tools, **kwargs
1162        )
1163        client = await self._get_async_client()
1164        try:
1165            async with client.stream(
1166                "POST",
1167                self._endpoint(),
1168                json=payload,
1169                headers=self._headers(),
1170            ) as response:
1171                async for event in self._iter_openai_stream_async(response):
1172                    yield event
1173        except httpx.ConnectError as e:
1174            raise RuntimeError(
1175                f"Cannot connect to OpenAI API ({self.config.base_url})."
1176            ) from e

Async streaming generation.

Concrete adapters implement this as async def (an async generator); the ABC declares it without async so the override is type-compatible (see mypy's async-iterator override rules).

StreamEvent = typing.Union[TextDeltaEvent, ToolUseEvent, DoneEvent]
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextDeltaEvent:

171class TextDeltaEvent:
172    """A chunk of generated text."""
173
174    text: str

A chunk of generated text.

text: str
class ToolCall(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
130class ToolCall(BaseModel):
131    """A single tool invocation returned by the model.
132
133    ``arguments`` is the parsed argument dict; ``raw_arguments`` preserves the
134    raw string for debugging when parsing fails.
135    """
136
137    id: str = Field(description="Tool-call id assigned by the provider")
138    name: str = Field(description="Tool name to invoke")
139    arguments: dict[str, Any] = Field(
140        default_factory=dict, description="Parsed arguments"
141    )
142
143    model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)

A single tool invocation returned by the model.

arguments is the parsed argument dict; raw_arguments preserves the raw string for debugging when parsing fails.

id: str = PydanticUndefined

Tool-call id assigned by the provider

name: str = PydanticUndefined

Tool name to invoke

arguments: dict[str, typing.Any] = PydanticUndefined

Parsed arguments

class ToolSpec(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
114class ToolSpec(BaseModel):
115    """Tool specification passed to a provider for function calling.
116
117    ``input_schema`` is a JSON Schema dict describing the tool's parameters.
118    """
119
120    name: str = Field(description="Tool name")
121    description: str = Field(description="What the tool does")
122    input_schema: dict[str, Any] = Field(
123        default_factory=lambda: {"type": "object", "properties": {}},
124        description="JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters",
125    )
126
127    model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)

input_schema is a JSON Schema dict describing the tool's parameters.

description: str = PydanticUndefined

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ToolUseEvent: 
177@dataclass(frozen=True)
178class ToolUseEvent:
179    """A completed tool-use block emitted at the end of the tool block.
180
181    Streaming providers emit incremental argument fragments internally and
182    surface a single :class:`ToolUseEvent` once the block closes, so consumers
183    receive one event per tool call with the full argument string.
184    """
185
186    id: str
187    name: str
188    arguments: str

A completed tool-use block emitted at the end of the tool block.

Streaming providers emit incremental argument fragments internally and surface a single ToolUseEvent once the block closes, so consumers receive one event per tool call with the full argument string.

ToolUseEvent(id: str, name: str, arguments: str)
id: str
name: str
arguments: str
def create_provider( config: LLMConfig) -> LLMProvider: 
1466def create_provider(config: LLMConfig) -> LLMProvider:
1467    """Route a :class:`LLMConfig` to the appropriate :class:`LLMProvider`.
1468
1469    Routing:
1470        - ``OLLAMA`` -> :class:`OllamaProvider`
1471        - ``OPENAI`` -> :class:`OpenAIProvider` (real ``/chat/completions`` +
1472          Bearer header; fixes C4)
1473        - ``ANTHROPIC`` -> :class:`AnthropicProvider`
1474        - ``DEEPSEEK`` -> :class:`DeepSeekProvider` (Anthropic-compatible)
1475        - ``CUSTOM`` -> :class:`AnthropicProvider` (default; configurable via
1476          ``base_url``)
1477    """
1478    provider = config.provider
1479    if provider is ModelProvider.OLLAMA:
1480        return OllamaProvider(config)
1481    if provider is ModelProvider.OPENAI:
1482        return OpenAIProvider(config)
1483    if provider is ModelProvider.ANTHROPIC:
1484        return AnthropicProvider(config)
1485    if provider is ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK:
1486        return DeepSeekProvider(config)
1487    if provider is ModelProvider.CUSTOM:
1488        return AnthropicProvider(config)
1489    # Unreachable: enum is exhaustive, but keep a defensive fallback.
1490    raise ValueError(f"Unknown provider: {provider!r}")

Route a LLMConfig to the appropriate LLMProvider.

Routing: - OLLAMA -> OllamaProvider - OPENAI -> OpenAIProvider (real /chat/completions + Bearer header; fixes C4) - ANTHROPIC -> AnthropicProvider - DEEPSEEK -> DeepSeekProvider (Anthropic-compatible) - CUSTOM -> AnthropicProvider (default; configurable via base_url)

@dataclass
class Message: 
157@dataclass
158class Message:
159    """Conversation message."""
160
161    role: str  # "system", "user", "assistant"
162    content: str
163    timestamp: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
164    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
165
166    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
167        """Convert message to dictionary."""
168        return {
169            "role": self.role,
170            "content": self.content,
171            "timestamp": self.timestamp,
172            "metadata": self.metadata,
173        }
174
175    @classmethod
176    def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> "Message":
177        """Create message from dictionary."""
178        return cls(
179            role=data["role"],
180            content=data["content"],
181            timestamp=data.get("timestamp", time.time()),
182            metadata=data.get("metadata", {}),
183        )

content: str
timestamp: float
metadata: dict[str, typing.Any]

class SessionConfig: 
186@dataclass
187class SessionConfig:
188    """Session memory configuration.
189
190    Attributes:
191        max_messages: Maximum number of messages to keep in history.
192        max_tokens: Maximum total tokens for context window (estimated).
193        auto_save: Automatically save session on changes.
194        persist_directory: Directory for persistent session storage.
195    """
196
197    max_messages: int = 100
198    max_tokens: int = 4096
199    auto_save: bool = False
200    persist_directory: str | None = None

Session memory configuration.
