class SessionCorruptedError(builtins.ValueError): 
48class SessionCorruptedError(ValueError):
49    """Raised when a persisted session file cannot be parsed.
50
51    Carries the offending path so callers can surface a clear, actionable
52    message rather than a bare ``json.JSONDecodeError`` / ``KeyError``.
53    """
54
55    def __init__(self, path: str | Path, reason: str) -> None:
56        self.path = str(path)
57        self.reason = reason
58        super().__init__(f"Session file corrupted ({reason}): {path}")

Raised when a persisted session file cannot be parsed.

Carries the offending path so callers can surface a clear, actionable message rather than a bare json.JSONDecodeError / KeyError.

SessionCorruptedError(path: str | pathlib._local.Path, reason: str) 
55    def __init__(self, path: str | Path, reason: str) -> None:
56        self.path = str(path)
57        self.reason = reason
58        super().__init__(f"Session file corrupted ({reason}): {path}")
path
reason
class SessionMemory: 
203class SessionMemory:
204    """Session memory for conversation history and context persistence.
205
206    Features:
207    - Store and retrieve conversation history
208    - Automatic context window management
209    - Atomic persistence (tmp file + ``os.replace``) so a crash mid-write
210      never corrupts an existing session file
211    - Friendly handling of corrupted session files
212      (``SessionCorruptedError``)
213    - ``session_id`` whitelist defends against path traversal
214
215    Examples:
216        >>> from vertai.core import SessionMemory
217        >>> memory = SessionMemory()
218        >>> memory.add_message("user", "Hello!")
219        >>> memory.add_message("assistant", "Hi there!")
220        >>> history = memory.get_history()
221        >>> memory.save("session.json")
222        >>> loaded = SessionMemory.from_file("session.json")
223    """
224
225    def __init__(
226        self,
227        session_id: str | None = None,
228        config: SessionConfig | None = None,
229    ) -> None:
230        """Initialize session memory.
231
232        Args:
233            session_id: Unique session identifier. Auto-generated (uuid4) if
234                None. If supplied, must match ``^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`` (an empty
235                string is *not* auto-generated — it is rejected, so callers
236                learn about bad input instead of silently getting a new id).
237            config: Session configuration.
238
239        Raises:
240            ValueError: If a supplied ``session_id`` is empty or contains
241                characters outside ``[A-Za-z0-9_-]``.
242        """
243        if session_id is None:
244            self.session_id = self._generate_session_id()
245        else:
246            # Explicit (incl. empty) id: validate strictly. We deliberately do
247            # NOT treat "" as "please auto-generate" — silent fallback would
248            # mask caller bugs.
249            self._validate_session_id(session_id)
250            self.session_id = session_id
251        self.config = config or SessionConfig()
252        # A plain list, not a deque(maxlen=...): maxlen would silently evict
253        # from the left on append, which conflicts with pinning the system
254        # prompt. _trim_if_needed enforces max_messages with awareness of the
255        # pinned system prompt.
256        self._messages: list[Message] = []
257        self._context: dict[str, Any] = {}
258        self._created_at = time.time()
259        self._updated_at = time.time()
260        self._token_estimate = 0
261
262    @staticmethod
263    def _validate_session_id(session_id: str) -> None:
264        """Reject session IDs that are unsafe as filename components."""
265        if not session_id or not _SESSION_ID_PATTERN.match(session_id):
266            raise ValueError(
267                "Invalid session_id: must match '^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$' "
268                f"(got {session_id!r}). Path traversal separators and "
269                "non-filename-safe characters are rejected."
270            )
271
272    @staticmethod
273    def _generate_session_id() -> str:
274        """Generate a unique session ID using uuid4.
275
276        ``uuid4`` removes the same-millisecond collision the previous
277        ``int(time.time() * 1000)`` scheme had when two sessions were created
278        back-to-back (which would silently overwrite each other's persisted
279        file). 12 hex chars give 48 bits of entropy, ample for a single host.
280        """
281        return f"session_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
282
283    @staticmethod
284    def _estimate_tokens(text: str) -> int:
285        """Estimate token count for text.
286
287        Uses ``tiktoken`` (cl100k_base) when the optional dependency is
288        installed; otherwise falls back to ``_heuristic_token_count`` which
289        counts each CJK / non-ASCII character as one token and ~4 ASCII
290        characters as one token. The heuristic is documented and consistently
291        better than the legacy ``len // 4`` rule for Chinese text, which is
292        important because the SDK targets a Chinese-first audience.
293        """
294        return _TIKTOKEN.count(text)
295
296    @property
297    def message_count(self) -> int:
298        """Get number of messages in history."""
299        return len(self._messages)
300
301    @property
302    def token_estimate(self) -> int:
303        """Get estimated total tokens in history."""
304        return self._token_estimate
305
306    def add_message(
307        self,
308        role: str,
309        content: str,
310        metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
311    ) -> Message:
312        """Add a message to the conversation history.
313
314        Args:
315            role: Message role ("system", "user", "assistant").
316            content: Message content.
317            metadata: Optional metadata.
318
319        Returns:
320            The created message.
321
322        Raises:
323            ValueError: If ``role`` is not one of system/user/assistant.
324        """
325        if role not in ("system", "user", "assistant"):
326            raise ValueError(
327                f"Invalid role: {role}. Must be 'system', 'user', or 'assistant'."
328            )
329
330        message = Message(
331            role=role,
332            content=content,
333            metadata=metadata or {},
334        )
335
336        self._messages.append(message)
337        self._token_estimate += self._estimate_tokens(content)
338        self._updated_at = time.time()
339
340        # Trim history to fit max_messages and max_tokens, keeping the leading
341        # system prompt pinned (see _trim_if_needed).
342        self._trim_if_needed()
343
344        # Auto-save if enabled
345        if self.config.auto_save and self.config.persist_directory:
346            self.save(self._get_default_save_path())
347
348        return message
349
350    def _trim_if_needed(self) -> None:
351        """Trim history to fit within ``max_messages`` and ``max_tokens``.
352
353        The leading message is pinned when it is the system prompt - losing
354        it silently would change agent behavior mid-conversation. Everything
355        else is FIFO-evicted from the front of the (possibly system-skipped)
356        region. At least one message is always kept so the agent loop never
357        loses all context.
358        """
359        # The first message is pinned iff it's a system prompt AND there is
360        # more than one message (a lone system prompt is just "the history").
361        pinned_system = (
362            len(self._messages) > 1 and self._messages[0].role == "system"
363        )
364        # Index of the first evictable message.
365        first_evictable = 1 if pinned_system else 0
366
367        # Enforce max_messages. Several may need to be evicted at once if the
368        # caller lowered max_messages after construction.
369        max_messages = max(self.config.max_messages, 1)
370        while (
371            len(self._messages) > max_messages
372            and first_evictable < len(self._messages)
373        ):
374            removed = self._messages.pop(first_evictable)
375            self._token_estimate -= self._estimate_tokens(removed.content)
376
377        # Enforce max_tokens on top of the message-count cap. Keep at least
378        # one message so token trimming alone never empties the history.
379        while (
380            self._token_estimate > self.config.max_tokens
381            and len(self._messages) > 1
382            and first_evictable < len(self._messages)
383        ):
384            removed = self._messages.pop(first_evictable)
385            self._token_estimate -= self._estimate_tokens(removed.content)
386
387    def get_history(
388        self,
389        limit: int | None = None,
390        roles: list[str] | None = None,
391    ) -> list[Message]:
392        """Get conversation history.
393
394        Args:
395            limit: Maximum number of messages to return.
396            roles: Filter by roles (e.g., ["user", "assistant"]).
397
398        Returns:
399            List of messages.
400        """
401        messages: list[Message] = list(self._messages)
402
403        if roles:
404            messages = [m for m in messages if m.role in roles]
405
406        if limit:
407            messages = messages[-limit:]
408
409        return messages
410
411    def get_context(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
412        """Get session context data."""
413        return self._context.copy()
414
415    def set_context(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None:
416        """Set a context value.
417
418        Args:
419            key: Context key.
420            value: Context value.
421        """
422        self._context[key] = value
423        self._updated_at = time.time()
424
425    def clear_history(self) -> None:
426        """Clear conversation history."""
427        self._messages.clear()
428        self._token_estimate = 0
429        self._updated_at = time.time()
430
431    def clear_context(self) -> None:
432        """Clear session context."""
433        self._context.clear()
434        self._updated_at = time.time()
435
436    def clear(self) -> None:
437        """Clear all session data."""
438        self.clear_history()
439        self.clear_context()
440
441    def _get_default_save_path(self) -> str:
442        """Get default save path for this session."""
443        if not self.config.persist_directory:
444            raise ValueError("persist_directory not configured")
445        # session_id is whitelisted at construction + save() time, so this
446        # path join is safe from traversal.
447        return str(
448            Path(self.config.persist_directory) / f"{self.session_id}.json"
449        )
450
451    def save(self, filepath: str | Path | None = None) -> Path:
452        """Save session to a file atomically.
453
454        Writes a sibling temp file in the same directory and then
455        ``os.replace``-es it into the final path. ``os.replace`` is atomic on
456        POSIX and on Windows for same-filesystem renames, so a crash mid-write
457        leaves the previously-saved file (or no file at all if this is the
458        first save) untouched rather than half-written.
459
460        Args:
461            filepath: Path to save file. Uses default if None.
462
463        Returns:
464            The final path the session was written to.
465
466        Raises:
467            ValueError: If the session_id fails whitelist validation (defence
468                in depth — it is also validated at construction time).
469        """
470        # Defence in depth: re-validate in case session_id was mutated after
471        # construction.
472        self._validate_session_id(self.session_id)
473
474        path = Path(filepath or self._get_default_save_path())
475        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
476
477        data = {
478            "session_id": self.session_id,
479            "created_at": self._created_at,
480            "updated_at": self._updated_at,
481            "messages": [m.to_dict() for m in self._messages],
482            "context": self._context,
483            "config": {
484                "max_messages": self.config.max_messages,
485                "max_tokens": self.config.max_tokens,
486            },
487        }
488
489        serialized = json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
490
491        # Write to a temp file in the *same directory* so os.replace stays on
492        # one filesystem (and therefore atomic). tempfile.mkstemp gives us a
493        # predictable, race-free fd.
494        tmp_fd, tmp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(
495            prefix=f".{path.name}.", suffix=".tmp", dir=str(path.parent)
496        )
497        tmp_path = Path(tmp_name)
498        try:
499            with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
500                f.write(serialized)
501                f.flush()
502                try:
503                    os.fsync(f.fileno())
504                except OSError:
505                    # fsync is best-effort on some filesystems / platforms;
506                    # a missing fsync must not abort the save.
507                    logger.debug("fsync failed for %s (non-fatal)", tmp_path)
508            os.replace(tmp_path, path)
509        except BaseException:
510            # Clean up the orphaned temp file on any failure (including
511            # KeyboardInterrupt) so we don't leak .*.tmp files.
512            try:
513                if tmp_path.exists():
514                    tmp_path.unlink()
515            except OSError:
516                pass
517            raise
518
519        logger.info("Session saved: %s", path)
520        return path
521
522    def load(self, filepath: str | Path | None = None) -> None:
523        """Load session from a file into this instance (in place).
524
525        Replaces this session's messages/context/metadata with the contents of
526        the file. On a corrupted (truncated / malformed JSON / missing
527        required keys) file, raises ``SessionCorruptedError`` with the path
528        rather than a low-level ``json.JSONDecodeError`` / ``KeyError``.
529
530        Args:
531            filepath: Path to load file. Uses default if None.
532
533        Raises:
534            FileNotFoundError: If the file does not exist.
535            SessionCorruptedError: If the file exists but cannot be parsed.
536        """
537        path = Path(filepath or self._get_default_save_path())
538
539        if not path.exists():
540            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Session file not found: {path}")
541
542        try:
543            with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
544                raw = f.read()
545            data = json.loads(raw)
546        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
547            raise SessionCorruptedError(path, f"invalid JSON: {exc.msg}") from exc
548        except UnicodeDecodeError as exc:
549            raise SessionCorruptedError(path, f"not UTF-8: {exc.reason}") from exc
550
551        if not isinstance(data, dict):
552            raise SessionCorruptedError(
553                path, f"expected JSON object, got {type(data).__name__}"
554            )
555        for required in ("session_id", "created_at", "updated_at", "messages"):
556            if required not in data:
557                raise SessionCorruptedError(
558                    path, f"missing required key: {required}"
559                )
560
561        try:
562            loaded_session_id = str(data["session_id"])
563            self._validate_session_id(loaded_session_id)
564            self.session_id = loaded_session_id
565            self._created_at = float(data["created_at"])
566            self._updated_at = float(data["updated_at"])
567            messages_data = data["messages"]
568            if not isinstance(messages_data, list):
569                raise SessionCorruptedError(path, "'messages' must be a list")
570            self._messages = [Message.from_dict(m) for m in messages_data]
571            loaded_context = data.get("context", {})
572            if not isinstance(loaded_context, dict):
573                raise SessionCorruptedError(
574                    path, "'context' must be a JSON object"
575                )
576            self._context = loaded_context
577        except SessionCorruptedError:
578            raise
579        except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
580            # ValueError covers both _validate_session_id and float() failures.
581            raise SessionCorruptedError(path, f"invalid field: {exc}") from exc
582
583        # Recalculate token estimate from the loaded messages.
584        self._token_estimate = sum(
585            self._estimate_tokens(m.content) for m in self._messages
586        )
587
588        logger.info("Session loaded: %s", path)
589
590    @classmethod
591    def from_file(cls, filepath: str | Path) -> "SessionMemory":
592        """Create a session from a file (class-level convenience).
593
594        Mirrors the contract documented in ARCHITECTURE §3.9: returns a fresh
595        ``SessionMemory`` populated from the file, with friendly corruption
596        handling via ``SessionCorruptedError``.
597
598        Args:
599            filepath: Path to session file.
600
601        Returns:
602            Loaded session.
603
604        Raises:
605            FileNotFoundError: If the file does not exist.
606            SessionCorruptedError: If the file cannot be parsed.
607        """
608        session = cls()
609        session.load(filepath)
610        return session
611
612    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
613        """Convert session to dictionary.
614
615        Returns:
616            Dictionary representation of the session.
617        """
618        return {
619            "session_id": self.session_id,
620            "created_at": self._created_at,
621            "updated_at": self._updated_at,
622            "message_count": len(self._messages),
623            "token_estimate": self._token_estimate,
624            "messages": [m.to_dict() for m in self._messages],
625            "context": self._context,
626        }
627
628    def get_last_n_messages(self, n: int) -> list[Message]:
629        """Get last N messages from history.
630
631        Args:
632            n: Number of messages to retrieve.
633
634        Returns:
635            List of most recent messages.
636        """
637        if n <= 0:
638            return []
639        return list(self._messages)[-n:]
640
641    def get_formatted_history(
642        self,
643        format_type: str = "default",
644    ) -> str:
645        """Get formatted conversation history.
646
647        Args:
648            format_type: Output format ("default", "markdown", "json").
649
650        Returns:
651            Formatted history string.
652        """
653        if not self._messages:
654            return ""
655
656        if format_type == "json":
657            return json.dumps(
658                [m.to_dict() for m in self._messages],
659                ensure_ascii=False,
660                indent=2,
661            )
662
663        lines = []
664        for msg in self._messages:
665            if format_type == "markdown":
666                lines.append(f"**{msg.role.title()}**: {msg.content}")
667            else:
668                lines.append(f"[{msg.role}] {msg.content}")
669
670        return "\n".join(lines)
671
672    def __repr__(self) -> str:
673        return (
674            f"SessionMemory(session_id={self.session_id!r}, "
675            f"messages={len(self._messages)}, tokens~={self._token_estimate})"
676        )

Session memory for conversation history and context persistence.

Features:

  • Store and retrieve conversation history
  • Automatic context window management
  • Atomic persistence (tmp file + os.replace) so a crash mid-write never corrupts an existing session file
  • Friendly handling of corrupted session files (SessionCorruptedError)
  • session_id whitelist defends against path traversal

Examples:

from vertai.core import SessionMemory memory = SessionMemory() memory.add_message("user", "Hello!") memory.add_message("assistant", "Hi there!") history = memory.get_history() memory.save("session.json") loaded = SessionMemory.from_file("session.json")

SessionMemory( session_id: str | None = None, config: SessionConfig | None = None) 
225    def __init__(
226        self,
227        session_id: str | None = None,
228        config: SessionConfig | None = None,
229    ) -> None:
230        """Initialize session memory.
231
232        Args:
233            session_id: Unique session identifier. Auto-generated (uuid4) if
234                None. If supplied, must match ``^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`` (an empty
235                string is *not* auto-generated — it is rejected, so callers
236                learn about bad input instead of silently getting a new id).
237            config: Session configuration.
238
239        Raises:
240            ValueError: If a supplied ``session_id`` is empty or contains
241                characters outside ``[A-Za-z0-9_-]``.
242        """
243        if session_id is None:
244            self.session_id = self._generate_session_id()
245        else:
246            # Explicit (incl. empty) id: validate strictly. We deliberately do
247            # NOT treat "" as "please auto-generate" — silent fallback would
248            # mask caller bugs.
249            self._validate_session_id(session_id)
250            self.session_id = session_id
251        self.config = config or SessionConfig()
252        # A plain list, not a deque(maxlen=...): maxlen would silently evict
253        # from the left on append, which conflicts with pinning the system
254        # prompt. _trim_if_needed enforces max_messages with awareness of the
255        # pinned system prompt.
256        self._messages: list[Message] = []
257        self._context: dict[str, Any] = {}
258        self._created_at = time.time()
259        self._updated_at = time.time()
260        self._token_estimate = 0

Initialize session memory.

Args: session_id: Unique session identifier. Auto-generated (uuid4) if None. If supplied, must match ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ (an empty string is not auto-generated — it is rejected, so callers learn about bad input instead of silently getting a new id). config: Session configuration.

Raises: ValueError: If a supplied session_id is empty or contains characters outside [A-Za-z0-9_-].

config
message_count: int 
296    @property
297    def message_count(self) -> int:
298        """Get number of messages in history."""
299        return len(self._messages)

Get number of messages in history.

token_estimate: int 
301    @property
302    def token_estimate(self) -> int:
303        """Get estimated total tokens in history."""
304        return self._token_estimate

Get estimated total tokens in history.

def add_message( self, role: str, content: str, metadata: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None) -> Message: 
306    def add_message(
307        self,
308        role: str,
309        content: str,
310        metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
311    ) -> Message:
312        """Add a message to the conversation history.
313
314        Args:
315            role: Message role ("system", "user", "assistant").
316            content: Message content.
317            metadata: Optional metadata.
318
319        Returns:
320            The created message.
321
322        Raises:
323            ValueError: If ``role`` is not one of system/user/assistant.
324        """
325        if role not in ("system", "user", "assistant"):
326            raise ValueError(
327                f"Invalid role: {role}. Must be 'system', 'user', or 'assistant'."
328            )
329
330        message = Message(
331            role=role,
332            content=content,
333            metadata=metadata or {},
334        )
335
336        self._messages.append(message)
337        self._token_estimate += self._estimate_tokens(content)
338        self._updated_at = time.time()
339
340        # Trim history to fit max_messages and max_tokens, keeping the leading
341        # system prompt pinned (see _trim_if_needed).
342        self._trim_if_needed()
343
344        # Auto-save if enabled
345        if self.config.auto_save and self.config.persist_directory:
346            self.save(self._get_default_save_path())
347
348        return message

Add a message to the conversation history.

Args: role: Message role ("system", "user", "assistant"). content: Message content. metadata: Optional metadata.

Returns: The created message.

Raises: ValueError: If role is not one of system/user/assistant.

def get_history( self, limit: int | None = None, roles: list[str] | None = None) -> list[Message]: 
387    def get_history(
388        self,
389        limit: int | None = None,
390        roles: list[str] | None = None,
391    ) -> list[Message]:
392        """Get conversation history.
393
394        Args:
395            limit: Maximum number of messages to return.
396            roles: Filter by roles (e.g., ["user", "assistant"]).
397
398        Returns:
399            List of messages.
400        """
401        messages: list[Message] = list(self._messages)
402
403        if roles:
404            messages = [m for m in messages if m.role in roles]
405
406        if limit:
407            messages = messages[-limit:]
408
409        return messages

Get conversation history.

Args: limit: Maximum number of messages to return. roles: Filter by roles (e.g., ["user", "assistant"]).

Returns: List of messages.

def get_context(self) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
411    def get_context(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
412        """Get session context data."""
413        return self._context.copy()

Get session context data.

def set_context(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None: 
415    def set_context(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None:
416        """Set a context value.
417
418        Args:
419            key: Context key.
420            value: Context value.
421        """
422        self._context[key] = value
423        self._updated_at = time.time()

Set a context value.

Args: key: Context key. value: Context value.

def clear_history(self) -> None: 
425    def clear_history(self) -> None:
426        """Clear conversation history."""
427        self._messages.clear()
428        self._token_estimate = 0
429        self._updated_at = time.time()

Clear conversation history.

def clear_context(self) -> None: 
431    def clear_context(self) -> None:
432        """Clear session context."""
433        self._context.clear()
434        self._updated_at = time.time()

Clear session context.

def clear(self) -> None: 
436    def clear(self) -> None:
437        """Clear all session data."""
438        self.clear_history()
439        self.clear_context()

Clear all session data.

def save( self, filepath: str | pathlib._local.Path | None = None) -> pathlib._local.Path: 
451    def save(self, filepath: str | Path | None = None) -> Path:
452        """Save session to a file atomically.
453
454        Writes a sibling temp file in the same directory and then
455        ``os.replace``-es it into the final path. ``os.replace`` is atomic on
456        POSIX and on Windows for same-filesystem renames, so a crash mid-write
457        leaves the previously-saved file (or no file at all if this is the
458        first save) untouched rather than half-written.
459
460        Args:
461            filepath: Path to save file. Uses default if None.
462
463        Returns:
464            The final path the session was written to.
465
466        Raises:
467            ValueError: If the session_id fails whitelist validation (defence
468                in depth — it is also validated at construction time).
469        """
470        # Defence in depth: re-validate in case session_id was mutated after
471        # construction.
472        self._validate_session_id(self.session_id)
473
474        path = Path(filepath or self._get_default_save_path())
475        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
476
477        data = {
478            "session_id": self.session_id,
479            "created_at": self._created_at,
480            "updated_at": self._updated_at,
481            "messages": [m.to_dict() for m in self._messages],
482            "context": self._context,
483            "config": {
484                "max_messages": self.config.max_messages,
485                "max_tokens": self.config.max_tokens,
486            },
487        }
488
489        serialized = json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
490
491        # Write to a temp file in the *same directory* so os.replace stays on
492        # one filesystem (and therefore atomic). tempfile.mkstemp gives us a
493        # predictable, race-free fd.
494        tmp_fd, tmp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(
495            prefix=f".{path.name}.", suffix=".tmp", dir=str(path.parent)
496        )
497        tmp_path = Path(tmp_name)
498        try:
499            with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
500                f.write(serialized)
501                f.flush()
502                try:
503                    os.fsync(f.fileno())
504                except OSError:
505                    # fsync is best-effort on some filesystems / platforms;
506                    # a missing fsync must not abort the save.
507                    logger.debug("fsync failed for %s (non-fatal)", tmp_path)
508            os.replace(tmp_path, path)
509        except BaseException:
510            # Clean up the orphaned temp file on any failure (including
511            # KeyboardInterrupt) so we don't leak .*.tmp files.
512            try:
513                if tmp_path.exists():
514                    tmp_path.unlink()
515            except OSError:
516                pass
517            raise
518
519        logger.info("Session saved: %s", path)
520        return path

Save session to a file atomically.

Writes a sibling temp file in the same directory and then os.replace-es it into the final path. os.replace is atomic on POSIX and on Windows for same-filesystem renames, so a crash mid-write leaves the previously-saved file (or no file at all if this is the first save) untouched rather than half-written.

Args: filepath: Path to save file. Uses default if None.

Returns: The final path the session was written to.

Raises: ValueError: If the session_id fails whitelist validation (defence in depth — it is also validated at construction time).

def load(self, filepath: str | pathlib._local.Path | None = None) -> None: 
522    def load(self, filepath: str | Path | None = None) -> None:
523        """Load session from a file into this instance (in place).
524
525        Replaces this session's messages/context/metadata with the contents of
526        the file. On a corrupted (truncated / malformed JSON / missing
527        required keys) file, raises ``SessionCorruptedError`` with the path
528        rather than a low-level ``json.JSONDecodeError`` / ``KeyError``.
529
530        Args:
531            filepath: Path to load file. Uses default if None.
532
533        Raises:
534            FileNotFoundError: If the file does not exist.
535            SessionCorruptedError: If the file exists but cannot be parsed.
536        """
537        path = Path(filepath or self._get_default_save_path())
538
539        if not path.exists():
540            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Session file not found: {path}")
541
542        try:
543            with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
544                raw = f.read()
545            data = json.loads(raw)
546        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
547            raise SessionCorruptedError(path, f"invalid JSON: {exc.msg}") from exc
548        except UnicodeDecodeError as exc:
549            raise SessionCorruptedError(path, f"not UTF-8: {exc.reason}") from exc
550
551        if not isinstance(data, dict):
552            raise SessionCorruptedError(
553                path, f"expected JSON object, got {type(data).__name__}"
554            )
555        for required in ("session_id", "created_at", "updated_at", "messages"):
556            if required not in data:
557                raise SessionCorruptedError(
558                    path, f"missing required key: {required}"
559                )
560
561        try:
562            loaded_session_id = str(data["session_id"])
563            self._validate_session_id(loaded_session_id)
564            self.session_id = loaded_session_id
565            self._created_at = float(data["created_at"])
566            self._updated_at = float(data["updated_at"])
567            messages_data = data["messages"]
568            if not isinstance(messages_data, list):
569                raise SessionCorruptedError(path, "'messages' must be a list")
570            self._messages = [Message.from_dict(m) for m in messages_data]
571            loaded_context = data.get("context", {})
572            if not isinstance(loaded_context, dict):
573                raise SessionCorruptedError(
574                    path, "'context' must be a JSON object"
575                )
576            self._context = loaded_context
577        except SessionCorruptedError:
578            raise
579        except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
580            # ValueError covers both _validate_session_id and float() failures.
581            raise SessionCorruptedError(path, f"invalid field: {exc}") from exc
582
583        # Recalculate token estimate from the loaded messages.
584        self._token_estimate = sum(
585            self._estimate_tokens(m.content) for m in self._messages
586        )
587
588        logger.info("Session loaded: %s", path)

Load session from a file into this instance (in place).

Replaces this session's messages/context/metadata with the contents of the file. On a corrupted (truncated / malformed JSON / missing required keys) file, raises SessionCorruptedError with the path rather than a low-level json.JSONDecodeError / KeyError.

Args: filepath: Path to load file. Uses default if None.

Raises: FileNotFoundError: If the file does not exist. SessionCorruptedError: If the file exists but cannot be parsed.

@classmethod
def from_file( cls, filepath: str | pathlib._local.Path) -> SessionMemory: 
590    @classmethod
591    def from_file(cls, filepath: str | Path) -> "SessionMemory":
592        """Create a session from a file (class-level convenience).
593
594        Mirrors the contract documented in ARCHITECTURE §3.9: returns a fresh
595        ``SessionMemory`` populated from the file, with friendly corruption
596        handling via ``SessionCorruptedError``.
597
598        Args:
599            filepath: Path to session file.
600
601        Returns:
602            Loaded session.
603
604        Raises:
605            FileNotFoundError: If the file does not exist.
606            SessionCorruptedError: If the file cannot be parsed.
607        """
608        session = cls()
609        session.load(filepath)
610        return session

Create a session from a file (class-level convenience).

Mirrors the contract documented in ARCHITECTURE §3.9: returns a fresh SessionMemory populated from the file, with friendly corruption handling via SessionCorruptedError.

Args: filepath: Path to session file.

Returns: Loaded session.

Raises: FileNotFoundError: If the file does not exist. SessionCorruptedError: If the file cannot be parsed.

def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
612    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
613        """Convert session to dictionary.
614
615        Returns:
616            Dictionary representation of the session.
617        """
618        return {
619            "session_id": self.session_id,
620            "created_at": self._created_at,
621            "updated_at": self._updated_at,
622            "message_count": len(self._messages),
623            "token_estimate": self._token_estimate,
624            "messages": [m.to_dict() for m in self._messages],
625            "context": self._context,
626        }

Convert session to dictionary.

Returns: Dictionary representation of the session.

def get_last_n_messages(self, n: int) -> list[Message]: 
628    def get_last_n_messages(self, n: int) -> list[Message]:
629        """Get last N messages from history.
630
631        Args:
632            n: Number of messages to retrieve.
633
634        Returns:
635            List of most recent messages.
636        """
637        if n <= 0:
638            return []
639        return list(self._messages)[-n:]

Get last N messages from history.

Args: n: Number of messages to retrieve.

Returns: List of most recent messages.

def get_formatted_history(self, format_type: str = 'default') -> str: 
641    def get_formatted_history(
642        self,
643        format_type: str = "default",
644    ) -> str:
645        """Get formatted conversation history.
646
647        Args:
648            format_type: Output format ("default", "markdown", "json").
649
650        Returns:
651            Formatted history string.
652        """
653        if not self._messages:
654            return ""
655
656        if format_type == "json":
657            return json.dumps(
658                [m.to_dict() for m in self._messages],
659                ensure_ascii=False,
660                indent=2,
661            )
662
663        lines = []
664        for msg in self._messages:
665            if format_type == "markdown":
666                lines.append(f"**{msg.role.title()}**: {msg.content}")
667            else:
668                lines.append(f"[{msg.role}] {msg.content}")
669
670        return "\n".join(lines)

Get formatted conversation history.

Args: format_type: Output format ("default", "markdown", "json").

Returns: Formatted history string.

class EmbeddingProvider(abc.ABC): 
32class EmbeddingProvider(ABC):
33    """Embedding provider abstraction.
34
35    Implementations turn text into dense vectors. ``embed`` / ``aembed`` accept
36    a single string or a list of strings and always return
37    ``list[list[float]]`` (one vector per input text), so callers do not need to
38    special-case the single-text path. ``dimension`` exposes the vector width so
39    a :class:`~vertai.core.vector.VectorStore` can initialize itself.
40    """
41
42    @abstractmethod
43    def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
44        """Embed a single text or a batch. Returns one vector per input text."""
45
46    @abstractmethod
47    async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
48        """Async embed. Real providers offload blocking work to a thread."""
49
50    @property
51    @abstractmethod
52    def dimension(self) -> int:
53        """Vector dimensionality."""

Embedding provider abstraction.

Implementations turn text into dense vectors. embed / aembed accept a single string or a list of strings and always return list[list[float]] (one vector per input text), so callers do not need to special-case the single-text path. dimension exposes the vector width so a ~vertai.core.vector.VectorStore can initialize itself.

@abstractmethod
def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]: 
42    @abstractmethod
43    def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
44        """Embed a single text or a batch. Returns one vector per input text."""

Embed a single text or a batch. Returns one vector per input text.

@abstractmethod
async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]: 
46    @abstractmethod
47    async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
48        """Async embed. Real providers offload blocking work to a thread."""

Async embed. Real providers offload blocking work to a thread.

dimension: int 
50    @property
51    @abstractmethod
52    def dimension(self) -> int:
53        """Vector dimensionality."""

Vector dimensionality.

class FunctionEmbeddingProvider(vertai.EmbeddingProvider): 
 56class FunctionEmbeddingProvider(EmbeddingProvider):
 57    """Wrap a single-text callable ``str -> list[float]`` as an
 58    :class:`EmbeddingProvider`.
 59
 60    Kept for backward compatibility with the ``embedding_fn`` parameter of
 61    :class:`~vertai.core.vector.VectorEngine`. The callable should be
 62    deterministic for a given text (non-deterministic callables produce
 63    inconsistent retrieval). Batch calls loop over the callable.
 64
 65    Note: this provider does **not** add semantic similarity by itself; supply a
 66    real embedding model (:class:`LocalSentenceTransformerProvider` or a cloud
 67    provider) for semantic search.
 68    """
 69
 70    def __init__(
 71        self,
 72        embedding_fn: Callable[[str], list[float]],
 73        *,
 74        dimension: int | None = None,
 75    ) -> None:
 76        self._fn = embedding_fn
 77        self._dimension = dimension
 78        self._probed_dimension: int | None = dimension
 79
 80    @property
 81    def dimension(self) -> int:
 82        if self._dimension is not None:
 83            return self._dimension
 84        if self._probed_dimension is not None:
 85            return self._probed_dimension
 86        raise RuntimeError(
 87            "FunctionEmbeddingProvider.dimension is unknown. Pass dimension= "
 88            "to the constructor or call embed() first."
 89        )
 90
 91    def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
 92        items = [texts] if isinstance(texts, str) else list(texts)
 93        vectors = [list(self._fn(t)) for t in items]
 94        if self._probed_dimension is None and vectors:
 95            self._probed_dimension = len(vectors[0])
 96        return vectors
 97
 98    async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
 99        # An arbitrary callable has no native async API; reuse the sync path.
100        return self.embed(texts)

Wrap a single-text callable str -> list[float] as an EmbeddingProvider.

Kept for backward compatibility with the embedding_fn parameter of ~vertai.core.vector.VectorEngine. The callable should be deterministic for a given text (non-deterministic callables produce inconsistent retrieval). Batch calls loop over the callable.

Note: this provider does not add semantic similarity by itself; supply a real embedding model (LocalSentenceTransformerProvider or a cloud provider) for semantic search.

FunctionEmbeddingProvider( embedding_fn: Callable[[str], list[float]], *, dimension: int | None = None) 
70    def __init__(
71        self,
72        embedding_fn: Callable[[str], list[float]],
73        *,
74        dimension: int | None = None,
75    ) -> None:
76        self._fn = embedding_fn
77        self._dimension = dimension
78        self._probed_dimension: int | None = dimension
dimension: int 
80    @property
81    def dimension(self) -> int:
82        if self._dimension is not None:
83            return self._dimension
84        if self._probed_dimension is not None:
85            return self._probed_dimension
86        raise RuntimeError(
87            "FunctionEmbeddingProvider.dimension is unknown. Pass dimension= "
88            "to the constructor or call embed() first."
89        )

Vector dimensionality.

def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]: 
91    def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
92        items = [texts] if isinstance(texts, str) else list(texts)
93        vectors = [list(self._fn(t)) for t in items]
94        if self._probed_dimension is None and vectors:
95            self._probed_dimension = len(vectors[0])
96        return vectors

Embed a single text or a batch. Returns one vector per input text.

async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]: 
 98    async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
 99        # An arbitrary callable has no native async API; reuse the sync path.
100        return self.embed(texts)

Async embed. Real providers offload blocking work to a thread.

class LocalSentenceTransformerProvider(vertai.EmbeddingProvider): 
103class LocalSentenceTransformerProvider(EmbeddingProvider):
104    """Real local embedding provider backed by ``sentence-transformers``.
105
106    Requires the ``vertai[embeddings]`` extra. The model is loaded lazily on
107    first use (loading is synchronous and may download weights on the first
108    run). ``encode_kwargs`` are forwarded to ``SentenceTransformer.encode``.
109
110    Example:
111        from vertai.core.embedding import LocalSentenceTransformerProvider
112        from vertai.core.vector import VectorEngine
113
114        provider = LocalSentenceTransformerProvider("BAAI/bge-small-zh-v1.5")
115        engine = VectorEngine(embedding_provider=provider)
116    """
117
118    def __init__(
119        self,
120        model_name: str = "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
121        *,
122        device: str | None = None,
123        encode_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
124    ) -> None:
125        self.model_name = model_name
126        self.device = device
127        self.encode_kwargs = encode_kwargs or {}
128        self._model: Any = None
129        self._dimension: int | None = None
130
131    def _ensure_model(self) -> None:
132        if self._model is None:
133            try:
134                from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
135            except ImportError as e:
136                raise RuntimeError(
137                    "LocalSentenceTransformerProvider requires the "
138                    "'vertai[embeddings]' extra (sentence-transformers). "
139                    "Install with: pip install 'vertai[embeddings]'"
140                ) from e
141            self._model = SentenceTransformer(self.model_name, device=self.device)
142
143    @property
144    def dimension(self) -> int:
145        if self._dimension is None:
146            self._ensure_model()
147            self._dimension = int(self._model.get_sentence_embedding_dimension())
148        return self._dimension
149
150    def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
151        self._ensure_model()
152        items = [texts] if isinstance(texts, str) else list(texts)
153        vectors = self._model.encode(items, **self.encode_kwargs)
154        return [list(v) for v in vectors]
155
156    async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
157        # sentence-transformers is synchronous; offload the (potentially
158        # blocking) encode to a thread so the event loop is not stalled.
159        return await asyncio.to_thread(self.embed, texts)

Real local embedding provider backed by sentence-transformers.

Requires the vertai[embeddings] extra. The model is loaded lazily on first use (loading is synchronous and may download weights on the first run). encode_kwargs are forwarded to SentenceTransformer.encode.

Example: from vertai.core.embedding import LocalSentenceTransformerProvider from vertai.core.vector import VectorEngine

provider = LocalSentenceTransformerProvider("BAAI/bge-small-zh-v1.5")
engine = VectorEngine(embedding_provider=provider)
LocalSentenceTransformerProvider( model_name: str = 'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2', *, device: str | None = None, encode_kwargs: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None)

119        self,
120        model_name: str = "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
121        *,
122        device: str | None = None,
123        encode_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
124    ) -> None:
125        self.model_name = model_name
126        self.device = device
127        self.encode_kwargs = encode_kwargs or {}
128        self._model: Any = None
129        self._dimension: int | None = None
model_name
device
encode_kwargs

144    def dimension(self) -> int:
145        if self._dimension is None:
146            self._ensure_model()
147            self._dimension = int(self._model.get_sentence_embedding_dimension())
148        return self._dimension

Vector dimensionality.

def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]: 
150    def embed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
151        self._ensure_model()
152        items = [texts] if isinstance(texts, str) else list(texts)
153        vectors = self._model.encode(items, **self.encode_kwargs)
154        return [list(v) for v in vectors]

Embed a single text or a batch. Returns one vector per input text.

async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]: 
156    async def aembed(self, texts: Union[str, list[str]]) -> list[list[float]]:
157        # sentence-transformers is synchronous; offload the (potentially
158        # blocking) encode to a thread so the event loop is not stalled.
159        return await asyncio.to_thread(self.embed, texts)

Async embed. Real providers offload blocking work to a thread.

class TextSplitter(abc.ABC): 
22class TextSplitter(ABC):
23    """Text splitter abstraction. Splits a long text into retrievable chunks."""
24
25    @abstractmethod
26    def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
27        """Split ``text`` into a list of non-empty chunks."""

Text splitter abstraction. Splits a long text into retrievable chunks.

@abstractmethod
def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]: 
25    @abstractmethod
26    def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
27        """Split ``text`` into a list of non-empty chunks."""

Split text into a list of non-empty chunks.

class RecursiveTextSplitter(vertai.TextSplitter): 
 30class RecursiveTextSplitter(TextSplitter):
 31    """Split text by recursively trying a hierarchy of separators.
 32
 33    Tries each separator in order (paragraph, line, sentence, word, character);
 34    when a piece is still longer than ``chunk_size`` it is split further by the
 35    next separator. The final fallback is a hard character split. Small adjacent
 36    pieces are merged greedily up to ``chunk_size``; ``chunk_overlap`` carries a
 37    tail of the previous chunk into the next to preserve boundary context.
 38
 39    Every emitted chunk is non-empty, stripped, and no longer than
 40    ``chunk_size`` (the overlap tail is truncated when needed to keep this
 41    invariant).
 42    """
 43
 44    _DEFAULT_SEPARATORS: list[str] = ["\n\n", "\n", ". ", "。", " ", ""]
 45
 46    def __init__(
 47        self,
 48        *,
 49        chunk_size: int = 512,
 50        chunk_overlap: int = 50,
 51        separators: list[str] | None = None,
 52    ) -> None:
 53        if chunk_size <= 0:
 54            raise ValueError("chunk_size must be positive")
 55        if not (0 <= chunk_overlap < chunk_size):
 56            raise ValueError("chunk_overlap must be in [0, chunk_size)")
 57        self.chunk_size = chunk_size
 58        self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap
 59        self.separators = (
 60            list(separators) if separators is not None else list(self._DEFAULT_SEPARATORS)
 61        )
 62
 63    def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
 64        if not text or not text.strip():
 65            return []
 66        chunks = self._split(text, self.separators)
 67        return [c for c in chunks if c.strip()]
 68
 69    def _split(self, text: str, separators: list[str]) -> list[str]:
 70        final_chunks: list[str] = []
 71
 72        # Pick the first separator present in the text; "" means char-level.
 73        sep = separators[-1]
 74        new_separators: list[str] = []
 75        for i, candidate in enumerate(separators):
 76            if candidate == "":
 77                sep = ""
 78                new_separators = []
 79                break
 80            if candidate in text:
 81                sep = candidate
 82                new_separators = separators[i + 1:]
 83                break
 84
 85        if sep == "":
 86            # Character-level fallback.
 87            return self._hard_split(text)
 88
 89        splits = text.split(sep)
 90        good: list[str] = []
 91        for piece in splits:
 92            if len(piece) < self.chunk_size:
 93                good.append(piece)
 94            else:
 95                if good:
 96                    final_chunks.extend(self._merge(good, sep))
 97                    good = []
 98                if not new_separators:
 99                    final_chunks.extend(self._hard_split(piece))
100                else:
101                    final_chunks.extend(self._split(piece, new_separators))
102        if good:
103            final_chunks.extend(self._merge(good, sep))
104        return final_chunks
105
106    def _merge(self, pieces: list[str], sep: str) -> list[str]:
107        chunks: list[str] = []
108        current = ""
109        for piece in pieces:
110            if not piece:
111                continue
112            candidate = piece if not current else current + sep + piece
113            if len(candidate) <= self.chunk_size:
114                current = candidate
115            else:
116                if current:
117                    chunks.append(current)
118                if self.chunk_overlap > 0 and current:
119                    tail = current[-self.chunk_overlap:]
120                    next_chunk = tail + (sep if sep else "") + piece
121                    if len(next_chunk) <= self.chunk_size:
122                        current = next_chunk
123                    else:
124                        # Overlap would overflow; drop overlap for this chunk.
125                        current = piece
126                else:
127                    current = piece
128        if current:
129            chunks.append(current)
130        return chunks
131
132    def _hard_split(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
133        if not text:
134            return []
135        if self.chunk_overlap == 0:
136            return [
137                text[i:i + self.chunk_size]
138                for i in range(0, len(text), self.chunk_size)
139            ]
140        step = self.chunk_size - self.chunk_overlap
141        chunks: list[str] = []
142        start = 0
143        text_len = len(text)
144        while start < text_len:
145            chunk = text[start:start + self.chunk_size]
146            chunks.append(chunk)
147            if start + self.chunk_size >= text_len:
148                break
149            start += step
150        return chunks

Split text by recursively trying a hierarchy of separators.

Tries each separator in order (paragraph, line, sentence, word, character); when a piece is still longer than chunk_size it is split further by the next separator. The final fallback is a hard character split. Small adjacent pieces are merged greedily up to chunk_size; chunk_overlap carries a tail of the previous chunk into the next to preserve boundary context.

Every emitted chunk is non-empty, stripped, and no longer than chunk_size (the overlap tail is truncated when needed to keep this invariant).

RecursiveTextSplitter( *, chunk_size: int = 512, chunk_overlap: int = 50, separators: list[str] | None = None) 
46    def __init__(
47        self,
48        *,
49        chunk_size: int = 512,
50        chunk_overlap: int = 50,
51        separators: list[str] | None = None,
52    ) -> None:
53        if chunk_size <= 0:
54            raise ValueError("chunk_size must be positive")
55        if not (0 <= chunk_overlap < chunk_size):
56            raise ValueError("chunk_overlap must be in [0, chunk_size)")
57        self.chunk_size = chunk_size
58        self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap
59        self.separators = (
60            list(separators) if separators is not None else list(self._DEFAULT_SEPARATORS)
61        )
chunk_size
chunk_overlap
separators
def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]: 
63    def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
64        if not text or not text.strip():
65            return []
66        chunks = self._split(text, self.separators)
67        return [c for c in chunks if c.strip()]

Split text into a list of non-empty chunks.

class FixedLengthSplitter(vertai.TextSplitter): 
153class FixedLengthSplitter(TextSplitter):
154    """Split text into fixed-length chunks with overlap.
155
156    ``chunk_size`` is the maximum chunk length (in characters); ``chunk_overlap``
157    is the number of characters carried from the end of one chunk into the next.
158    """
159
160    def __init__(self, *, chunk_size: int = 512, chunk_overlap: int = 50) -> None:
161        if chunk_size <= 0:
162            raise ValueError("chunk_size must be positive")
163        if not (0 <= chunk_overlap < chunk_size):
164            raise ValueError("chunk_overlap must be in [0, chunk_size)")
165        self.chunk_size = chunk_size
166        self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap
167
168    def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
169        if not text or not text.strip():
170            return []
171        step = self.chunk_size - self.chunk_overlap
172        if step <= 0:
173            step = 1
174        chunks: list[str] = []
175        start = 0
176        text_len = len(text)
177        while start < text_len:
178            end = start + self.chunk_size
179            chunk = text[start:end]
180            if chunk.strip():
181                chunks.append(chunk)
182            if end >= text_len:
183                break
184            start += step
185        return chunks

Split text into fixed-length chunks with overlap.

chunk_size is the maximum chunk length (in characters); chunk_overlap is the number of characters carried from the end of one chunk into the next.

FixedLengthSplitter(*, chunk_size: int = 512, chunk_overlap: int = 50) 
160    def __init__(self, *, chunk_size: int = 512, chunk_overlap: int = 50) -> None:
161        if chunk_size <= 0:
162            raise ValueError("chunk_size must be positive")
163        if not (0 <= chunk_overlap < chunk_size):
164            raise ValueError("chunk_overlap must be in [0, chunk_size)")
165        self.chunk_size = chunk_size
166        self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap
chunk_size
chunk_overlap
def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]: 
168    def split(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
169        if not text or not text.strip():
170            return []
171        step = self.chunk_size - self.chunk_overlap
172        if step <= 0:
173            step = 1
174        chunks: list[str] = []
175        start = 0
176        text_len = len(text)
177        while start < text_len:
178            end = start + self.chunk_size
179            chunk = text[start:end]
180            if chunk.strip():
181                chunks.append(chunk)
182            if end >= text_len:
183                break
184            start += step
185        return chunks

Split text into a list of non-empty chunks.

class Retriever(abc.ABC): 
22class Retriever(ABC):
23    """Retriever abstraction. Returns ranked :class:`SearchResult` for a query."""
24
25    @abstractmethod
26    def retrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
27        """Retrieve the top-``k`` results for ``query``."""
28
29    @abstractmethod
30    async def aretrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
31        """Async retrieve."""

Retriever abstraction. Returns ranked SearchResult for a query.

@abstractmethod
def retrieve( self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]: 
25    @abstractmethod
26    def retrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
27        """Retrieve the top-``k`` results for ``query``."""

Retrieve the top-k results for query.

@abstractmethod
async def aretrieve( self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]: 
29    @abstractmethod
30    async def aretrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
31        """Async retrieve."""

Async retrieve.

class VectorRetriever(vertai.Retriever): 
34class VectorRetriever(Retriever):
35    """Combine an :class:`EmbeddingProvider` and a :class:`VectorStore`.
36
37    The query is embedded by the provider and the resulting vector is handed to
38    the store's :meth:`VectorStore.search`. Subclasses override
39    :meth:`_transform_query` / :meth:`_rerank` to add query rewriting or
40    reranking (1.0 extension point; concrete advanced retrievers arrive in 1.x).
41
42    The embedding provider is also exposed via :attr:`embedding` so callers that
43    index documents (e.g. :class:`~vertai.scenarios.knowledge_qa.KnowledgeQA`)
44    can reuse the same provider for indexing and querying.
45    """
46
47    def __init__(self, embedding: EmbeddingProvider, store: VectorStore) -> None:
48        self.embedding = embedding
49        self.store = store
50
51    def retrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
52        transformed = self._transform_query(query)
53        query_vector = self.embedding.embed(transformed)[0]
54        results = self.store.search(query_vector, top_k=top_k)
55        return self._rerank(transformed, results)[:top_k]
56
57    async def aretrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
58        transformed = self._transform_query(query)
59        vectors = await self.embedding.aembed(transformed)
60        results = self.store.search(vectors[0], top_k=top_k)
61        return self._rerank(transformed, results)[:top_k]
62
63    def _transform_query(self, query: str) -> str:
64        """Hook for query rewriting. Default: identity."""
65        return query
66
67    def _rerank(
68        self, query: str, results: list[SearchResult]
69    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
70        """Hook for reranking. Default: preserve store ordering."""
71        return results

Combine an EmbeddingProvider and a VectorStore.

The query is embedded by the provider and the resulting vector is handed to the store's VectorStore.search(). Subclasses override _transform_query() / _rerank() to add query rewriting or reranking (1.0 extension point; concrete advanced retrievers arrive in 1.x).

The embedding provider is also exposed via embedding so callers that index documents (e.g. ~vertai.scenarios.knowledge_qa.KnowledgeQA) can reuse the same provider for indexing and querying.

VectorRetriever( embedding: EmbeddingProvider, store: VectorStore) 
47    def __init__(self, embedding: EmbeddingProvider, store: VectorStore) -> None:
48        self.embedding = embedding
49        self.store = store
embedding
store
def retrieve( self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]: 
51    def retrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
52        transformed = self._transform_query(query)
53        query_vector = self.embedding.embed(transformed)[0]
54        results = self.store.search(query_vector, top_k=top_k)
55        return self._rerank(transformed, results)[:top_k]

Retrieve the top-k results for query.

async def aretrieve( self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]: 
57    async def aretrieve(self, query: str, *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]:
58        transformed = self._transform_query(query)
59        vectors = await self.embedding.aembed(transformed)
60        results = self.store.search(vectors[0], top_k=top_k)
61        return self._rerank(transformed, results)[:top_k]

Async retrieve.

class FunctionTool(vertai.Tool): 
456class FunctionTool(Tool):
457    """A :class:`Tool` generated from a Python function.
458
459    The function signature is inspected and a Pydantic input model is built
460    so that ``execute`` validates arguments and the generated JSON Schema
461    carries Field constraints. ``timeout`` (seconds) wraps execution;
462    ``failure_error_function`` controls how exceptions are surfaced.
463    """
464
465    def __init__(
466        self,
467        func: Callable[..., Any],
468        *,
469        name_override: str | None = None,
470        description_override: str | None = None,
471        timeout: float | None = None,
472        failure_error_function: _FailureHandler | _DefaultFailureHandler = _DEFAULT_FAILURE_HANDLER,
473        timeout_mode: str = "error_as_result",
474    ) -> None:
475        if not callable(func):
476            raise TypeError(f"FunctionTool requires a callable, got {type(func)!r}")
477        self._func = func
478        self._name = name_override or func.__name__
479        summary, param_docs = _parse_docstring(func.__doc__)
480        self._description = description_override or summary or f"Tool {self._name}"
481        self._param_docs = param_docs
482        self._input_model = _build_param_model(func, param_docs)
483        self._parameters = _model_to_json_schema(self._input_model)
484        self._timeout = timeout
485        self._failure_error_function = failure_error_function
486        if timeout_mode not in ("error_as_result", "raise_exception"):
487            raise ValueError(
488                f"timeout_mode must be 'error_as_result' or 'raise_exception', "
489                f"got {timeout_mode!r}"
490            )
491        self._timeout_mode = timeout_mode
492
493    # -- Tool API ---------------------------------------------------------
494
495    @property
496    def name(self) -> str:
497        return self._name
498
499    @property
500    def description(self) -> str:
501        return self._description
502
503    @property
504    def parameters(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
505        # Return a shallow copy so callers cannot mutate cached schema.
506        return dict(self._parameters)
507
508    @property
509    def func(self) -> Callable[..., Any]:
510        """The wrapped function (read-only)."""
511        return self._func
512
513    @property
514    def timeout(self) -> float | None:
515        return self._timeout
516
517    # -- execution --------------------------------------------------------
518
519    def _validate(self, kwargs: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
520        validated = self._input_model.model_validate(kwargs)
521        return dict(validated)
522
523    def execute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
524        """Run the wrapped function synchronously with validated arguments.
525
526        ``timeout`` is enforced when set; on timeout the behaviour is
527        controlled by ``timeout_mode`` (``error_as_result`` returns a message,
528        ``raise_exception`` raises :class:`ToolTimeoutError`). Other
529        exceptions are routed through ``failure_error_function`` (default
530        friendly message; ``None`` re-raises).
531        """
532        try:
533            args = self._validate(kwargs)
534        except Exception as exc:  # validation errors
535            return self._handle_failure(exc)
536
537        try:
538            if self._timeout is None:
539                result = self._func(**args)
540            else:
541                result = self._run_with_timeout_sync(args)
542        except ToolTimeoutError:
543            if self._timeout_mode == "raise_exception":
544                raise
545            return f"Tool '{self._name}' timed out after {self._timeout}s."
546        except Exception as exc:
547            return self._handle_failure(exc)
548        return _stringify_result(result)
549
550    async def aexecute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
551        """Run the wrapped function asynchronously.
552
553        If the wrapped function is a coroutine function it is awaited; any
554        other callable is run in a thread so the event loop is not blocked.
555        Timeout and failure handling mirror :meth:`execute`.
556        """
557        try:
558            args = self._validate(kwargs)
559        except Exception as exc:
560            return self._handle_failure(exc)
561
562        try:
563            if self._timeout is None:
564                result = await self._invoke_async(args)
565            else:
566                result = await self._run_with_timeout_async(args)
567        except ToolTimeoutError:
568            if self._timeout_mode == "raise_exception":
569                raise
570            return f"Tool '{self._name}' timed out after {self._timeout}s."
571        except Exception as exc:
572            return self._handle_failure(exc)
573        return _stringify_result(result)
574
575    # -- helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
576
577    async def _invoke_async(self, args: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
578        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self._func):
579            return await self._func(**args)
580        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
581        return await loop.run_in_executor(None, lambda: self._func(**args))
582
583    def _run_with_timeout_sync(self, args: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
584        """Enforce a wall-clock timeout for a (potentially blocking) sync
585        function by running it in a worker thread and joining with a deadline.
586        """
587        result_box: dict[str, Any] = {}
588
589        def _runner() -> None:
590            try:
591                result_box["value"] = self._func(**args)
592            except BaseException as exc:  # propagate to caller
593                result_box["error"] = exc
594
595        thread = _start_daemon(_runner)
596        thread.join(timeout=self._timeout)
597        if thread.is_alive():
598            # Thread is still running past the deadline. We cannot forcefully
599            # kill it in CPython; leave it as a daemon and raise a timeout.
600            raise ToolTimeoutError(
601                f"Tool '{self._name}' exceeded timeout of {self._timeout}s"
602            )
603        if "error" in result_box:
604            raise result_box["error"]
605        return result_box.get("value")
606
607    async def _run_with_timeout_async(self, args: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
608        # ``asyncio.timeout`` is only available on Python 3.11+; ``wait_for``
609        # works on 3.10 (the project minimum) and is sufficient here.
610        try:
611            return await asyncio.wait_for(
612                self._invoke_async(args), timeout=self._timeout
613            )
614        except asyncio.TimeoutError as exc:
615            raise ToolTimeoutError(
616                f"Tool '{self._name}' exceeded timeout of {self._timeout}s"
617            ) from exc
618
619    def _handle_failure(self, exc: Exception) -> str:
620        handler = self._failure_error_function
621        if isinstance(handler, _DefaultFailureHandler):
622            return (
623                f"Tool '{self._name}' failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
624            )
625        if handler is None:
626            # ``failure_error_function=None`` means re-raise.
627            raise exc
628        return handler(exc)

A Tool generated from a Python function.

The function signature is inspected and a Pydantic input model is built so that execute validates arguments and the generated JSON Schema carries Field constraints. timeout (seconds) wraps execution; failure_error_function controls how exceptions are surfaced.

FunctionTool( func: Callable[..., Any], *, name_override: str | None = None, description_override: str | None = None, timeout: float | None = None, failure_error_function: Union[Callable[[Exception], str], vertai.core.tool._DefaultFailureHandler] = <vertai.core.tool._DefaultFailureHandler object>, timeout_mode: str = 'error_as_result') 
465    def __init__(
466        self,
467        func: Callable[..., Any],
468        *,
469        name_override: str | None = None,
470        description_override: str | None = None,
471        timeout: float | None = None,
472        failure_error_function: _FailureHandler | _DefaultFailureHandler = _DEFAULT_FAILURE_HANDLER,
473        timeout_mode: str = "error_as_result",
474    ) -> None:
475        if not callable(func):
476            raise TypeError(f"FunctionTool requires a callable, got {type(func)!r}")
477        self._func = func
478        self._name = name_override or func.__name__
479        summary, param_docs = _parse_docstring(func.__doc__)
480        self._description = description_override or summary or f"Tool {self._name}"
481        self._param_docs = param_docs
482        self._input_model = _build_param_model(func, param_docs)
483        self._parameters = _model_to_json_schema(self._input_model)
484        self._timeout = timeout
485        self._failure_error_function = failure_error_function
486        if timeout_mode not in ("error_as_result", "raise_exception"):
487            raise ValueError(
488                f"timeout_mode must be 'error_as_result' or 'raise_exception', "
489                f"got {timeout_mode!r}"
490            )
491        self._timeout_mode = timeout_mode
name: str 
495    @property
496    def name(self) -> str:
497        return self._name
description: str 
499    @property
500    def description(self) -> str:
501        return self._description
parameters: dict[str, typing.Any] 
503    @property
504    def parameters(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
505        # Return a shallow copy so callers cannot mutate cached schema.
506        return dict(self._parameters)

JSON Schema describing this tool's input parameters.

func: Callable[..., Any] 
508    @property
509    def func(self) -> Callable[..., Any]:
510        """The wrapped function (read-only)."""
511        return self._func

The wrapped function (read-only).

timeout: float | None 
513    @property
514    def timeout(self) -> float | None:
515        return self._timeout
def execute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str: 
523    def execute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
524        """Run the wrapped function synchronously with validated arguments.
525
526        ``timeout`` is enforced when set; on timeout the behaviour is
527        controlled by ``timeout_mode`` (``error_as_result`` returns a message,
528        ``raise_exception`` raises :class:`ToolTimeoutError`). Other
529        exceptions are routed through ``failure_error_function`` (default
530        friendly message; ``None`` re-raises).
531        """
532        try:
533            args = self._validate(kwargs)
534        except Exception as exc:  # validation errors
535            return self._handle_failure(exc)
536
537        try:
538            if self._timeout is None:
539                result = self._func(**args)
540            else:
541                result = self._run_with_timeout_sync(args)
542        except ToolTimeoutError:
543            if self._timeout_mode == "raise_exception":
544                raise
545            return f"Tool '{self._name}' timed out after {self._timeout}s."
546        except Exception as exc:
547            return self._handle_failure(exc)
548        return _stringify_result(result)

Run the wrapped function synchronously with validated arguments.

timeout is enforced when set; on timeout the behaviour is controlled by timeout_mode (error_as_result returns a message, raise_exception raises ToolTimeoutError). Other exceptions are routed through failure_error_function (default friendly message; None re-raises).

async def aexecute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str: 
550    async def aexecute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
551        """Run the wrapped function asynchronously.
552
553        If the wrapped function is a coroutine function it is awaited; any
554        other callable is run in a thread so the event loop is not blocked.
555        Timeout and failure handling mirror :meth:`execute`.
556        """
557        try:
558            args = self._validate(kwargs)
559        except Exception as exc:
560            return self._handle_failure(exc)
561
562        try:
563            if self._timeout is None:
564                result = await self._invoke_async(args)
565            else:
566                result = await self._run_with_timeout_async(args)
567        except ToolTimeoutError:
568            if self._timeout_mode == "raise_exception":
569                raise
570            return f"Tool '{self._name}' timed out after {self._timeout}s."
571        except Exception as exc:
572            return self._handle_failure(exc)
573        return _stringify_result(result)

Run the wrapped function asynchronously.

If the wrapped function is a coroutine function it is awaited; any other callable is run in a thread so the event loop is not blocked. Timeout and failure handling mirror execute().

class Tool(abc.ABC): 
406class Tool(ABC):
407    """Tool abstraction.
408
409    Subclasses expose a ``name``, ``description``, a JSON-Schema
410    ``parameters`` dict, and ``execute`` / ``aexecute`` entry points. The
411    result is currently a string (LLM-friendly); structured outputs are a
412    1.x extension point.
413    """
414
415    @property
416    @abstractmethod
417    def name(self) -> str: ...
418
419    @property
420    @abstractmethod
421    def description(self) -> str: ...
422
423    @property
424    @abstractmethod
425    def parameters(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
426        """JSON Schema describing this tool's input parameters."""
427        ...
428
429    @abstractmethod
430    def execute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
431        """Run the tool synchronously and return a string result."""
432
433    @abstractmethod
434    async def aexecute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
435        """Run the tool asynchronously and return a string result."""
436
437    def to_spec(self) -> ToolSpec:
438        """Render this tool as a :class:`ToolSpec` for LLMProvider consumption."""
439        return ToolSpec(
440            name=self.name,
441            description=self.description,
442            input_schema=self.parameters,
443        )

Tool abstraction.

Subclasses expose a name, description, a JSON-Schema parameters dict, and execute / aexecute entry points. The result is currently a string (LLM-friendly); structured outputs are a 1.x extension point.

name: str 
415    @property
416    @abstractmethod
417    def name(self) -> str: ...
description: str 
419    @property
420    @abstractmethod
421    def description(self) -> str: ...
parameters: dict[str, typing.Any] 
423    @property
424    @abstractmethod
425    def parameters(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
426        """JSON Schema describing this tool's input parameters."""
427        ...

JSON Schema describing this tool's input parameters.

@abstractmethod
def execute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str: 
429    @abstractmethod
430    def execute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
431        """Run the tool synchronously and return a string result."""

Run the tool synchronously and return a string result.

@abstractmethod
async def aexecute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str: 
433    @abstractmethod
434    async def aexecute(self, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
435        """Run the tool asynchronously and return a string result."""

Run the tool asynchronously and return a string result.

def to_spec(self) -> ToolSpec: 
437    def to_spec(self) -> ToolSpec:
438        """Render this tool as a :class:`ToolSpec` for LLMProvider consumption."""
439        return ToolSpec(
440            name=self.name,
441            description=self.description,
442            input_schema=self.parameters,
443        )
