class ToolError(builtins.RuntimeError): 
60class ToolError(RuntimeError):
61    """Raised when a tool is invoked before it has been registered, or when
62    arguments fail validation and no failure handler is configured."""

Raised when a tool is invoked before it has been registered, or when arguments fail validation and no failure handler is configured.

class ToolRegistry: 
732class ToolRegistry:
733    """Registry of tools, keyed by name.
734
735    ``to_specs()`` emits the :class:`ToolSpec` list consumed by
736    :class:`~vertai.core.provider.LLMProvider`. ``call()`` resolves a
737    :class:`~vertai.core.provider.ToolCall` returned by a provider into a
738    real tool invocation and string result, so an agent loop can wire
739    provider -> registry -> tool with one call.
740    """
741
742    def __init__(self, tools: list[Tool] | None = None) -> None:
743        self._tools: dict[str, Tool] = {}
744        if tools:
745            for t in tools:
746                self.register(t)
747
748    def register(self, tool: Tool) -> None:
749        """Register a tool. Raises :class:`ToolError` on duplicate names."""
750        if not isinstance(tool, Tool):
751            raise TypeError(
752                f"ToolRegistry.register requires a Tool, got {type(tool)!r}"
753            )
754        if tool.name in self._tools:
755            raise ToolError(f"Tool already registered with name '{tool.name}'")
756        self._tools[tool.name] = tool
757
758    def unregister(self, name: str) -> Tool | None:
759        """Remove and return a tool by name, or ``None`` if not present."""
760        return self._tools.pop(name, None)
761
762    def get(self, name: str) -> Tool:
763        """Return the tool registered under ``name``.
764
765        Raises :class:`ToolError` if no such tool is registered.
766        """
767        try:
768            return self._tools[name]
769        except KeyError:
770            raise ToolError(
771                f"No tool registered with name '{name}'. "
772                f"Known tools: {sorted(self._tools)}"
773            ) from None
774
775    def __contains__(self, name: object) -> bool:
776        return isinstance(name, str) and name in self._tools
777
778    def __len__(self) -> int:
779        return len(self._tools)
780
781    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[Tool]:
782        return iter(self._tools.values())
783
784    @property
785    def names(self) -> list[str]:
786        """Sorted list of registered tool names."""
787        return sorted(self._tools)
788
789    def to_specs(self) -> list[ToolSpec]:
790        """Emit :class:`ToolSpec` objects for every registered tool, in
791        deterministic name order."""
792        return [self._tools[name].to_spec() for name in sorted(self._tools)]
793
794    def call(self, name: str, arguments: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
795        """Resolve ``name`` and invoke ``execute(**arguments)`` synchronously."""
796        return self.get(name).execute(**arguments)
797
798    async def acall(self, name: str, arguments: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
799        """Resolve ``name`` and invoke ``aexecute(**arguments)``."""
800        return await self.get(name).aexecute(**arguments)

Registry of tools, keyed by name.

to_specs() emits the ToolSpec list consumed by ~vertai.core.provider.LLMProvider. call() resolves a ~vertai.core.provider.ToolCall returned by a provider into a real tool invocation and string result, so an agent loop can wire provider -> registry -> tool with one call.

ToolRegistry(tools: list[Tool] | None = None) 
742    def __init__(self, tools: list[Tool] | None = None) -> None:
743        self._tools: dict[str, Tool] = {}
744        if tools:
745            for t in tools:
746                self.register(t)
def register(self, tool: Tool) -> None: 
748    def register(self, tool: Tool) -> None:
749        """Register a tool. Raises :class:`ToolError` on duplicate names."""
750        if not isinstance(tool, Tool):
751            raise TypeError(
752                f"ToolRegistry.register requires a Tool, got {type(tool)!r}"
753            )
754        if tool.name in self._tools:
755            raise ToolError(f"Tool already registered with name '{tool.name}'")
756        self._tools[tool.name] = tool

760        return self._tools.pop(name, None)

def call(self, name: str, arguments: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> str: 
794    def call(self, name: str, arguments: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
795        """Resolve ``name`` and invoke ``execute(**arguments)`` synchronously."""
796        return self.get(name).execute(**arguments)

Resolve name and invoke execute(**arguments) synchronously.

async def acall(self, name: str, arguments: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> str: 
798    async def acall(self, name: str, arguments: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
799        """Resolve ``name`` and invoke ``aexecute(**arguments)``."""
800        return await self.get(name).aexecute(**arguments)

Resolve name and invoke aexecute(**arguments).

class ToolTimeoutError(builtins.TimeoutError): 
55class ToolTimeoutError(TimeoutError):
56    """Raised when a tool exceeds its configured ``timeout`` in
57    ``raise_exception`` mode."""

Raised when a tool exceeds its configured timeout in raise_exception mode.

calculator = <FunctionTool object>
file_read = <FunctionTool object>
file_write = <FunctionTool object>
http_request = <vertai.core.tools.http._HTTPRequestTool object>
def tool( func: Optional[Callable[..., Any]] = None, *, name: str | None = None, description: str | None = None, timeout: float | None = None, failure_error_function: Union[Callable[[Exception], str], vertai.core.tool._DefaultFailureHandler] = <vertai.core.tool._DefaultFailureHandler object>, timeout_mode: str = 'error_as_result', registry: ToolRegistry | None = None) -> Any: 
662def tool(
663    func: Callable[..., Any] | None = None,
664    *,
665    name: str | None = None,
666    description: str | None = None,
667    timeout: float | None = None,
668    failure_error_function: _FailureHandler | _DefaultFailureHandler = _DEFAULT_FAILURE_HANDLER,
669    timeout_mode: str = "error_as_result",
670    registry: "ToolRegistry | None" = None,
671) -> Any:
672    """Decorator that turns a Python function into a :class:`FunctionTool`.
673
674    Mirrors the OpenAI Agents SDK ``@function_tool`` shape:
675
676    * ``name`` / ``description`` override the function name / docstring
677      summary when supplied.
678    * ``timeout`` enforces a per-tool wall-clock budget. ``timeout_mode``
679      selects between ``error_as_result`` (default; return a timeout message
680      so the agent can recover) and ``raise_exception`` (raise
681      :class:`ToolTimeoutError`).
682    * ``failure_error_function`` controls how exceptions are surfaced to the
683      model. Default: a friendly ``"Tool '<name>' failed: ..."`` string.
684      Pass ``None`` to re-raise. Pass a callable to customise.
685    * ``registry`` optionally registers the produced tool immediately.
686
687    Usable bare (``@tool``) or parameterised (``@tool(name="...")``).
688    """
689    def _build(target: Callable[..., Any]) -> FunctionTool:
690        ft = FunctionTool(
691            target,
692            name_override=name,
693            description_override=description,
694            timeout=timeout,
695            failure_error_function=failure_error_function,
696            timeout_mode=timeout_mode,
697        )
698        if registry is not None:
699            registry.register(ft)
700        # Attach the tool to the function so callers can introspect it.
701        setattr(target, "__vertai_tool__", ft)
702        return ft
703
704    if func is not None and callable(func):
705        # Bare ``@tool`` usage.
706        return _build(func)
707    return _build

Decorator that turns a Python function into a FunctionTool.

Mirrors the OpenAI Agents SDK @function_tool shape:

  • name / description override the function name / docstring summary when supplied.
  • timeout enforces a per-tool wall-clock budget. timeout_mode selects between error_as_result (default; return a timeout message so the agent can recover) and raise_exception (raise ToolTimeoutError).
  • failure_error_function controls how exceptions are surfaced to the model. Default: a friendly "Tool '<name>' failed: ..." string. Pass None to re-raise. Pass a callable to customise.
  • registry optionally registers the produced tool immediately.

Usable bare (@tool) or parameterised (@tool(name="...")).

web_search = <vertai.core.tools.web_search._WebSearchTool object>
@dataclass
class Document: 
47@dataclass
48class Document:
49    """Document stored in a :class:`VectorStore`.
50
51    ``doc_id`` defaults to an md5 prefix of ``content`` so identical content
52    deduplicates naturally.
53    """
54
55    content: str
56    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
57    doc_id: str = ""
58
59    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
60        if not self.doc_id:
61            self.doc_id = hashlib.md5(self.content.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]

Document stored in a VectorStore.

doc_id defaults to an md5 prefix of content so identical content deduplicates naturally.

Document( content: str, metadata: dict[str, typing.Any] = <factory>, doc_id: str = '')
content: str
metadata: dict[str, typing.Any]
doc_id: str = ''
class VectorEngine: 
407class VectorEngine:
408    """Facade composing an :class:`EmbeddingProvider` and a :class:`VectorStore`.
409
410    Selects a backend by ``store_type`` (``memory`` / ``chroma`` / ``faiss`` /
411    ``auto``). ``auto`` honors the documented priority ChromaDB > FAISS >
412    InMemory (previously FAISS was never selected).
413
414    C2 fix: there is no hash-random fallback. If no embedding provider is
415    configured, :meth:`index_documents` and :meth:`search` raise explicitly
416    rather than silently producing non-semantic vectors. Construct the engine
417    freely (e.g. ``VectorEngine(store_type="memory")``) and inject a provider
418    via ``embedding_provider=`` (preferred) or the legacy ``embedding_fn=``
419    (wrapped in :class:`FunctionEmbeddingProvider`).
420
421    Example:
422        from vertai.core.embedding import LocalSentenceTransformerProvider
423        from vertai.core.vector import VectorEngine, Document
424
425        provider = LocalSentenceTransformerProvider()
426        engine = VectorEngine(embedding_provider=provider, store_type="memory")
427        engine.index_documents([Document(content="...")])
428        results = engine.search("query")
429    """
430
431    def __init__(
432        self,
433        config: VectorConfig | None = None,
434        store_type: str = "auto",
435        embedding_provider: EmbeddingProvider | None = None,
436        embedding_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], list[float]]] = None,
437    ) -> None:
438        self.config = config or VectorConfig()
439        if embedding_provider is not None:
440            self._embedding: EmbeddingProvider | None = embedding_provider
441        elif embedding_fn is not None:
442            self._embedding = FunctionEmbeddingProvider(embedding_fn)
443        else:
444            # No default hash-random provider (C2 fix).
445            self._embedding = None
446        self._store: VectorStore | None = None
447        self._store_type = store_type
448
449    @property
450    def store(self) -> VectorStore:
451        if self._store is None:
452            self._store = self._create_store()
453        return self._store
454
455    @property
456    def embedding(self) -> EmbeddingProvider | None:
457        """The configured embedding provider, or ``None`` if none set."""
458        return self._embedding
459
460    def get_embedding(self) -> EmbeddingProvider:
461        """Return the embedding provider or raise (C2 fix: no silent random)."""
462        if self._embedding is None:
463            raise RuntimeError(
464                "No EmbeddingProvider configured. VectorEngine no longer falls "
465                "back to random vectors (they produced non-semantic results). "
466                "Inject one via VectorEngine(embedding_provider=...) or "
467                "embedding_fn=..., or install 'vertai[embeddings]' and use "
468                "LocalSentenceTransformerProvider."
469            )
470        return self._embedding
471
472    def _create_store(self) -> VectorStore:
473        store_type = self._store_type
474        if store_type == "memory":
475            return InMemoryVectorStore()
476        if store_type == "chroma":
477            if not ChromaVectorStore.is_available():
478                raise RuntimeError(
479                    "ChromaDB not installed. Install with: pip install chromadb"
480                )
481            return ChromaVectorStore(
482                collection_name=self.config.collection_name,
483                persist_directory=self.config.persist_directory,
484            )
485        if store_type == "faiss":
486            if not FAISSVectorStore.is_available():
487                raise RuntimeError(
488                    "FAISS not installed. Install with: pip install faiss-cpu"
489                )
490            return FAISSVectorStore(dimension=self._infer_dimension())
491
492        # auto: ChromaDB > FAISS > InMemory (honest priority).
493        if ChromaVectorStore.is_available():
494            logger.info("auto: using ChromaDB as vector store")
495            return ChromaVectorStore(
496                collection_name=self.config.collection_name,
497                persist_directory=self.config.persist_directory,
498            )
499        if FAISSVectorStore.is_available():
500            logger.info("auto: using FAISS as vector store")
501            return FAISSVectorStore(dimension=self._infer_dimension())
502        logger.info("auto: using in-memory vector store")
503        return InMemoryVectorStore()
504
505    def _infer_dimension(self) -> int:
506        if self._embedding is not None:
507            return self._embedding.dimension
508        return 384
509
510    def index_documents(self, documents: list[Document]) -> list[str]:
511        """Embed (via the provider) and store ``documents``. Returns their ids."""
512        if not documents:
513            return []
514        provider = self.get_embedding()
515        embeddings = provider.embed([doc.content for doc in documents])
516        self.store.add(documents, embeddings)
517        return [doc.doc_id for doc in documents]
518
519    def search(
520        self, query: str, top_k: int | None = None
521    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
522        """Embed ``query`` and search the store."""
523        provider = self.get_embedding()
524        query_vector = provider.embed(query)[0]
525        return self.store.search(query_vector, top_k=top_k or self.config.top_k)
526
527    def delete_documents(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
528        """Delete documents by id."""
529        self.store.delete(ids)
530
531    def count(self) -> int:
532        """Live document count."""
533        return self.store.count()
534
535    def hybrid_search(
536        self,
537        query: str,
538        keywords: list[str] | None = None,
539        top_k: int | None = None,
540        alpha: float = 0.7,
541    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
542        """Hybrid retrieval: vector similarity + keyword overlap fusion.
543
544        ``alpha`` is the vector-score weight (``1 - alpha`` is the keyword
545        weight). Without ``keywords`` this degenerates to plain vector search.
546        """
547        top_k = top_k or self.config.top_k
548        vector_results = self.search(query, top_k=top_k * 2)
549        if not keywords:
550            return vector_results[:top_k]
551        scored = self._compute_keyword_scores(vector_results, keywords, alpha)
552        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x.score, reverse=True)
553        return scored[:top_k]
554
555    def _compute_keyword_scores(
556        self,
557        results: list[SearchResult],
558        keywords: list[str],
559        alpha: float,
560    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
561        keywords_lower = [kw.lower() for kw in keywords]
562        scored: list[SearchResult] = []
563        for result in results:
564            content_lower = result.document.content.lower()
565            keyword_score = sum(
566                1 for kw in keywords_lower if kw in content_lower
567            ) / len(keywords)
568            final_score = alpha * result.score + (1.0 - alpha) * keyword_score
569            scored.append(
570                SearchResult(
571                    document=result.document,
572                    score=final_score,
573                    distance=result.distance,
574                )
575            )
576        return scored

Facade composing an EmbeddingProvider and a VectorStore.

Selects a backend by store_type (memory / chroma / faiss / auto). auto honors the documented priority ChromaDB > FAISS > InMemory (previously FAISS was never selected).

C2 fix: there is no hash-random fallback. If no embedding provider is configured, index_documents() and search() raise explicitly rather than silently producing non-semantic vectors. Construct the engine freely (e.g. VectorEngine(store_type="memory")) and inject a provider via embedding_provider= (preferred) or the legacy embedding_fn= (wrapped in FunctionEmbeddingProvider).

Example: from vertai.core.embedding import LocalSentenceTransformerProvider from vertai.core.vector import VectorEngine, Document

provider = LocalSentenceTransformerProvider()
engine = VectorEngine(embedding_provider=provider, store_type="memory")
engine.index_documents([Document(content="...")])
results = engine.search("query")
VectorEngine( config: VectorConfig | None = None, store_type: str = 'auto', embedding_provider: EmbeddingProvider | None = None, embedding_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], list[float]]] = None) 
431    def __init__(
432        self,
433        config: VectorConfig | None = None,
434        store_type: str = "auto",
435        embedding_provider: EmbeddingProvider | None = None,
436        embedding_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], list[float]]] = None,
437    ) -> None:
438        self.config = config or VectorConfig()
439        if embedding_provider is not None:
440            self._embedding: EmbeddingProvider | None = embedding_provider
441        elif embedding_fn is not None:
442            self._embedding = FunctionEmbeddingProvider(embedding_fn)
443        else:
444            # No default hash-random provider (C2 fix).
445            self._embedding = None
446        self._store: VectorStore | None = None
447        self._store_type = store_type
config
store: VectorStore 
449    @property
450    def store(self) -> VectorStore:
451        if self._store is None:
452            self._store = self._create_store()
453        return self._store
embedding: EmbeddingProvider | None 
455    @property
456    def embedding(self) -> EmbeddingProvider | None:
457        """The configured embedding provider, or ``None`` if none set."""
458        return self._embedding

The configured embedding provider, or None if none set.

def get_embedding(self) -> EmbeddingProvider: 
460    def get_embedding(self) -> EmbeddingProvider:
461        """Return the embedding provider or raise (C2 fix: no silent random)."""
462        if self._embedding is None:
463            raise RuntimeError(
464                "No EmbeddingProvider configured. VectorEngine no longer falls "
465                "back to random vectors (they produced non-semantic results). "
466                "Inject one via VectorEngine(embedding_provider=...) or "
467                "embedding_fn=..., or install 'vertai[embeddings]' and use "
468                "LocalSentenceTransformerProvider."
469            )
470        return self._embedding

Return the embedding provider or raise (C2 fix: no silent random).

def index_documents(self, documents: list[Document]) -> list[str]: 
510    def index_documents(self, documents: list[Document]) -> list[str]:
511        """Embed (via the provider) and store ``documents``. Returns their ids."""
512        if not documents:
513            return []
514        provider = self.get_embedding()
515        embeddings = provider.embed([doc.content for doc in documents])
516        self.store.add(documents, embeddings)
517        return [doc.doc_id for doc in documents]

Embed (via the provider) and store documents. Returns their ids.

def search( self, query: str, top_k: int | None = None) -> list[SearchResult]: 
519    def search(
520        self, query: str, top_k: int | None = None
521    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
522        """Embed ``query`` and search the store."""
523        provider = self.get_embedding()
524        query_vector = provider.embed(query)[0]
525        return self.store.search(query_vector, top_k=top_k or self.config.top_k)

Embed query and search the store.

def delete_documents(self, ids: list[str]) -> None: 
527    def delete_documents(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
528        """Delete documents by id."""
529        self.store.delete(ids)

Delete documents by id.

def count(self) -> int:

532        """Live document count."""

def hybrid_search( self, query: str, keywords: list[str] | None = None, top_k: int | None = None, alpha: float = 0.7) -> list[SearchResult]: 
535    def hybrid_search(
536        self,
537        query: str,
538        keywords: list[str] | None = None,
539        top_k: int | None = None,
540        alpha: float = 0.7,
541    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
542        """Hybrid retrieval: vector similarity + keyword overlap fusion.
543
544        ``alpha`` is the vector-score weight (``1 - alpha`` is the keyword
545        weight). Without ``keywords`` this degenerates to plain vector search.
546        """
547        top_k = top_k or self.config.top_k
548        vector_results = self.search(query, top_k=top_k * 2)
549        if not keywords:
550            return vector_results[:top_k]
551        scored = self._compute_keyword_scores(vector_results, keywords, alpha)
552        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x.score, reverse=True)
553        return scored[:top_k]

Hybrid retrieval: vector similarity + keyword overlap fusion.

alpha is the vector-score weight (1 - alpha is the keyword weight). Without keywords this degenerates to plain vector search.

@dataclass
class VectorConfig: 
73@dataclass
74class VectorConfig:
75    """Configuration for :class:`VectorEngine` and its backends."""
76
77    collection_name: str = "default"
78    persist_directory: str | None = None
79    chunk_size: int = 512
80    chunk_overlap: int = 50

Configuration for VectorEngine and its backends.

VectorConfig( collection_name: str = 'default', persist_directory: str | None = None, chunk_size: int = 512, chunk_overlap: int = 50, top_k: int = 5)
collection_name: str = 'default'

chunk_size: int = 512

class VectorStore(abc.ABC): 
 90class VectorStore(ABC):
 91    """Vector store abstraction. Stores documents + precomputed embeddings."""
 92
 93    @abstractmethod
 94    def add(
 95        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
 96    ) -> None:
 97        """Add ``documents`` with their precomputed ``embeddings``.
 98
 99        Embeddings are computed externally by an
100        :class:`~vertai.core.embedding.EmbeddingProvider`; the store never
101        embeds text itself. The two lists must have equal length.
102        """
103
104    @abstractmethod
105    def search(
106        self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5
107    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
108        """Search by a precomputed ``query_embedding`` vector."""
109
110    @abstractmethod
111    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
112        """Delete documents by id. Real deletion: ``count`` and ``search`` must
113        stay consistent afterwards (fixes C3)."""
114
115    @abstractmethod
116    def count(self) -> int:
117        """Number of live documents."""

Vector store abstraction. Stores documents + precomputed embeddings.

@abstractmethod
def add( self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]) -> None: 
 93    @abstractmethod
 94    def add(
 95        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
 96    ) -> None:
 97        """Add ``documents`` with their precomputed ``embeddings``.
 98
 99        Embeddings are computed externally by an
100        :class:`~vertai.core.embedding.EmbeddingProvider`; the store never
101        embeds text itself. The two lists must have equal length.
102        """

Add documents with their precomputed embeddings.

107    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
108        """Search by a precomputed ``query_embedding`` vector."""

Search by a precomputed query_embedding vector.

110    @abstractmethod
111    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
112        """Delete documents by id. Real deletion: ``count`` and ``search`` must
113        stay consistent afterwards (fixes C3)."""

Delete documents by id. Real deletion: count and search must stay consistent afterwards (fixes C3).

class InMemoryVectorStore(vertai.VectorStore): 
120class InMemoryVectorStore(VectorStore):
121    """In-memory vector store using cosine similarity.
122
123    Suitable for tests and small datasets. No external dependencies.
124    """
125
126    def __init__(self) -> None:
127        self._documents: dict[str, Document] = {}
128        self._vectors: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
129
130    def add(
131        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
132    ) -> None:
133        if len(documents) != len(embeddings):
134            raise ValueError(
135                "documents and embeddings must have the same length "
136                f"({len(documents)} vs {len(embeddings)})"
137            )
138        for doc, vec in zip(documents, embeddings):
139            self._documents[doc.doc_id] = doc
140            self._vectors[doc.doc_id] = list(vec)
141
142    def search(
143        self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5
144    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
145        if not self._documents:
146            return []
147        scored: list[tuple[str, float, float]] = []
148        for doc_id, doc in self._documents.items():
149            score = self._cosine_similarity(query_embedding, self._vectors[doc_id])
150            scored.append((doc_id, score, 1.0 - score))
151        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
152        return [
153            SearchResult(
154                document=self._documents[doc_id],
155                score=score,
156                distance=distance,
157            )
158            for doc_id, score, distance in scored[:top_k]
159        ]
160
161    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
162        for doc_id in ids:
163            self._documents.pop(doc_id, None)
164            self._vectors.pop(doc_id, None)
165
166    def count(self) -> int:
167        return len(self._documents)
168
169    @staticmethod
170    def _cosine_similarity(a: list[float], b: list[float]) -> float:
171        dot = 0.0
172        norm_a_sq = 0.0
173        norm_b_sq = 0.0
174        for x, y in zip(a, b):
175            dot += x * y
176            norm_a_sq += x * x
177            norm_b_sq += y * y
178        if norm_a_sq == 0.0 or norm_b_sq == 0.0:
179            return 0.0
180        return dot / (math.sqrt(norm_a_sq) * math.sqrt(norm_b_sq))

In-memory vector store using cosine similarity.

Suitable for tests and small datasets. No external dependencies.

def add( self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]) -> None: 
130    def add(
131        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
132    ) -> None:
133        if len(documents) != len(embeddings):
134            raise ValueError(
135                "documents and embeddings must have the same length "
136                f"({len(documents)} vs {len(embeddings)})"
137            )
138        for doc, vec in zip(documents, embeddings):
139            self._documents[doc.doc_id] = doc
140            self._vectors[doc.doc_id] = list(vec)

Add documents with their precomputed embeddings.

Embeddings are computed externally by an ~vertai.core.embedding.EmbeddingProvider; the store never embeds text itself. The two lists must have equal length.

def search( self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]: 
142    def search(
143        self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5
144    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
145        if not self._documents:
146            return []
147        scored: list[tuple[str, float, float]] = []
148        for doc_id, doc in self._documents.items():
149            score = self._cosine_similarity(query_embedding, self._vectors[doc_id])
150            scored.append((doc_id, score, 1.0 - score))
151        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
152        return [
153            SearchResult(
154                document=self._documents[doc_id],
155                score=score,
156                distance=distance,
157            )
158            for doc_id, score, distance in scored[:top_k]
159        ]

Search by a precomputed query_embedding vector.

def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None: 
161    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
162        for doc_id in ids:
163            self._documents.pop(doc_id, None)
164            self._vectors.pop(doc_id, None)

Delete documents by id. Real deletion: count and search must stay consistent afterwards (fixes C3).

def count(self) -> int:

167        return len(self._documents)

Number of live documents.

class ChromaVectorStore(vertai.VectorStore): 
183class ChromaVectorStore(VectorStore):
184    """ChromaDB-backed vector store. Supports persistence.
185
186    Requires the ``chromadb`` package. Embeddings are supplied to
187    :meth:`add` (the store does not embed text).
188    """
189
190    def __init__(
191        self,
192        collection_name: str = "default",
193        persist_directory: str | None = None,
194    ) -> None:
195        self.collection_name = collection_name
196        self.persist_directory = persist_directory
197        self._client: Any = None
198        self._collection: Any = None
199        self._initialized = False
200
201    @staticmethod
202    def is_available() -> bool:
203        """Check whether ChromaDB is importable."""
204        try:
205            import chromadb  # noqa: F401
206        except ImportError:
207            return False
208        return True
209
210    def _init_chroma(self) -> None:
211        if self._initialized:
212            return
213        try:
214            import chromadb
215        except ImportError as e:
216            raise RuntimeError(
217                "ChromaDB not installed. Install with: pip install chromadb"
218            ) from e
219
220        if self.persist_directory:
221            self._client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path=self.persist_directory)
222        else:
223            self._client = chromadb.EphemeralClient()
224        self._collection = self._client.get_or_create_collection(
225            name=self.collection_name,
226        )
227        self._initialized = True
228        logger.info("ChromaDB initialized: %s", self.collection_name)
229
230    def add(
231        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
232    ) -> None:
233        self._init_chroma()
234        if not documents:
235            return
236        if len(documents) != len(embeddings):
237            raise ValueError(
238                "documents and embeddings must have the same length "
239                f"({len(documents)} vs {len(embeddings)})"
240            )
241        self._collection.add(
242            ids=[doc.doc_id for doc in documents],
243            documents=[doc.content for doc in documents],
244            metadatas=[doc.metadata for doc in documents],
245            embeddings=embeddings,
246        )
247
248    def search(
249        self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5
250    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
251        self._init_chroma()
252        results = self._collection.query(
253            query_embeddings=[query_embedding],
254            n_results=top_k,
255            include=["documents", "metadatas", "distances"],
256        )
257        search_results: list[SearchResult] = []
258        if results and results.get("ids"):
259            ids_row = results["ids"][0]
260            docs_row = results["documents"][0] if results.get("documents") else []
261            metas_row = (
262                results["metadatas"][0] if results.get("metadatas") else []
263            )
264            dists_row = (
265                results["distances"][0] if results.get("distances") else []
266            )
267            for i, doc_id in enumerate(ids_row):
268                content = docs_row[i] if i < len(docs_row) else ""
269                metadata = metas_row[i] if i < len(metas_row) else {}
270                distance = float(dists_row[i]) if i < len(dists_row) else 0.0
271                score = 1.0 / (1.0 + distance) if distance >= 0 else 0.0
272                search_results.append(
273                    SearchResult(
274                        document=Document(
275                            doc_id=doc_id, content=content, metadata=metadata
276                        ),
277                        score=score,
278                        distance=distance,
279                    )
280                )
281        return search_results
282
283    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
284        self._init_chroma()
285        if not ids:
286            return
287        self._collection.delete(ids=ids)
288
289    def count(self) -> int:
290        self._init_chroma()
291        return int(self._collection.count())

ChromaDB-backed vector store. Supports persistence.

Requires the chromadb package. Embeddings are supplied to add() (the store does not embed text).

ChromaVectorStore( collection_name: str = 'default', persist_directory: str | None = None) 
190    def __init__(
191        self,
192        collection_name: str = "default",
193        persist_directory: str | None = None,
194    ) -> None:
195        self.collection_name = collection_name
196        self.persist_directory = persist_directory
197        self._client: Any = None
198        self._collection: Any = None
199        self._initialized = False
collection_name
persist_directory
@staticmethod
def is_available() -> bool: 
201    @staticmethod
202    def is_available() -> bool:
203        """Check whether ChromaDB is importable."""
204        try:
205            import chromadb  # noqa: F401
206        except ImportError:
207            return False
208        return True

Check whether ChromaDB is importable.

def add( self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]) -> None: 
230    def add(
231        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
232    ) -> None:
233        self._init_chroma()
234        if not documents:
235            return
236        if len(documents) != len(embeddings):
237            raise ValueError(
238                "documents and embeddings must have the same length "
239                f"({len(documents)} vs {len(embeddings)})"
240            )
241        self._collection.add(
242            ids=[doc.doc_id for doc in documents],
243            documents=[doc.content for doc in documents],
244            metadatas=[doc.metadata for doc in documents],
245            embeddings=embeddings,
246        )

Add documents with their precomputed embeddings.

Embeddings are computed externally by an ~vertai.core.embedding.EmbeddingProvider; the store never embeds text itself. The two lists must have equal length.

def search( self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]: 
248    def search(
249        self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5
250    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
251        self._init_chroma()
252        results = self._collection.query(
253            query_embeddings=[query_embedding],
254            n_results=top_k,
255            include=["documents", "metadatas", "distances"],
256        )
257        search_results: list[SearchResult] = []
258        if results and results.get("ids"):
259            ids_row = results["ids"][0]
260            docs_row = results["documents"][0] if results.get("documents") else []
261            metas_row = (
262                results["metadatas"][0] if results.get("metadatas") else []
263            )
264            dists_row = (
265                results["distances"][0] if results.get("distances") else []
266            )
267            for i, doc_id in enumerate(ids_row):
268                content = docs_row[i] if i < len(docs_row) else ""
269                metadata = metas_row[i] if i < len(metas_row) else {}
270                distance = float(dists_row[i]) if i < len(dists_row) else 0.0
271                score = 1.0 / (1.0 + distance) if distance >= 0 else 0.0
272                search_results.append(
273                    SearchResult(
274                        document=Document(
275                            doc_id=doc_id, content=content, metadata=metadata
276                        ),
277                        score=score,
278                        distance=distance,
279                    )
280                )
281        return search_results

Search by a precomputed query_embedding vector.

def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None: 
283    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
284        self._init_chroma()
285        if not ids:
286            return
287        self._collection.delete(ids=ids)

Delete documents by id. Real deletion: count and search must stay consistent afterwards (fixes C3).

def count(self) -> int: 
289    def count(self) -> int:
290        self._init_chroma()
291        return int(self._collection.count())

Number of live documents.

class FAISSVectorStore(vertai.VectorStore): 
294class FAISSVectorStore(VectorStore):
295    """FAISS-backed vector store (``IndexFlatIP``, inner product on normalized
296    vectors).
297
298    Requires the ``faiss-cpu`` (or ``faiss-gpu``) and ``numpy`` packages.
299
300    FAISS ``IndexFlatIP`` does not support deletion; deleted documents are
301    removed from the live document map and filtered out of search results, and
302    :meth:`count` reports the live count (not ``index.ntotal``). This keeps
303    ``count`` and ``search`` consistent after deletion (fixes C3). Stale vectors
304    remain in the index and are compacted only by rebuilding — acceptable for
305    1.0; a ``compact`` method can arrive in 1.x.
306    """
307
308    def __init__(self, dimension: int = 384) -> None:
309        self.dimension = dimension
310        self._index: Any = None
311        self._documents: dict[int, Document] = {}
312        self._id_to_idx: dict[str, int] = {}
313        self._id_counter = 0
314        self._initialized = False
315        self._np: Any = None
316
317    @staticmethod
318    def is_available() -> bool:
319        """Check whether both ``faiss`` and ``numpy`` are importable."""
320        try:
321            import faiss  # noqa: F401
322            import numpy  # noqa: F401
323        except ImportError:
324            return False
325        return True
326
327    def _init_faiss(self) -> None:
328        if self._initialized:
329            return
330        try:
331            import faiss
332            import numpy as _np
333        except ImportError as e:
334            raise RuntimeError(
335                "FAISS not installed. Install with: pip install faiss-cpu"
336            ) from e
337        self._np = _np
338        self._index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(self.dimension)
339        self._initialized = True
340        logger.info("FAISS initialized with dimension %d", self.dimension)
341
342    def add(
343        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
344    ) -> None:
345        self._init_faiss()
346        if not documents:
347            return
348        if len(documents) != len(embeddings):
349            raise ValueError(
350                "documents and embeddings must have the same length "
351                f"({len(documents)} vs {len(embeddings)})"
352            )
353        if not embeddings:
354            return
355        dim = len(embeddings[0])
356        if dim != self.dimension:
357            raise ValueError(
358                f"embedding dimension {dim} does not match store dimension "
359                f"{self.dimension}"
360            )
361        vectors = self._np.array(embeddings, dtype=self._np.float32)
362        self._index.add(vectors)
363        for doc in documents:
364            idx = self._id_counter
365            self._documents[idx] = doc
366            self._id_to_idx[doc.doc_id] = idx
367            self._id_counter += 1
368
369    def search(
370        self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5
371    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
372        self._init_faiss()
373        if not self._documents:
374            return []
375        query_vector = self._np.array([query_embedding], dtype=self._np.float32)
376        k = min(top_k, len(self._documents))
377        scores, indices = self._index.search(query_vector, k)
378        results: list[SearchResult] = []
379        scores_row = scores[0]
380        indices_row = indices[0]
381        for i, idx in enumerate(indices_row):
382            idx_int = int(idx)
383            # C3 fix: filter out deleted / stale indices.
384            if idx_int in self._documents:
385                doc = self._documents[idx_int]
386                score = float(scores_row[i])
387                results.append(
388                    SearchResult(
389                        document=doc, score=score, distance=1.0 - score
390                    )
391                )
392        return results
393
394    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
395        for doc_id in ids:
396            idx = self._id_to_idx.pop(doc_id, None)
397            if idx is not None:
398                self._documents.pop(idx, None)
399        # NOTE: stale vectors remain in the FAISS index but are filtered out of
400        # search results above; count() reports only live documents.
401
402    def count(self) -> int:
403        # C3 fix: live documents, not index.ntotal (which still counts deleted).
404        return len(self._documents)

FAISS-backed vector store (IndexFlatIP, inner product on normalized vectors).

Requires the faiss-cpu (or faiss-gpu) and numpy packages.

FAISS IndexFlatIP does not support deletion; deleted documents are removed from the live document map and filtered out of search results, and count() reports the live count (not index.ntotal). This keeps count and search consistent after deletion (fixes C3). Stale vectors remain in the index and are compacted only by rebuilding — acceptable for 1.0; a compact method can arrive in 1.x.

FAISSVectorStore(dimension: int = 384) 
308    def __init__(self, dimension: int = 384) -> None:
309        self.dimension = dimension
310        self._index: Any = None
311        self._documents: dict[int, Document] = {}
312        self._id_to_idx: dict[str, int] = {}
313        self._id_counter = 0
314        self._initialized = False
315        self._np: Any = None
dimension
@staticmethod
def is_available() -> bool: 
317    @staticmethod
318    def is_available() -> bool:
319        """Check whether both ``faiss`` and ``numpy`` are importable."""
320        try:
321            import faiss  # noqa: F401
322            import numpy  # noqa: F401
323        except ImportError:
324            return False
325        return True

Check whether both faiss and numpy are importable.

def add( self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]) -> None: 
342    def add(
343        self, documents: list[Document], embeddings: list[list[float]]
344    ) -> None:
345        self._init_faiss()
346        if not documents:
347            return
348        if len(documents) != len(embeddings):
349            raise ValueError(
350                "documents and embeddings must have the same length "
351                f"({len(documents)} vs {len(embeddings)})"
352            )
353        if not embeddings:
354            return
355        dim = len(embeddings[0])
356        if dim != self.dimension:
357            raise ValueError(
358                f"embedding dimension {dim} does not match store dimension "
359                f"{self.dimension}"
360            )
361        vectors = self._np.array(embeddings, dtype=self._np.float32)
362        self._index.add(vectors)
363        for doc in documents:
364            idx = self._id_counter
365            self._documents[idx] = doc
366            self._id_to_idx[doc.doc_id] = idx
367            self._id_counter += 1

Add documents with their precomputed embeddings.

Embeddings are computed externally by an ~vertai.core.embedding.EmbeddingProvider; the store never embeds text itself. The two lists must have equal length.

def search( self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5) -> list[SearchResult]: 
369    def search(
370        self, query_embedding: list[float], *, top_k: int = 5
371    ) -> list[SearchResult]:
372        self._init_faiss()
373        if not self._documents:
374            return []
375        query_vector = self._np.array([query_embedding], dtype=self._np.float32)
376        k = min(top_k, len(self._documents))
377        scores, indices = self._index.search(query_vector, k)
378        results: list[SearchResult] = []
379        scores_row = scores[0]
380        indices_row = indices[0]
381        for i, idx in enumerate(indices_row):
382            idx_int = int(idx)
383            # C3 fix: filter out deleted / stale indices.
384            if idx_int in self._documents:
385                doc = self._documents[idx_int]
386                score = float(scores_row[i])
387                results.append(
388                    SearchResult(
389                        document=doc, score=score, distance=1.0 - score
390                    )
391                )
392        return results

Search by a precomputed query_embedding vector.

def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None: 
394    def delete(self, ids: list[str]) -> None:
395        for doc_id in ids:
396            idx = self._id_to_idx.pop(doc_id, None)
397            if idx is not None:
398                self._documents.pop(idx, None)
399        # NOTE: stale vectors remain in the FAISS index but are filtered out of
400        # search results above; count() reports only live documents.

Delete documents by id. Real deletion: count and search must stay consistent afterwards (fixes C3).

def count(self) -> int: 
402    def count(self) -> int:
403        # C3 fix: live documents, not index.ntotal (which still counts deleted).
404        return len(self._documents)

Number of live documents.

@dataclass
class SearchResult: 
64@dataclass
65class SearchResult:
66    """A single retrieval result: the matched document plus similarity score."""
67
68    document: Document
69    score: float
70    distance: float = 0.0

A single retrieval result: the matched document plus similarity score.

SearchResult( document: Document, score: float, distance: float = 0.0)
document: Document
score: float
distance: float = 0.0
class DocParser: 
325class DocParser:
326    """
327    Unified document parser supporting multiple file formats.
328
329    Supported formats: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, MD
330
331    Usage:
332        >>> from vertai.data import DocParser
333        >>> parser = DocParser()
334        >>> doc = parser.parse("report.pdf")
335        >>> print(doc["text"])
336        >>> print(doc["metadata"])
337    """
338
339    PARSERS: dict[str, type[BaseParser]] = {
340        ".pdf": PDFParser,
341        ".docx": WordParser,
342        ".xlsx": ExcelParser,
343        ".pptx": PPTParser,
344        ".md": MarkdownParser,
345        ".markdown": MarkdownParser,
346    }
347
348    def __init__(self) -> None:
349        self._parsers: dict[str, BaseParser] = {}
350
351    def _get_parser(self, extension: str) -> BaseParser:
352        """Get or create parser for given extension."""
353        ext = extension.lower()
354        if ext not in self._parsers:
355            parser_class = self.PARSERS.get(ext)
356            if not parser_class:
357                raise ValueError(
358                    f"Unsupported file format: {ext}. "
359                    f"Supported formats: {', '.join(self.PARSERS.keys())}"
360                )
361            self._parsers[ext] = parser_class()
362        return self._parsers[ext]
363
364    def parse(self, file_path: str | Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
365        """
366        Parse a document and return structured content.
367
368        Args:
369            file_path: Path to the document file.
370
371        Returns:
372            Dictionary containing:
373                - text: Full document text
374                - metadata: Document metadata (pages, author, etc.)
375                - chunks: List of content chunks with position info
376
377        Raises:
378            FileNotFoundError: If file doesn't exist.
379            ValueError: If file format is not supported.
380        """
381        path = Path(file_path)
382
383        if not path.exists():
384            raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}")
385
386        if not path.is_file():
387            raise ValueError(f"Not a file: {path}")
388
389        parser = self._get_parser(path.suffix)
390        result = parser.parse(path)
391        result["metadata"]["file_path"] = str(path.absolute())
392        result["metadata"]["file_name"] = path.name
393
394        return result
395
396    def supports(self, file_path: str | Path) -> bool:
397        """Check if file format is supported."""
398        path = Path(file_path)
399        return path.suffix.lower() in self.PARSERS
400
401    @property
402    def supported_formats(self) -> list[str]:
403        """Return list of supported file extensions."""
404        return list(self.PARSERS.keys())

Unified document parser supporting multiple file formats.

Supported formats: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, MD

Usage:

from vertai.data import DocParser parser = DocParser() doc = parser.parse("report.pdf") print(doc["text"]) print(doc["metadata"])

PARSERS: dict[str, type[vertai.data.parser.BaseParser]] = {'.pdf': <class 'vertai.data.parser.PDFParser'>, '.docx': <class 'vertai.data.parser.WordParser'>, '.xlsx': <class 'vertai.data.parser.ExcelParser'>, '.pptx': <class 'vertai.data.parser.PPTParser'>, '.md': <class 'vertai.data.parser.MarkdownParser'>, '.markdown': <class 'vertai.data.parser.MarkdownParser'>}
def parse(self, file_path: str | pathlib._local.Path) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
364    def parse(self, file_path: str | Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
365        """
366        Parse a document and return structured content.
367
368        Args:
369            file_path: Path to the document file.
370
371        Returns:
372            Dictionary containing:
373                - text: Full document text
374                - metadata: Document metadata (pages, author, etc.)
375                - chunks: List of content chunks with position info
376
377        Raises:
378            FileNotFoundError: If file doesn't exist.
379            ValueError: If file format is not supported.
380        """
381        path = Path(file_path)
382
383        if not path.exists():
384            raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}")
385
386        if not path.is_file():
387            raise ValueError(f"Not a file: {path}")
388
389        parser = self._get_parser(path.suffix)
390        result = parser.parse(path)
391        result["metadata"]["file_path"] = str(path.absolute())
392        result["metadata"]["file_name"] = path.name
393
394        return result

Parse a document and return structured content.

Args: file_path: Path to the document file.

Returns: Dictionary containing: - text: Full document text - metadata: Document metadata (pages, author, etc.) - chunks: List of content chunks with position info

Raises: FileNotFoundError: If file doesn't exist. ValueError: If file format is not supported.

def supports(self, file_path: str | pathlib._local.Path) -> bool: 
396    def supports(self, file_path: str | Path) -> bool:
397        """Check if file format is supported."""
398        path = Path(file_path)
399        return path.suffix.lower() in self.PARSERS

Check if file format is supported.

supported_formats: list[str] 
401    @property
402    def supported_formats(self) -> list[str]:
403        """Return list of supported file extensions."""
404        return list(self.PARSERS.keys())

Return list of supported file extensions.

class StructuredOutput: 
 79class StructuredOutput:
 80    """Extract structured data from unstructured text with schema validation.
 81
 82    Examples - LLM mode (recommended for production):
 83
 84        >>> from vertai import LLMEngine, LLMConfig, ModelProvider
 85        >>> llm = LLMEngine(LLMConfig(
 86        ...     provider=ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
 87        ...     api_key="sk-xxx",
 88        ... ))
 89        >>> schema = {"name": "string", "amount": "number"}
 90        >>> output = StructuredOutput(schema, llm=llm)
 91        >>> result = output.extract("张三报销500元")
 92        >>> result.data
 93        {'name': '张三', 'amount': 500}
 94
 95    Examples - local mode (testing only):
 96
 97        >>> schema = {"amount": "number"}
 98        >>> output = StructuredOutput(schema)
 99        >>> result = output.extract("金额500元")  # regex extraction
100    """
101
102    def __init__(
103        self,
104        schema: dict[str, str],
105        config: Optional[ExtractionConfig] = None,
106        *,
107        max_retries: int = DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES,
108        strict: bool = True,
109        llm: Optional["LLMEngine"] = None,
110    ) -> None:
111        """Initialize StructuredOutput with schema definition.
112
113        Args:
114            schema: Schema definition mapping field names to types.
115                    Types: "string", "number", "integer", "boolean",
116                           "enum[val1,val2,...]", "array", "object"
117            config: ExtractionConfig instance (overrides max_retries/strict).
118            max_retries: Maximum retry attempts for correction.
119            strict: If True, raise on validation failure after retries.
120            llm: LLMEngine for semantic extraction (recommended for production).
121        """
122        self.schema = schema
123        self._llm = llm
124        if config is not None:
125            self._config = config
126        else:
127            self._config = ExtractionConfig(max_retries=max_retries, strict=strict)
128
129        self._parsers: dict[str, Callable[[str, str, str], Any]] = {
130            "string": self._parse_string,
131            "number": self._parse_number,
132            "integer": self._parse_integer,
133            "boolean": self._parse_boolean,
134            "array": self._parse_array,
135            "object": self._parse_object,
136        }
137
138    @property
139    def max_retries(self) -> int:
140        return self._config.max_retries
141
142    @property
143    def strict(self) -> bool:
144        return self._config.strict
145
146    def extract(self, text: str) -> ExtractionResult:
147        """Extract structured data from text.
148
149        Uses the LLM for semantic extraction when one was provided; otherwise
150        falls back to local regex extraction (testing only).
151
152        Args:
153            text: Unstructured text to extract data from.
154
155        Returns:
156            ExtractionResult with data, success status, and retry count.
157
158        Raises:
159            SchemaValidationError: In strict mode when validation fails after
160                all retries.
161        """
162        # LLM mode: semantic extraction with schema validation + retry.
163        if self._llm is not None:
164            return self._extract_with_llm(text)
165
166        # Local mode: regex extraction.
167        result = self._extract_initial(text)
168
169        if result.success:
170            return result
171
172        # Retry with corrections.
173        for attempt in range(self.max_retries):
174            corrected = self._correct_extraction(text, result, attempt)
175            if corrected.success:
176                corrected.retries = attempt + 1
177                return corrected
178
179        # All retries exhausted.
180        if self.strict:
181            raise SchemaValidationError(
182                f"Failed to extract valid data after {self.max_retries} retries. "
183                "Schema validation failed for one or more fields."
184            )
185
186        return ExtractionResult(
187            data=result.data,
188            success=False,
189            retries=self.max_retries,
190            error=result.error
191        )
192
193    def _extract_with_llm(self, text: str) -> ExtractionResult:
194        """Extract using the LLM, then schema-validate with retry.
195
196        The raw LLM JSON is validated field-by-field via ``_validate_field``.
197        Invalid fields are dropped and the LLM is re-prompted with the
198        validation errors, up to ``max_retries`` times.
199        """
200        llm = self._llm
201        assert llm is not None  # narrowed by caller
202
203        schema_desc = json.dumps(self.schema, ensure_ascii=False)
204        prompt = (
205            "Extract structured data from the following text.\n\n"
206            f"Text: {text}\n\n"
207            f"Required fields and types:\n{schema_desc}\n\n"
208            "Return only a JSON object. If a field cannot be extracted, set it "
209            "to null."
210        )
211
212        last_error: Optional[str] = None
213        for attempt in range(self.max_retries + 1):
214            try:
215                result = llm.generate(prompt)
216                content = result.content.strip()
217
218                # Strip optional ```json ... ``` fences.
219                if "```json" in content:
220                    content = content.split("```json")[1].split("```")[0].strip()
221                elif "```" in content:
222                    content = content.split("```")[1].split("```")[0].strip()
223
224                data: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(content)
225            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
226                last_error = f"JSON parse error: {e}"
227                continue
228            except Exception as e:  # network / provider error
229                last_error = str(e)
230                continue
231
232            # Schema-validate every field. Collect failures so we can
233            # re-prompt the LLM with actionable feedback.
234            validation_errors: list[str] = []
235            validated_data: dict[str, Any] = {}
236            for field_name, field_type in self.schema.items():
237                value = data.get(field_name)
238                if value is None:
239                    validation_errors.append(
240                        f"{field_name}: missing or null"
241                    )
242                    continue
243                try:
244                    self._validate_field(value, field_type)
245                    validated_data[field_name] = value
246                except ValueError as e:
247                    validation_errors.append(f"{field_name}: {e}")
248
249            if not validation_errors:
250                return ExtractionResult(
251                    data=validated_data,
252                    success=True,
253                    retries=attempt,
254                )
255
256            last_error = "; ".join(validation_errors)
257            prompt = (
258                "Extract structured data from the following text.\n\n"
259                f"Text: {text}\n\n"
260                f"Required fields and types:\n{schema_desc}\n\n"
261                "Return only a JSON object. If a field cannot be extracted, "
262                "set it to null.\n\n"
263                f"Your previous answer had these validation errors:\n"
264                f"{last_error}\n\nPlease correct them."
265            )
266
267        # Exhausted retries.
268        if self.strict:
269            raise SchemaValidationError(
270                f"LLM extraction failed schema validation after "
271                f"{self.max_retries} retries: {last_error}"
272            )
273        return ExtractionResult(
274            data={},
275            success=False,
276            retries=self.max_retries,
277            error=last_error,
278        )
279
280    def _extract_initial(self, text: str) -> ExtractionResult:
281        """Perform initial extraction attempt."""
282        try:
283            data: dict[str, Any] = {}
284            errors: list[str] = []
285
286            for field_name, field_type in self.schema.items():
287                try:
288                    value = self._parse_field(text, field_name, field_type)
289                    data[field_name] = value
290                except ValueError:
291                    errors.append(field_name)
292
293            if errors:
294                return ExtractionResult(
295                    data=data,
296                    success=False,
297                    error=f"Failed fields: {', '.join(errors)}"
298                )
299
300            return ExtractionResult(data=data, success=True)
301
302        except (ValueError, TypeError, KeyError, re.error):
303            return ExtractionResult(
304                data={},
305                success=False,
306                error="Extraction failed"
307            )
308
309    def _correct_extraction(
310        self,
311        text: str,
312        previous_result: ExtractionResult,
313        attempt: int
314    ) -> ExtractionResult:
315        """Attempt to correct extraction based on previous failure.
316
317        Progressive strategies:
318        - Attempt 0: Relaxed parsing
319        - Attempt 1: Pattern-based extraction
320        - Attempt 2+: Aggressive fallbacks
321        """
322        # Pre-initialise so the except branch always has a defined binding
323        # (replaces the previous ``data if 'data' in dir() else {}`` introspection).
324        data: dict[str, Any] = {}
325        try:
326            for field_name, field_type in self.schema.items():
327                # Skip if already parsed correctly.
328                if field_name in previous_result.data and previous_result.data[field_name] is not None:
329                    try:
330                        self._validate_field(previous_result.data[field_name], field_type)
331                        data[field_name] = previous_result.data[field_name]
332                        continue
333                    except ValueError:
334                        pass
335
336                # Try corrected parsing.
337                value = self._parse_field_corrected(text, field_name, field_type, attempt)
338                data[field_name] = value
339
340            # Validate all fields.
341            for field_name, field_type in self.schema.items():
342                self._validate_field(data[field_name], field_type)
343
344            return ExtractionResult(data=data, success=True)
345
346        except (ValueError, TypeError, KeyError, re.error):
347            return ExtractionResult(
348                data=data,
349                success=False,
350                error="Correction failed"
351            )
352
353    def _parse_field(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> Any:
354        """Parse a single field from text."""
355        parser = self._get_parser(field_type)
356        return parser(text, field_name, field_type)
357
358    def _parse_field_corrected(
359        self,
360        text: str,
361        field_name: str,
362        field_type: str,
363        attempt: int
364    ) -> Any:
365        """Parse field with correction strategies."""
366        if attempt == 0:
367            return self._parse_relaxed(text, field_name, field_type)
368        elif attempt == 1:
369            return self._parse_pattern(text, field_name, field_type)
370        else:
371            return self._parse_aggressive(text, field_name, field_type)
372
373    def _get_parser(self, field_type: str) -> Callable[[str, str, str], Any]:
374        """Get appropriate parser for field type."""
375        if field_type.startswith("enum["):
376            return self._parse_enum
377
378        base_type = field_type.split("[")[0]
379        return self._parsers.get(base_type, self._parse_string)
380
381    def _parse_string(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> str:
382        """Extract a string value (generic).
383
384        For the small set of field names in ``_CHINESE_NAME_FIELD_HINTS`` we
385        apply a documented domain-example heuristic that recognises the common
386        Chinese ``<name>报销|采购|支付|预|于`` reimbursement/purchase phrasing.
387        For all other field names we return the first non-whitespace token,
388        which is a deliberately weak default — use the LLM mode for robust
389        extraction.
390        """
391        # Domain-example heuristic (NOT part of the general contract).
392        if field_name in _CHINESE_NAME_FIELD_HINTS:
393            name_match = _CHINESE_NAME_CONTEXT_PATTERN.search(text)
394            if name_match:
395                return name_match.group()
396
397        # Generic fallback: first non-whitespace token (works for both CJK
398        # runs and ASCII words).
399        match = re.search(r"\S+", text)
400        if match:
401            return match.group()
402
403        raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract string for '{field_name}'")
404
405    def _parse_number(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> float:
406        """Extract numeric value."""
407        # Look for numbers with currency units.
408        units_pattern = "|".join(CHINESE_CURRENCY_UNITS)
409        match = re.search(rf'(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:{units_pattern})', text)
410        if match:
411            return float(match.group(1))
412
413        # Plain number.
414        match = re.search(r'(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', text)
415        if match:
416            return float(match.group(1))
417
418        # Chinese number words.
419        for cn, num in CHINESE_NUMBERS.items():
420            if cn in text:
421                return float(num)
422
423        raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract number for '{field_name}'")
424
425    def _parse_integer(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> int:
426        """Extract integer value."""
427        num = self._parse_number(text, field_name, field_type)
428        return int(num)
429
430    def _parse_boolean(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> bool:
431        """Extract boolean value."""
432        true_patterns = r'(是|有|true|yes|1|对|真)'
433        false_patterns = r'(否|无|false|no|0|错|假)'
434
435        if re.search(true_patterns, text, re.IGNORECASE):
436            return True
437        if re.search(false_patterns, text, re.IGNORECASE):
438            return False
439
440        raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract boolean for '{field_name}'")
441
442    def _parse_enum(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> str:
443        """Extract enum value."""
444        match = re.match(r'enum\[(.+)\]', field_type)
445        if not match:
446            raise ValueError(f"Invalid enum type: {field_type}")
447
448        values = [v.strip() for v in match.group(1).split(",")]
449
450        for value in values:
451            if value in text:
452                return value
453
454        raise ValueError(f"Cannot match enum for '{field_name}'")
455
456    def _parse_array(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> list[Any]:
457        """Extract array value."""
458        # Try JSON array first.
459        match = re.search(r'\[.*?\]', text)
460        if match:
461            try:
462                parsed: Any = json.loads(match.group())
463                return list(parsed)
464            except json.JSONDecodeError:
465                # JSON failed but brackets found - strip them and parse as CSV.
466                bracket_content = match.group()
467                inner = bracket_content[1:-1]  # Remove [ and ]
468                items = re.split(r'[,，、]', inner)
469                return [item.strip() for item in items if item.strip()]
470
471        # No brackets - split by commas.
472        items = re.split(r'[,，、]', text)
473        return [item.strip() for item in items if item.strip()]
474
475    def _parse_object(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
476        """Extract object value."""
477        match = re.search(r'\{.*?\}', text)
478        if match:
479            try:
480                parsed_obj: Any = json.loads(match.group())
481                return dict(parsed_obj)
482            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
483                pass
484
485        return {}
486
487    def _parse_relaxed(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> Any:
488        """Parse with relaxed rules - still requires meaningful extraction."""
489        if field_type == "string":
490            # Domain-example heuristic for the name field family.
491            if field_name in _CHINESE_NAME_FIELD_HINTS:
492                match = _CHINESE_NAME_CONTEXT_PATTERN.search(text)
493                if match:
494                    return match.group()
495            match = re.search(r'[一-龥]+', text)
496            if match:
497                return match.group()
498            match = re.search(r'[^\s]+', text)
499            if match:
500                return match.group()
501            raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract string for '{field_name}'")
502
503        if field_type in ("number", "integer"):
504            match = re.search(r'\d+', text)
505            if match:
506                val = float(match.group())
507                return int(val) if field_type == "integer" else val
508            for cn, num in CHINESE_NUMBERS.items():
509                if cn in text:
510                    return num if field_type == "integer" else float(num)
511            raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract number for '{field_name}'")
512
513        if field_type.startswith("enum["):
514            match = re.match(r'enum\[(.+)\]', field_type)
515            if match:
516                values = [v.strip() for v in match.group(1).split(",")]
517                for value in values:
518                    if value in text:
519                        return value
520                raise ValueError(f"Cannot match enum for '{field_name}'")
521
522        if field_type == "boolean":
523            if re.search(r'(是|有|true|yes|1|对|真|active|激活|开)', text, re.IGNORECASE):
524                return True
525            if re.search(r'(否|无|false|no|0|错|假|inactive|关闭|关)', text, re.IGNORECASE):
526                return False
527            raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract boolean for '{field_name}'")
528
529        return None
530
531    def _parse_pattern(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> Any:
532        """Parse using predefined patterns."""
533        patterns: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {
534            ("amount", "number"): r'(\d+)',
535            ("name", "string"): r'([一-龥]+)',
536        }
537
538        key = (field_name, field_type)
539        if key in patterns:
540            match = re.search(patterns[key], text)
541            if match:
542                if field_type == "number":
543                    return float(match.group(1))
544                return match.group(1)
545
546        return self._parse_relaxed(text, field_name, field_type)
547
548    def _parse_aggressive(self, text: str, field_name: str, field_type: str) -> Any:
549        """Aggressive fallback parsing - last meaningful attempt."""
550        if field_type == "string":
551            # Domain-example heuristic for the name field family.
552            if field_name in _CHINESE_NAME_FIELD_HINTS:
553                match = _CHINESE_NAME_CONTEXT_PATTERN.search(text)
554                if match:
555                    return match.group()
556            match = re.search(r'[一-龥]+|\w+', text)
557            if match:
558                return match.group()
559            raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract string for '{field_name}'")
560
561        if field_type in ("number", "integer"):
562            match = re.search(r'[-+]?\d+', text)
563            if match:
564                val = float(match.group())
565                return int(val) if field_type == "integer" else val
566            raise ValueError(f"Cannot extract number for '{field_name}'")
567
568        if field_type.startswith("enum["):
569            match = re.match(r'enum\[(.+)\]', field_type)
570            if match:
571                values = [v.strip() for v in match.group(1).split(",")]
572                text_lower = text.lower()
573                for value in values:
574                    if value.lower() in text_lower or value in text:
575                        return value
576                raise ValueError(f"Cannot match enum for '{field_name}'")
577
578        if field_type == "boolean":
579            if re.search(r'\b(true|yes|1|是|有)\b', text, re.IGNORECASE):
580                return True
581            return False
582
583        return None
584
585    def _validate_field(self, value: Any, field_type: str) -> None:
586        """Validate a field value against its type."""
587        if value is None:
588            raise ValueError("Value cannot be None")
589
590        if field_type == "string":
591            if not isinstance(value, str):
592                raise ValueError(f"Expected string, got {type(value).__name__}")
593
594        elif field_type == "number":
595            if not isinstance(value, (int, float)):
596                raise ValueError(f"Expected number, got {type(value).__name__}")
597
598        elif field_type == "integer":
599            if not isinstance(value, int) or isinstance(value, bool):
600                raise ValueError(f"Expected integer, got {type(value).__name__}")
601
602        elif field_type == "boolean":
603            if not isinstance(value, bool):
604                raise ValueError(f"Expected boolean, got {type(value).__name__}")
605
606        elif field_type == "array":
607            if not isinstance(value, list):
608                raise ValueError(f"Expected array, got {type(value).__name__}")
609
610        elif field_type == "object":
611            if not isinstance(value, dict):
612                raise ValueError(f"Expected object, got {type(value).__name__}")
613
614        elif field_type.startswith("enum["):
615            match = re.match(r'enum\[(.+)\]', field_type)
616            if match:
617                valid_values = [v.strip() for v in match.group(1).split(",")]
618                if value not in valid_values:
619                    raise ValueError("Value not in valid enum values")

Extract structured data from unstructured text with schema validation.

Examples - LLM mode (recommended for production):

>>> from vertai import LLMEngine, LLMConfig, ModelProvider
>>> llm = LLMEngine(LLMConfig(
...     provider=ModelProvider.DEEPSEEK,
...     api_key="sk-xxx",
... ))
>>> schema = {"name": "string", "amount": "number"}
>>> output = StructuredOutput(schema, llm=llm)
>>> result = output.extract("张三报销500元")
>>> result.data
{'name': '张三', 'amount': 500}

Examples - local mode (testing only):

>>> schema = {"amount": "number"}
>>> output = StructuredOutput(schema)
>>> result = output.extract("金额500元")  # regex extraction
StructuredOutput( schema: dict[str, str], config: Optional[vertai.output.structured.ExtractionConfig] = None, *, max_retries: int = 3, strict: bool = True, llm: Optional[LLMEngine] = None) 
102    def __init__(
103        self,
104        schema: dict[str, str],
105        config: Optional[ExtractionConfig] = None,
106        *,
107        max_retries: int = DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES,
108        strict: bool = True,
109        llm: Optional["LLMEngine"] = None,
110    ) -> None:
111        """Initialize StructuredOutput with schema definition.
112
113        Args:
114            schema: Schema definition mapping field names to types.
115                    Types: "string", "number", "integer", "boolean",
116                           "enum[val1,val2,...]", "array", "object"
117            config: ExtractionConfig instance (overrides max_retries/strict).
118            max_retries: Maximum retry attempts for correction.
119            strict: If True, raise on validation failure after retries.
120            llm: LLMEngine for semantic extraction (recommended for production).
121        """
122        self.schema = schema
123        self._llm = llm
124        if config is not None:
125            self._config = config
126        else:
127            self._config = ExtractionConfig(max_retries=max_retries, strict=strict)
128
129        self._parsers: dict[str, Callable[[str, str, str], Any]] = {
130            "string": self._parse_string,
131            "number": self._parse_number,
132            "integer": self._parse_integer,
133            "boolean": self._parse_boolean,
134            "array": self._parse_array,
135            "object": self._parse_object,
136        }

Initialize StructuredOutput with schema definition.

Args: schema: Schema definition mapping field names to types. Types: "string", "number", "integer", "boolean", "enum[val1,val2,...]", "array", "object" config: ExtractionConfig instance (overrides max_retries/strict). max_retries: Maximum retry attempts for correction. strict: If True, raise on validation failure after retries. llm: LLMEngine for semantic extraction (recommended for production).

schema
max_retries: int 
138    @property
139    def max_retries(self) -> int:
140        return self._config.max_retries
strict: bool 
142    @property
143    def strict(self) -> bool:
144        return self._config.strict
def extract(self, text: str) -> vertai.output.structured.ExtractionResult: 
146    def extract(self, text: str) -> ExtractionResult:
147        """Extract structured data from text.
148
149        Uses the LLM for semantic extraction when one was provided; otherwise
150        falls back to local regex extraction (testing only).
151
152        Args:
153            text: Unstructured text to extract data from.
154
155        Returns:
156            ExtractionResult with data, success status, and retry count.
157
158        Raises:
159            SchemaValidationError: In strict mode when validation fails after
160                all retries.
161        """
162        # LLM mode: semantic extraction with schema validation + retry.
163        if self._llm is not None:
164            return self._extract_with_llm(text)
165
166        # Local mode: regex extraction.
167        result = self._extract_initial(text)
168
169        if result.success:
170            return result
171
172        # Retry with corrections.
173        for attempt in range(self.max_retries):
174            corrected = self._correct_extraction(text, result, attempt)
175            if corrected.success:
176                corrected.retries = attempt + 1
177                return corrected
178
179        # All retries exhausted.
180        if self.strict:
181            raise SchemaValidationError(
