182                f"Failed to extract valid data after {self.max_retries} retries. "
183                "Schema validation failed for one or more fields."
184            )
185
186        return ExtractionResult(
187            data=result.data,
188            success=False,
189            retries=self.max_retries,
190            error=result.error
191        )

Extract structured data from text.

Uses the LLM for semantic extraction when one was provided; otherwise falls back to local regex extraction (testing only).

Args: text: Unstructured text to extract data from.

Returns: ExtractionResult with data, success status, and retry count.

Raises: SchemaValidationError: In strict mode when validation fails after all retries.

class DocGen: 
 83class DocGen:
 84    """Document generator supporting multiple output formats and templates.
 85
 86    Examples:
 87        >>> from vertai import DocGen
 88        >>> doc = DocGen(template="report", format="markdown")
 89        >>> result = doc.generate({
 90        ...     "title": "Annual Report",
 91        ...     "author": "John Doe",
 92        ...     "summary": "Key findings from the year."
 93        ... })
 94    """
 95
 96    def __init__(
 97        self,
 98        template: str | TemplateType | Template = "report",
 99        format: str | OutputFormat = OutputFormat.MARKDOWN,
100        locale: str = "en_US",
101    ) -> None:
102        """Initialize the document generator.
103
104        Args:
105            template: Template name, TemplateType enum, or Template instance.
106            format: Output format (markdown, html, pdf).
107            locale: Locale for date and number formatting.
108        """
109        self._template = self._resolve_template(template)
110        self._format = OutputFormat(format) if isinstance(format, str) else format
111        self._locale = locale
112
113    def _resolve_template(self, template: str | TemplateType | Template) -> Template:
114        """Resolve template parameter to a Template instance."""
115        if isinstance(template, Template):
116            return template
117        if isinstance(template, TemplateType):
118            template_name = template.value
119        else:
120            template_name = template
121
122        if template_name not in BUILTIN_TEMPLATES:
123            raise ValueError(
124                f"Unknown template: {template_name}. "
125                f"Available templates: {list(BUILTIN_TEMPLATES.keys())}"
126            )
127        return deepcopy(BUILTIN_TEMPLATES[template_name])
128
129    @property
130    def template(self) -> Template:
131        """Get current template."""
132        return self._template
133
134    @property
135    def format(self) -> OutputFormat:
136        """Get current output format."""
137        return self._format
138
139    def generate(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> str | bytes:
140        """Generate a document from the provided data.
141
142        Args:
143            data: Dictionary containing document content. Keys should match
144                  template sections.
145
146        Returns:
147            Generated document. ``str`` for markdown/html formats,
148            ``bytes`` for the PDF format (when a real PDF backend is wired in).
149
150        Raises:
151            ValueError: If required template sections are missing from data.
152            NotImplementedError: For the PDF format until a real backend is wired.
153        """
154        content = self._build_content(data)
155
156        if self._format == OutputFormat.MARKDOWN:
157            return self._render_markdown(content)
158        elif self._format == OutputFormat.HTML:
159            return self._render_html(content)
160        elif self._format == OutputFormat.PDF:
161            return self._render_pdf(content)
162        else:
163            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported format: {self._format}")
164
165    def _build_content(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
166        """Build content dictionary with processed sections."""
167        content = {}
168
169        for section in self._template.sections:
170            if section in data:
171                content[section] = self._process_section(section, data[section])
172            elif section in ["date"] and section not in data:
173                content[section] = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
174
175        return content
176
177    def _process_section(self, section: str, value: Any) -> Any:
178        """Process a section value based on its type."""
179        if isinstance(value, list):
180            return [self._process_item(item) for item in value]
181        return value
182
183    def _process_item(self, item: Any) -> str:
184        """Convert an item to string representation."""
185        if isinstance(item, dict):
186            parts = [f"{k}: {v}" for k, v in item.items()]
187            return " | ".join(parts)
188        return str(item)
189
190    def _render_markdown(self, content: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
191        """Render content as Markdown."""
192        lines = []
193
194        title_section = self._template.sections[0] if self._template.sections else None
195        if title_section and title_section in content:
196            title = content[title_section]
197            if isinstance(title, str):
198                lines.append(self._template.title_format.format(title=title))
199                lines.append("")
200
201        if self._template.metadata.get("include_toc"):
202            lines.append("## Table of Contents")
203            lines.append("")
204            for section in self._template.sections[1:]:
205                if section in content:
206                    section_title = section.replace("_", " ").title()
207                    lines.append(f"- [{section_title}](#{section})")
208            lines.append("")
209
210        for section in self._template.sections:
211            if section == title_section or section not in content:
212                continue
213
214            section_title = section.replace("_", " ").title()
215            value = content[section]
216
217            if self._template.section_format:
218                lines.append(self._template.section_format.format(section=section_title))
219                lines.append("")
220
221            if isinstance(value, list):
222                for item in value:
223                    lines.append(self._template.item_format.format(item=item))
224            else:
225                lines.append(str(value))
226            lines.append("")
227
228        return "\n".join(lines).strip()
229
230    def _render_html(self, content: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
231        """Render content as HTML."""
232        html_parts = [
233            "<!DOCTYPE html>",
234            '<html lang="en">',
235            "<head>",
236            '<meta charset="UTF-8">',
237            '<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">',
238        ]
239
240        title_section = self._template.sections[0] if self._template.sections else None
241        if title_section and title_section in content:
242            title = html.escape(str(content[title_section]))
243            html_parts.append(f"<title>{title}</title>")
244
245        html_parts.extend([
246            "<style>",
247            "body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 2rem; }",
248            "h1 { border-bottom: 2px solid #333; padding-bottom: 0.5rem; }",
249            "h2 { color: #555; margin-top: 2rem; }",
250            "h3 { color: #666; }",
251            "ul { line-height: 1.8; }",
252            ".toc { background: #f5f5f5; padding: 1rem; border-radius: 4px; }",
253            ".toc ul { list-style: none; padding-left: 0; }",
254            ".toc a { color: #0066cc; text-decoration: none; }",
255            "</style>",
256            "</head>",
257            "<body>",
258        ])
259
260        if title_section and title_section in content:
261            title = html.escape(str(content[title_section]))
262            html_parts.append(f"<h1>{title}</h1>")
263
264        if self._template.metadata.get("include_toc"):
265            html_parts.append('<div class="toc">')
266            html_parts.append("<h2>Table of Contents</h2>")
267            html_parts.append("<ul>")
268            for section in self._template.sections[1:]:
269                if section in content:
270                    section_title = section.replace("_", " ").title()
271                    html_parts.append(f'<li><a href="#{section}">{html.escape(section_title)}</a></li>')
272            html_parts.append("</ul>")
273            html_parts.append("</div>")
274
275        for section in self._template.sections:
276            if section == title_section or section not in content:
277                continue
278
279            section_title = section.replace("_", " ").title()
280            value = content[section]
281
282            html_parts.append(f'<section id="{section}">')
283            html_parts.append(f"<h2>{html.escape(section_title)}</h2>")
284
285            if isinstance(value, list):
286                html_parts.append("<ul>")
287                for item in value:
288                    html_parts.append(f"<li>{html.escape(str(item))}</li>")
289                html_parts.append("</ul>")
290            else:
291                html_parts.append(f"<p>{html.escape(str(value))}</p>")
292
293            html_parts.append("</section>")
294
295        html_parts.extend([
296            "</body>",
297            "</html>",
298        ])
299
300        return "\n".join(html_parts)
301
302    def _render_pdf(self, content: dict[str, Any]) -> bytes:
303        """Render content as PDF.
304
305        Raises:
306            NotImplementedError: PDF generation requires external dependencies.
307                Use HTML output and convert with weasyprint/pdfkit/xhtml2pdf.
308
309        Note:
310            For PDF output, use the following workflow:
311            1. Generate HTML: doc.set_format("html").generate(data)
312            2. Convert with weasyprint: weasyprint.HTML(string=html).write_pdf()
313        """
314        raise NotImplementedError(
315            "PDF generation requires external dependencies.\n"
316            "Use HTML output and convert with:\n"
317            "  - weasyprint: pip install weasyprint\n"
318            "  - pdfkit: pip install pdfkit (requires wkhtmltopdf)\n"
319            "  - xhtml2pdf: pip install xhtml2pdf\n\n"
320            "Example:\n"
321            "  from weasyprint import HTML\n"
322            "  html = doc.set_format('html').generate(data)\n"
323            "  pdf_bytes = HTML(string=html).write_pdf()"
324        )
325
326    def add_section(self, name: str, after: str | None = None) -> "DocGen":
327        """Add a custom section to the template.
328
329        Args:
330            name: Section name.
331            after: Insert after this section. If None, appends to end.
332
333        Returns:
334            Self for method chaining.
335        """
336        if after is None:
337            self._template.sections.append(name)
338        else:
339            try:
340                idx = self._template.sections.index(after)
341                self._template.sections.insert(idx + 1, name)
342            except ValueError:
343                self._template.sections.append(name)
344        return self
345
346    def remove_section(self, name: str) -> "DocGen":
347        """Remove a section from the template.
348
349        Args:
350            name: Section name to remove.
351
352        Returns:
353            Self for method chaining.
354        """
355        if name in self._template.sections:
356            self._template.sections.remove(name)
357        return self
358
359    def set_format(self, format: str | OutputFormat) -> "DocGen":
360        """Set the output format.
361
362        Args:
363            format: Output format (markdown, html, pdf).
364
365        Returns:
366            Self for method chaining.
367        """
368        self._format = OutputFormat(format) if isinstance(format, str) else format
369        return self
370
371    def to_markdown(self) -> "DocGen":
372        """Set output format to Markdown."""
373        self._format = OutputFormat.MARKDOWN
374        return self
375
376    def to_html(self) -> "DocGen":
377        """Set output format to HTML."""
378        self._format = OutputFormat.HTML
379        return self
380
381    def to_pdf(self) -> "DocGen":
382        """Set output format to PDF."""
383        self._format = OutputFormat.PDF
384        return self
385
386    def save(self, data: dict[str, Any], filepath: str) -> None:
387        """Generate and save document to a file.
388
389        Writes text output in UTF-8 text mode (markdown/html) and binary
390        output in bytes mode (pdf), matching the type returned by
391        :meth:`generate`.
392
393        Args:
394            data: Document content data.
395            filepath: Output file path.
396        """
397        content = self.generate(data)
398
399        if isinstance(content, bytes):
400            with open(filepath, "wb") as f:
401                f.write(content)
402        else:
403            with open(filepath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
404                f.write(content)
405
406    @classmethod
407    def list_templates(cls) -> list[str]:
408        """List available built-in templates."""
409        return list(BUILTIN_TEMPLATES.keys())
410
411    @classmethod
412    def list_formats(cls) -> list[str]:
413        """List available output formats."""
414        return [f.value for f in OutputFormat]

Document generator supporting multiple output formats and templates.

Examples:

from vertai import DocGen doc = DocGen(template="report", format="markdown") result = doc.generate({ ... "title": "Annual Report", ... "author": "John Doe", ... "summary": "Key findings from the year." ... })

DocGen( template: str | vertai.output.docgen.TemplateType | vertai.output.docgen.Template = 'report', format: str | vertai.output.docgen.OutputFormat = <OutputFormat.MARKDOWN: 'markdown'>, locale: str = 'en_US') 
 96    def __init__(
 97        self,
 98        template: str | TemplateType | Template = "report",
 99        format: str | OutputFormat = OutputFormat.MARKDOWN,
100        locale: str = "en_US",
101    ) -> None:
102        """Initialize the document generator.
103
104        Args:
105            template: Template name, TemplateType enum, or Template instance.
106            format: Output format (markdown, html, pdf).
107            locale: Locale for date and number formatting.
108        """
109        self._template = self._resolve_template(template)
110        self._format = OutputFormat(format) if isinstance(format, str) else format
111        self._locale = locale

Initialize the document generator.

Args: template: Template name, TemplateType enum, or Template instance. format: Output format (markdown, html, pdf). locale: Locale for date and number formatting.

template: vertai.output.docgen.Template 
129    @property
130    def template(self) -> Template:
131        """Get current template."""
132        return self._template

Get current template.

format: vertai.output.docgen.OutputFormat 
134    @property
135    def format(self) -> OutputFormat:
136        """Get current output format."""
137        return self._format

Get current output format.

def generate(self, data: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> str | bytes: 
139    def generate(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> str | bytes:
140        """Generate a document from the provided data.
141
142        Args:
143            data: Dictionary containing document content. Keys should match
144                  template sections.
145
146        Returns:
147            Generated document. ``str`` for markdown/html formats,
148            ``bytes`` for the PDF format (when a real PDF backend is wired in).
149
150        Raises:
151            ValueError: If required template sections are missing from data.
152            NotImplementedError: For the PDF format until a real backend is wired.
153        """
154        content = self._build_content(data)
155
156        if self._format == OutputFormat.MARKDOWN:
157            return self._render_markdown(content)
158        elif self._format == OutputFormat.HTML:
159            return self._render_html(content)
160        elif self._format == OutputFormat.PDF:
161            return self._render_pdf(content)
162        else:
163            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported format: {self._format}")

Generate a document from the provided data.

Args: data: Dictionary containing document content. Keys should match template sections.

Returns: Generated document. str for markdown/html formats, bytes for the PDF format (when a real PDF backend is wired in).

Raises: ValueError: If required template sections are missing from data. NotImplementedError: For the PDF format until a real backend is wired.

def add_section(self, name: str, after: str | None = None) -> DocGen: 
326    def add_section(self, name: str, after: str | None = None) -> "DocGen":
327        """Add a custom section to the template.
328
329        Args:
330            name: Section name.
331            after: Insert after this section. If None, appends to end.
332
333        Returns:
334            Self for method chaining.
335        """
336        if after is None:
337            self._template.sections.append(name)
338        else:
339            try:
340                idx = self._template.sections.index(after)
341                self._template.sections.insert(idx + 1, name)
342            except ValueError:
343                self._template.sections.append(name)
344        return self

Add a custom section to the template.

Args: name: Section name. after: Insert after this section. If None, appends to end.

Returns: Self for method chaining.

def remove_section(self, name: str) -> DocGen: 
346    def remove_section(self, name: str) -> "DocGen":
347        """Remove a section from the template.
348
349        Args:
350            name: Section name to remove.
351
352        Returns:
353            Self for method chaining.
354        """
355        if name in self._template.sections:
356            self._template.sections.remove(name)
357        return self

Remove a section from the template.

Args: name: Section name to remove.

Returns: Self for method chaining.

def set_format( self, format: str | vertai.output.docgen.OutputFormat) -> DocGen: 
359    def set_format(self, format: str | OutputFormat) -> "DocGen":
360        """Set the output format.
361
362        Args:
363            format: Output format (markdown, html, pdf).
364
365        Returns:
366            Self for method chaining.
367        """
368        self._format = OutputFormat(format) if isinstance(format, str) else format
369        return self

Set the output format.

Args: format: Output format (markdown, html, pdf).

Returns: Self for method chaining.

def to_markdown(self) -> DocGen: 
371    def to_markdown(self) -> "DocGen":
372        """Set output format to Markdown."""
373        self._format = OutputFormat.MARKDOWN
374        return self

Set output format to Markdown.

def to_html(self) -> DocGen: 
376    def to_html(self) -> "DocGen":
377        """Set output format to HTML."""
378        self._format = OutputFormat.HTML
379        return self

Set output format to HTML.

def to_pdf(self) -> DocGen: 
381    def to_pdf(self) -> "DocGen":
382        """Set output format to PDF."""
383        self._format = OutputFormat.PDF
384        return self

Set output format to PDF.

def save(self, data: dict[str, typing.Any], filepath: str) -> None: 
386    def save(self, data: dict[str, Any], filepath: str) -> None:
387        """Generate and save document to a file.
388
389        Writes text output in UTF-8 text mode (markdown/html) and binary
390        output in bytes mode (pdf), matching the type returned by
391        :meth:`generate`.
392
393        Args:
394            data: Document content data.
395            filepath: Output file path.
396        """
397        content = self.generate(data)
398
399        if isinstance(content, bytes):
400            with open(filepath, "wb") as f:
401                f.write(content)
402        else:
403            with open(filepath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
404                f.write(content)

Generate and save document to a file.

Writes text output in UTF-8 text mode (markdown/html) and binary output in bytes mode (pdf), matching the type returned by generate().

Args: data: Document content data. filepath: Output file path.

@classmethod
def list_templates(cls) -> list[str]: 
406    @classmethod
407    def list_templates(cls) -> list[str]:
408        """List available built-in templates."""
409        return list(BUILTIN_TEMPLATES.keys())

List available built-in templates.

@classmethod
def list_formats(cls) -> list[str]: 
411    @classmethod
412    def list_formats(cls) -> list[str]:
413        """List available output formats."""
414        return [f.value for f in OutputFormat]

List available output formats.

class Evaluation(abc.ABC): 
 85class Evaluation(ABC):
 86    """Generalized LLM-as-judge evaluation abstraction.
 87
 88    Subclasses implement :meth:`evaluate`. The LLM is accessed through the
 89    :class:`LLMProvider` abstraction: pass ``provider=`` directly, or pass
 90    ``llm=`` an :class:`~vertai.core.llm.LLMEngine` (its ``.provider`` is
 91    extracted). With neither, a provider is built from
 92    :func:`~vertai.core.provider.create_provider` (default Ollama config).
 93    """
 94
 95    def __init__(
 96        self,
 97        *,
 98        config: ReviewerConfig | None = None,
 99        provider: LLMProvider | None = None,
100        llm: LLMEngine | None = None,
101        llm_config: LLMConfig | None = None,
102    ) -> None:
103        self.config = config or ReviewerConfig()
104        self._provider: LLMProvider | None = provider or (
105            llm.provider if isinstance(llm, LLMEngine) else None
106        )
107        self._llm_engine: LLMEngine | None = llm
108        self._llm_config: LLMConfig | None = llm_config
109
110    @abstractmethod
111    def evaluate(
112        self, submission: str, reference: Optional[str] = None
113    ) -> ReviewResult:
114        """Evaluate ``submission`` (optionally against ``reference``)."""
115
116    def get_provider(self) -> LLMProvider:
117        """Return the LLM provider (injected, or built from config)."""
118        if self._provider is not None:
119            return self._provider
120        if self._llm_engine is not None:
121            return self._llm_engine.provider
122        model = self.config.model
123        config = LLMConfig(model=model) if model else (self._llm_config or LLMConfig())
124        return create_provider(config)
125
126    @staticmethod
127    def _sanitize_input(text: str) -> str:
128        """Redact prompt-injection patterns and strip control characters."""
129        cleaned = _INJECTION_RE.sub(_REDACTION, text)
130        cleaned = re.sub(r"[\x00-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x7f]", "", cleaned)
131        return cleaned.strip()

Generalized LLM-as-judge evaluation abstraction.

Subclasses implement evaluate(). The LLM is accessed through the LLMProvider abstraction: pass provider= directly, or pass llm= an ~vertai.core.llm.LLMEngine (its .provider is extracted). With neither, a provider is built from ~vertai.core.provider.create_provider() (default Ollama config).

config
@abstractmethod
def evaluate( self, submission: str, reference: Optional[str] = None) -> ReviewResult: 
110    @abstractmethod
111    def evaluate(
112        self, submission: str, reference: Optional[str] = None
113    ) -> ReviewResult:
114        """Evaluate ``submission`` (optionally against ``reference``)."""

Evaluate submission (optionally against reference).

def get_provider(self) -> LLMProvider: 
116    def get_provider(self) -> LLMProvider:
117        """Return the LLM provider (injected, or built from config)."""
118        if self._provider is not None:
119            return self._provider
120        if self._llm_engine is not None:
121            return self._llm_engine.provider
122        model = self.config.model
123        config = LLMConfig(model=model) if model else (self._llm_config or LLMConfig())
124        return create_provider(config)

Return the LLM provider (injected, or built from config).

class Reviewer(vertai.Evaluation): 
134class Reviewer(Evaluation):
135    """Criteria-based LLM-as-judge reviewer.
136
137    Args:
138        criteria: review criteria; must be non-empty.
139        config: optional :class:`ReviewerConfig`.
140        provider: optional :class:`LLMProvider` (preferred injection point).
141        llm: optional :class:`LLMEngine` (its ``.provider`` is used).
142        llm_config: optional :class:`LLMConfig` for the default provider.
143
144    Raises:
145        ValueError: if ``criteria`` is empty.
146
147    Example:
148        reviewer = Reviewer(criteria=["accuracy", "completeness"], provider=prov)
149        result = reviewer.evaluate(submission)
150    """
151
152    def __init__(
153        self,
154        criteria: list[str],
155        config: ReviewerConfig | None = None,
156        provider: LLMProvider | None = None,
157        llm: LLMEngine | None = None,
158        llm_config: LLMConfig | None = None,
159    ) -> None:
160        super().__init__(
161            config=config, provider=provider, llm=llm, llm_config=llm_config
162        )
163        if not criteria:
164            raise ValueError("criteria must not be empty")
165        self.criteria = list(criteria)
166
167    @property
168    def max_score(self) -> int:
169        return self.config.max_score
170
171    @property
172    def model(self) -> Optional[str]:
173        return self.config.model
174
175    @property
176    def template(self) -> Optional[str]:
177        return self.config.template
178
179    def evaluate(
180        self, submission: str, reference: Optional[str] = None
181    ) -> ReviewResult:
182        """Evaluate ``submission`` (optionally against ``reference``).
183
184        Raises ``ValueError`` if the submission is empty or exceeds the length
185        limit.
186        """
187        if not submission or not submission.strip():
188            raise ValueError("submission must not be empty")
189
190        validated_submission = self._validate_input(
191            submission, self.config.max_submission_length, "submission"
192        )
193        validated_reference: Optional[str] = None
194        if reference:
195            validated_reference = self._validate_input(
196                reference, self.config.max_reference_length, "reference"
197            )
198
199        provider = self.get_provider()
200        prompt = self._build_prompt(validated_submission, validated_reference)
201        result = provider.generate([ChatMessage(role="user", content=prompt)])
202        return self._parse_response(result.content)
203
204    def _validate_input(
205        self, text: str, max_length: int, field_name: str
206    ) -> str:
207        if len(text) > max_length:
208            raise ValueError(
209                f"{field_name} exceeds max length limit ({max_length} characters)"
210            )
211        return self._sanitize_input(text)
212
213    def _build_prompt(self, submission: str, reference: Optional[str]) -> str:
214        criteria_text = ", ".join(self.criteria)
215        prompt_parts = [
216            f"Review the submission against these criteria: {criteria_text}",
217            "",
218            "Criteria detail:",
219        ]
220        per_criterion = self.max_score // max(len(self.criteria), 1)
221        for i, criterion in enumerate(self.criteria, 1):
222            prompt_parts.append(
223                f"{i}. {criterion} (max {per_criterion} points)"
224            )
225        prompt_parts.extend(["", "Submission:", submission])
226        if reference:
227            prompt_parts.extend(["", "Reference:", reference])
228        prompt_parts.extend(
229            [
230                "",
231                "Output the review result as JSON:",
232                "{",
233                '  "score": <int total>,',
234                '  "criteria_scores": {"<criterion>": <int>},',
235                '  "comments": "<overall comment>",',
236                '  "suggestions": ["<suggestion>", ...]',
237                "}",
238            ]
239        )
240        return "\n".join(prompt_parts)
241
242    def _parse_response(self, response: str) -> ReviewResult:
243        """Parse the LLM JSON response into a :class:`ReviewResult`."""
244        json_match = re.search(r"\{[\s\S]*\}", response)
245        if not json_match:
246            return ReviewResult(
247                score=0,
248                criteria_scores={c: 0 for c in self.criteria},
249                comments="unable to parse review result",
250                suggestions=["please resubmit the review"],
251                details={"raw_response": response},
252            )
253        try:
254            data = json.loads(json_match.group())
255        except json.JSONDecodeError:
256            return ReviewResult(
257                score=0,
258                criteria_scores={c: 0 for c in self.criteria},
259                comments="review result format error",
260                suggestions=["please resubmit the review"],
261                details={"raw_response": response},
262            )
263
264        criteria_scores: dict[str, int] = {
265            str(k): int(v) for k, v in dict(data.get("criteria_scores", {})).items()
266        }
267        for criterion in self.criteria:
268            if criterion not in criteria_scores:
269                criteria_scores[criterion] = 0
270        raw_score = data.get("score", 0)
271        try:
272            score_int = int(raw_score)
273        except (TypeError, ValueError):
274            score_int = 0
275        return ReviewResult(
276            score=min(max(score_int, 0), self.max_score),
277            criteria_scores=criteria_scores,
278            comments=str(data.get("comments", "")),
279            suggestions=list(data.get("suggestions", [])),
280            details={"raw_response": response},
281        )

Criteria-based LLM-as-judge reviewer.

Args: criteria: review criteria; must be non-empty. config: optional ReviewerConfig. provider: optional LLMProvider (preferred injection point). llm: optional LLMEngine (its .provider is used). llm_config: optional LLMConfig for the default provider.

Raises: ValueError: if criteria is empty.

Example: reviewer = Reviewer(criteria=["accuracy", "completeness"], provider=prov) result = reviewer.evaluate(submission)

Reviewer( criteria: list[str], config: ReviewerConfig | None = None, provider: LLMProvider | None = None, llm: LLMEngine | None = None, llm_config: LLMConfig | None = None) 
152    def __init__(
153        self,
154        criteria: list[str],
155        config: ReviewerConfig | None = None,
156        provider: LLMProvider | None = None,
157        llm: LLMEngine | None = None,
158        llm_config: LLMConfig | None = None,
159    ) -> None:
160        super().__init__(
161            config=config, provider=provider, llm=llm, llm_config=llm_config
162        )
163        if not criteria:
164            raise ValueError("criteria must not be empty")
165        self.criteria = list(criteria)
criteria
max_score: int 
167    @property
168    def max_score(self) -> int:
169        return self.config.max_score
model: Optional[str] 
171    @property
172    def model(self) -> Optional[str]:
173        return self.config.model
template: Optional[str] 
175    @property
176    def template(self) -> Optional[str]:
177        return self.config.template
def evaluate( self, submission: str, reference: Optional[str] = None) -> ReviewResult: 
179    def evaluate(
180        self, submission: str, reference: Optional[str] = None
181    ) -> ReviewResult:
182        """Evaluate ``submission`` (optionally against ``reference``).
183
184        Raises ``ValueError`` if the submission is empty or exceeds the length
185        limit.
186        """
187        if not submission or not submission.strip():
188            raise ValueError("submission must not be empty")
189
190        validated_submission = self._validate_input(
191            submission, self.config.max_submission_length, "submission"
192        )
193        validated_reference: Optional[str] = None
194        if reference:
195            validated_reference = self._validate_input(
196                reference, self.config.max_reference_length, "reference"
197            )
198
199        provider = self.get_provider()
200        prompt = self._build_prompt(validated_submission, validated_reference)
201        result = provider.generate([ChatMessage(role="user", content=prompt)])
202        return self._parse_response(result.content)

Evaluate submission (optionally against reference).

Raises ValueError if the submission is empty or exceeds the length limit.

class ReviewerConfig(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
52class ReviewerConfig(BaseModel):
53    """Reviewer configuration."""
54
55    max_score: int = Field(default=100, ge=1, le=1000, description="max score")
56    model: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="model name")
57    template: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="review template")
58    max_submission_length: int = Field(default=10000, ge=1, description="max submission length")
59    max_reference_length: int = Field(default=5000, ge=1, description="max reference length")
60
61    model_config = {"extra": "forbid"}

Reviewer configuration.

max_score: int = 100

max score

model: Optional[str] = None

model name

template: Optional[str] = None

review template

max_submission_length: int = 10000

max submission length

max_reference_length: int = 5000

max reference length

@dataclass
class ReviewResult: 
41@dataclass
42class ReviewResult:
43    """A single evaluation result."""
44
45    score: int
46    criteria_scores: dict[str, int]
47    comments: str
48    suggestions: list[str]
49    details: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

A single evaluation result.

ReviewResult( score: int, criteria_scores: dict[str, int], comments: str, suggestions: list[str], details: dict[str, typing.Any] = <factory>)
score: int
criteria_scores: dict[str, int]
comments: str
suggestions: list[str]
details: dict[str, typing.Any]
class KnowledgeQA: 
283class KnowledgeQA:
284    """Knowledge-base Q&A (RAG).
285
286    Retrieval goes through a :class:`Retriever` abstraction (default
287    :class:`VectorRetriever` over a :class:`VectorEngine`); answer generation
288    goes through an :class:`LLMProvider`. Both are injectable so the scenario
289    depends on core abstractions, not concrete adapters.
290
291    Example:
292        from vertai.core.embedding import LocalSentenceTransformerProvider
293        from vertai.core.provider import LLMConfig, create_provider
294
295        qa = KnowledgeQA(
296            docs_path="./docs",
297            embedding_provider=LocalSentenceTransformerProvider(),
298            provider=create_provider(LLMConfig()),
299        )
300        result = qa.ask("what is the reimbursement process?")
301        print(result.answer)
302    """
303
304    def __init__(
305        self,
306        docs_path: str | Path | None = None,
307        config: KnowledgeQAConfig | None = None,
308        vector_config: VectorConfig | None = None,
309        llm: LLMEngine | None = None,
310        provider: LLMProvider | None = None,
311        llm_config: LLMConfig | None = None,
312        embedding_provider: EmbeddingProvider | None = None,
313        embedding_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], list[float]]] = None,
314        text_splitter: TextSplitter | None = None,
315        retriever: Retriever | None = None,
316    ) -> None:
317        self.config = config or KnowledgeQAConfig()
318        self.vector_config = vector_config or VectorConfig(
319            chunk_size=self.config.chunk_size,
320            chunk_overlap=self.config.chunk_overlap,
321            top_k=self.config.top_k,
322        )
323        self._vector_engine = VectorEngine(
324            config=self.vector_config,
325            store_type="memory",
326            embedding_provider=embedding_provider,
327            embedding_fn=embedding_fn,
328        )
329        self._provider: LLMProvider | None = provider or (
330            llm.provider if isinstance(llm, LLMEngine) else None
331        )
332        self._llm_engine: LLMEngine | None = llm
333        self._llm_config = llm_config
334        self._retriever = retriever
335        self._loader = DocumentLoader(self.config, text_splitter)
336        self._indexed = False
337        self._documents: list[Document] = []
338
339        if docs_path:
340            self.index(docs_path)
341
342    # -- indexing ---------------------------------------------------------
343
344    def index(self, docs_path: str | Path) -> int:
345        """Index a document directory. Returns the number of chunks indexed."""
346        docs_path = Path(docs_path)
347        documents = self._loader.load_directory(docs_path)
348        self._documents = documents
349        if documents:
350            self._vector_engine.index_documents(documents)
351            self._indexed = True
352            logger.info("indexed %d document chunks", len(documents))
353        return len(documents)
354
355    def add_documents(self, documents: list[Document]) -> int:
356        """Add documents directly. Returns the number added."""
357        self._documents.extend(documents)
358        self._vector_engine.index_documents(documents)
359        self._indexed = True
360        return len(documents)
361
362    # -- retrieval --------------------------------------------------------
363
364    def _get_retriever(self) -> Retriever:
365        if self._retriever is not None:
366            return self._retriever
367        embedding = self._vector_engine.get_embedding()
368        return VectorRetriever(embedding, self._vector_engine.store)
369
370    # -- generation -------------------------------------------------------
371
372    def _get_provider(self) -> LLMProvider:
373        """Return the LLM provider (fixes C1: no ``LLMEngine(model=...)``)."""
374        if self._provider is not None:
375            return self._provider
376        if self._llm_engine is not None:
377            return self._llm_engine.provider
378        config = self._llm_config or LLMConfig()
379        return create_provider(config)
380
381    def ask(self, question: str) -> AnswerResult:
382        """Ask a question against the indexed knowledge base.
383
384        Raises ``ValueError`` if the question is empty or contains disallowed
385        content. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (from the vector engine) if no
386        embedding provider is configured (C2: no silent random fallback).
387        """
388        if not question or not question.strip():
389            raise ValueError("question must not be empty")
390        question = _sanitize_input(question)
391
392        if not self._indexed:
393            return AnswerResult(
394                question=question,
395                answer="knowledge base has no indexed documents; add documents first.",
396                confidence=0.0,
397            )
398
399        search_results = self._get_retriever().retrieve(
400            question, top_k=self.config.top_k
401        )
402        if not search_results:
403            return AnswerResult(
404                question=question,
405                answer="sorry, no relevant content was found.",
406                confidence=0.0,
407            )
408
409        relevant = [
410            r for r in search_results if r.score >= self.config.min_confidence
411        ]
412        if not relevant:
413            return AnswerResult(
414                question=question,
415                answer="sorry, no sufficiently relevant content was found.",
416                confidence=0.0,
417            )
418
419        context = self._build_context(relevant)
420        answer = self._generate_answer(question, context)
421        confidence = self._calculate_confidence(relevant)
422        sources = self._build_sources(relevant)
423        return AnswerResult(
424            question=question,
425            answer=answer,
426            sources=sources,
427            confidence=confidence,
428            metadata={
429                "context_length": len(context),
430                "num_sources": len(sources),
431            },
432        )
433
434    def _build_context(self, results: list[SearchResult]) -> str:
435        context_parts: list[str] = []
436        total = 0
437        for result in results:
438            content = result.document.content
439            if total + len(content) <= self.config.max_context_length:
440                context_parts.append(content)
441                total += len(content)
442            else:
443                remaining = self.config.max_context_length - total
444                if remaining > 100:
445                    context_parts.append(content[:remaining] + "...")
446                break
447        return "\n\n---\n\n".join(context_parts)
448
449    def _generate_answer(self, question: str, context: str) -> str:
450        """Generate an answer from the question + sanitized context.
451
452        Uses the LLMProvider abstraction (``generate([ChatMessage])``) and reads
453        ``result.content`` (fixes the GenerateResult/str mismatch that crashed
454        ``ask()``).
455        """
456        provider = self._get_provider()
457        safe_context = _sanitize_context(context)
458        prompt = (
459            "Answer the question based on the reference materials below. "
460            "If the materials do not contain the answer, say so explicitly.\n\n"
461            "---reference start---\n"
462            f"{safe_context}\n"
463            "---reference end---\n\n"
464            "---question start---\n"
465            f"{question}\n"
466            "---question end---\n\n"
467            "Give an accurate, concise answer based only on the reference "
468            "materials, and cite the source:"
469        )
470        result = provider.generate([ChatMessage(role="user", content=prompt)])
471        return self._parse_answer(result.content)
472
473    def _parse_answer(self, response: str) -> str:
474        """Parse an answer; unwrap a ``{"answer": ...}`` JSON envelope if present."""
475        if response.startswith("{") and response.endswith("}"):
476            try:
477                data = json.loads(response)
478                if isinstance(data, dict) and "answer" in data:
479                    return str(data["answer"])
480            except json.JSONDecodeError:
481                pass
482        return response.strip()
483
484    def _calculate_confidence(self, results: list[SearchResult]) -> float:
485        if not results:
486            return 0.0
487        scores = [r.score for r in results[:3]]
488        avg = sum(scores) / len(scores)
489        source_factor = min(len(results) / 3.0, 1.0)
490        return round(avg * source_factor, 2)
491
492    def _build_sources(self, results: list[SearchResult]) -> list[SourceReference]:
493        sources: list[SourceReference] = []
494        seen: set[str] = set()
495        for result in results:
496            doc = result.document
497            source_key = f"{doc.metadata.get('source', 'unknown')}:{doc.doc_id}"
498            if source_key in seen:
499                continue
500            seen.add(source_key)
501            content = doc.content
502            truncated = content[:200] + "..." if len(content) > 200 else content
503            sources.append(
504                SourceReference(
505                    content=truncated,
506                    source=str(doc.metadata.get("source", "unknown")),
507                    page=doc.metadata.get("page") if isinstance(doc.metadata.get("page"), int) else None,
508                    paragraph=doc.metadata.get("paragraph") if isinstance(doc.metadata.get("paragraph"), int) else None,
509                    relevance_score=round(result.score, 3),
510                )
511            )
512        return sources
513
514    # -- maintenance ------------------------------------------------------
515
516    def count_documents(self) -> int:
517        """Number of indexed live documents."""
518        return self._vector_engine.count()
519
520    def clear(self) -> None:
521        """Clear the knowledge base."""
522        if self._documents:
523            doc_ids = [doc.doc_id for doc in self._documents]
524            self._vector_engine.delete_documents(doc_ids)
525        self._documents = []
526        self._indexed = False

Knowledge-base Q&A (RAG).

Retrieval goes through a Retriever abstraction (default VectorRetriever over a VectorEngine); answer generation goes through an LLMProvider. Both are injectable so the scenario depends on core abstractions, not concrete adapters.

Example: from vertai.core.embedding import LocalSentenceTransformerProvider from vertai.core.provider import LLMConfig, create_provider

qa = KnowledgeQA(
    docs_path="./docs",
    embedding_provider=LocalSentenceTransformerProvider(),
    provider=create_provider(LLMConfig()),
)
result = qa.ask("what is the reimbursement process?")
print(result.answer)
KnowledgeQA( docs_path: str | pathlib._local.Path | None = None, config: KnowledgeQAConfig | None = None, vector_config: VectorConfig | None = None, llm: LLMEngine | None = None, provider: LLMProvider | None = None, llm_config: LLMConfig | None = None, embedding_provider: EmbeddingProvider | None = None, embedding_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], list[float]]] = None, text_splitter: TextSplitter | None = None, retriever: Retriever | None = None) 
304    def __init__(
305        self,
306        docs_path: str | Path | None = None,
307        config: KnowledgeQAConfig | None = None,
308        vector_config: VectorConfig | None = None,
309        llm: LLMEngine | None = None,
310        provider: LLMProvider | None = None,
311        llm_config: LLMConfig | None = None,
312        embedding_provider: EmbeddingProvider | None = None,
313        embedding_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], list[float]]] = None,
314        text_splitter: TextSplitter | None = None,
315        retriever: Retriever | None = None,
316    ) -> None:
317        self.config = config or KnowledgeQAConfig()
318        self.vector_config = vector_config or VectorConfig(
319            chunk_size=self.config.chunk_size,
320            chunk_overlap=self.config.chunk_overlap,
321            top_k=self.config.top_k,
322        )
323        self._vector_engine = VectorEngine(
324            config=self.vector_config,
325            store_type="memory",
326            embedding_provider=embedding_provider,
327            embedding_fn=embedding_fn,
328        )
329        self._provider: LLMProvider | None = provider or (
330            llm.provider if isinstance(llm, LLMEngine) else None
331        )
332        self._llm_engine: LLMEngine | None = llm
333        self._llm_config = llm_config
334        self._retriever = retriever
335        self._loader = DocumentLoader(self.config, text_splitter)
336        self._indexed = False
337        self._documents: list[Document] = []
338
339        if docs_path:
340            self.index(docs_path)
config
vector_config
def index(self, docs_path: str | pathlib._local.Path) -> int: 
344    def index(self, docs_path: str | Path) -> int:
345        """Index a document directory. Returns the number of chunks indexed."""
346        docs_path = Path(docs_path)
347        documents = self._loader.load_directory(docs_path)
348        self._documents = documents
349        if documents:
350            self._vector_engine.index_documents(documents)
351            self._indexed = True
352            logger.info("indexed %d document chunks", len(documents))
353        return len(documents)

Index a document directory. Returns the number of chunks indexed.

def add_documents(self, documents: list[Document]) -> int: 
355    def add_documents(self, documents: list[Document]) -> int:
356        """Add documents directly. Returns the number added."""
357        self._documents.extend(documents)
358        self._vector_engine.index_documents(documents)
359        self._indexed = True
360        return len(documents)

Add documents directly. Returns the number added.

def ask(self, question: str) -> AnswerResult: 
381    def ask(self, question: str) -> AnswerResult:
382        """Ask a question against the indexed knowledge base.
383
384        Raises ``ValueError`` if the question is empty or contains disallowed
385        content. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (from the vector engine) if no
386        embedding provider is configured (C2: no silent random fallback).
387        """
388        if not question or not question.strip():
389            raise ValueError("question must not be empty")
390        question = _sanitize_input(question)
391
392        if not self._indexed:
393            return AnswerResult(
394                question=question,
395                answer="knowledge base has no indexed documents; add documents first.",
396                confidence=0.0,
397            )
398
399        search_results = self._get_retriever().retrieve(
400            question, top_k=self.config.top_k
401        )
402        if not search_results:
403            return AnswerResult(
404                question=question,
405                answer="sorry, no relevant content was found.",
406                confidence=0.0,
407            )
408
409        relevant = [
410            r for r in search_results if r.score >= self.config.min_confidence
411        ]
412        if not relevant:
413            return AnswerResult(
414                question=question,
415                answer="sorry, no sufficiently relevant content was found.",
416                confidence=0.0,
417            )
418
419        context = self._build_context(relevant)
420        answer = self._generate_answer(question, context)
421        confidence = self._calculate_confidence(relevant)
422        sources = self._build_sources(relevant)
423        return AnswerResult(
424            question=question,
425            answer=answer,
426            sources=sources,
427            confidence=confidence,
428            metadata={
429                "context_length": len(context),
430                "num_sources": len(sources),
431            },
432        )

Ask a question against the indexed knowledge base.

Raises ValueError if the question is empty or contains disallowed content. Raises RuntimeError (from the vector engine) if no embedding provider is configured (C2: no silent random fallback).

def count_documents(self) -> int: 
516    def count_documents(self) -> int:
517        """Number of indexed live documents."""
518        return self._vector_engine.count()

Number of indexed live documents.

def clear(self) -> None: 
520    def clear(self) -> None:
521        """Clear the knowledge base."""
522        if self._documents:
523            doc_ids = [doc.doc_id for doc in self._documents]
524            self._vector_engine.delete_documents(doc_ids)
525        self._documents = []
526        self._indexed = False

Clear the knowledge base.

@dataclass
class KnowledgeQAConfig: 
161@dataclass
162class KnowledgeQAConfig:
163    """Knowledge-base Q&A configuration.
164
165    Environment overrides:
166        VERTAI_CHUNK_SIZE, VERTAI_CHUNK_OVERLAP, VERTAI_TOP_K,
167        VERTAI_MIN_CONFIDENCE, VERTAI_MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH,
168        VERTAI_SUPPORTED_FORMATS (comma-separated).
169    """
170
171    chunk_size: int = field(default_factory=lambda: _get_env_int("VERTAI_CHUNK_SIZE", 512))
172    chunk_overlap: int = field(default_factory=lambda: _get_env_int("VERTAI_CHUNK_OVERLAP", 50))
173    top_k: int = field(default_factory=lambda: _get_env_int("VERTAI_TOP_K", 5))
174    min_confidence: float = field(default_factory=lambda: _get_env_float("VERTAI_MIN_CONFIDENCE", 0.3))
175    max_context_length: int = field(default_factory=lambda: _get_env_int("VERTAI_MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH", 4000))
176    supported_formats: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: _get_env_list("VERTAI_SUPPORTED_FORMATS", ["txt", "md", "json"]))

Knowledge-base Q&A configuration.

Environment overrides: VERTAI_CHUNK_SIZE, VERTAI_CHUNK_OVERLAP, VERTAI_TOP_K, VERTAI_MIN_CONFIDENCE, VERTAI_MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH, VERTAI_SUPPORTED_FORMATS (comma-separated).

KnowledgeQAConfig( chunk_size: int = <factory>, chunk_overlap: int = <factory>, top_k: int = <factory>, min_confidence: float = <factory>, max_context_length: int = <factory>, supported_formats: list[str] = <factory>)
chunk_size: int
chunk_overlap: int
top_k: int
min_confidence: float
max_context_length: int
supported_formats: list[str]

class AnswerResult:

69    sources: list[SourceReference] = field(default_factory=list)
70    confidence: float = 0.0
71    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

Q&A result: the question, the generated answer, sources, confidence.

answer: str

@dataclass

59    paragraph: int | None = None

A cited source for an answer.

SourceReference( content: str, source: str, page: int | None = None, paragraph: int | None = None, relevance_score: float = 0.0)
content: str
source: str
page: int | None = None
