relevance_score: float = 0.0
class Workflow: 
318class Workflow:
319    """Workflow orchestration engine.
320
321    Supports sequential steps, conditional branches, loops, and parallel
322    execution. Builders return ``self`` so a workflow can be fluently chained
323    and then run in one expression:
324
325        >>> result = (
326        ...     Workflow()
327        ...     .step("setup", lambda ctx: ctx.set("ready", True))
328        ...     .parallel([("a", task_a), ("b", task_b)])
329        ...     .run()
330        ... )
331
332    For ergonomics, ``last_step`` exposes the most recently added :class:`Step`
333    object so callers that need the underlying definition can still reach it.
334    """
335
336    def __init__(self, config: Optional[WorkflowConfig] = None) -> None:
337        """Initialize with optional ``config`` (defaults are applied otherwise)."""
338        self.config = config or WorkflowConfig()
339        self._steps: list[Step] = []
340        self._step_names: set[str] = set()
341        self._last_step: Optional[Step] = None
342
343    # ----- builders (all return self for chaining) ---------------------------
344
345    def step(
346        self,
347        name: str,
348        func: Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any],
349        condition: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool]] = None,
350    ) -> "Workflow":
351        """Append a sequential step.
352
353        Args:
354            name: Unique step name.
355            func: Step body taking the shared context.
356            condition: Optional predicate; when it returns False the step is
357                skipped.
358
359        Returns:
360            ``self`` (for chaining).
361
362        Raises:
363            ValueError: Invalid or duplicate name.
364        """
365        validated_name = _validate_step_name(name)
366        self._check_unique(validated_name)
367
368        step_obj = Step(
369            name=validated_name,
370            step_type=StepType.SIMPLE,
371            func=func,
372            condition=condition,
373        )
374        self._append(step_obj)
375        return self
376
377    def branch(
378        self,
379        condition: Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool],
380        yes_steps: Optional[list[StepSpec]] = None,
381        no_steps: Optional[list[StepSpec]] = None,
382        name: Optional[str] = None,
383    ) -> "Workflow":
384        """Append a conditional branch.
385
386        Args:
387            condition: Predicate over the context. ``True`` selects
388                ``yes_steps``; ``False`` selects ``no_steps``.
389            yes_steps: Steps to run when the predicate is True.
390            no_steps: Steps to run when the predicate is False.
391            name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.
392
393        Returns:
394            ``self`` (for chaining).
395
396        Raises:
397            ValueError: Condition is None or name is invalid/duplicate.
398        """
399        if condition is None:
400            raise ValueError("Branch condition must not be None")
401
402        branch_name = name or f"branch_{len(self._step_names)}"
403        validated_name = _validate_step_name(branch_name)
404        self._check_unique(validated_name)
405
406        step_obj = Step(
407            name=validated_name,
408            step_type=StepType.BRANCH,
409            func=condition,
410            branch_yes=self._materialize_steps(yes_steps),
411            branch_no=self._materialize_steps(no_steps),
412        )
413        self._append(step_obj)
414        return self
415
416    def parallel(
417        self,
418        steps: list[StepSpec],
419        config: Optional[ParallelConfig] = None,
420        name: Optional[str] = None,
421    ) -> "Workflow":
422        """Append a parallel step.
423
424        Args:
425            steps: Non-empty list of ``(name, func)`` tuples run concurrently.
426            config: Optional :class:`ParallelConfig` (workers + timeout).
427            name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.
428
429        Returns:
430            ``self`` (for chaining).
431
432        Raises:
433            ValueError: Empty step list, or invalid/duplicate name.
434        """
435        if not steps:
436            raise ValueError("Parallel step list must not be empty")
437
438        parallel_name = name or f"parallel_{len(self._step_names)}"
439        validated_name = _validate_step_name(parallel_name)
440        self._check_unique(validated_name)
441
442        step_obj = Step(
443            name=validated_name,
444            step_type=StepType.PARALLEL,
445            parallel_steps=self._materialize_steps(steps),
446            parallel_config=config,
447        )
448        self._append(step_obj)
449        return self
450
451    def loop(
452        self,
453        items: Union[list[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], list[Any]]],
454        step_name_template: Callable[[Any], str],
455        step_func_template: Callable[[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]],
456        config: Optional[LoopConfig] = None,
457        name: Optional[str] = None,
458    ) -> "Workflow":
459        """Append a loop step.
460
461        Args:
462            items: Either a literal list or a callable that resolves the list
463                against the context at execution time.
464            step_name_template: Maps an item to a step name.
465            step_func_template: Maps an item to the step body.
466            config: Optional :class:`LoopConfig`.
467            name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.
468
469        Returns:
470            ``self`` (for chaining).
471
472        Raises:
473            ValueError: A template is None or name is invalid/duplicate.
474        """
475        if step_name_template is None or step_func_template is None:
476            raise ValueError("Loop name/func templates must not be None")
477
478        loop_name = name or f"loop_{len(self._step_names)}"
479        validated_name = _validate_step_name(loop_name)
480        self._check_unique(validated_name)
481
482        def _make_step(item: Any) -> Step:
483            return Step(
484                name=step_name_template(item),
485                step_type=StepType.SIMPLE,
486                func=step_func_template(item),
487            )
488
489        step_obj = Step(
490            name=validated_name,
491            step_type=StepType.LOOP,
492            loop_items=items,
493            step_template=_make_step,
494            loop_config=config or LoopConfig(),
495        )
496        self._append(step_obj)
497        return self
498
499    # ----- run ---------------------------------------------------------------
500
501    def run(
502        self,
503        initial_data: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
504    ) -> WorkflowResult:
505        """Execute the workflow.
506
507        Args:
508            initial_data: Optional seed for the shared context.
509
510        Returns:
511            The :class:`WorkflowResult`.
512
513        Timeout semantics:
514            ``config.timeout`` is a real wall-clock budget checked between
515            steps. If the deadline has already passed before dispatching the
516            next step, the run stops, is reported with ``success=False`` and
517            ``error="Workflow timed out after <N>s"``, and contains the results
518            gathered so far. Steps themselves are bounded by their own
519            mechanisms (parallel steps by ``ParallelConfig.timeout``); the
520            workflow-level timeout prevents a long chain of sequential steps
521            from running indefinitely.
522        """
523        start_time = time.time()
524        context = WorkflowContext(data=initial_data or {})
525        deadline = start_time + self.config.timeout
526
527        results: list[StepResult] = []
528        success = True
529        error_msg: Optional[str] = None
530        has_any_failure = False
531        timed_out = False
532
533        for step_obj in self._steps:
534            if time.time() >= deadline:
535                timed_out = True
536                break
537
538            result = self._execute_step(step_obj, context)
539            results.append(result)
540
541            if result.status == StepStatus.FAILED:
542                has_any_failure = True
543                if not self.config.continue_on_error:
544                    success = False
545                    error_msg = result.error
546                    break
547
548        # With continue_on_error=True, the run is still marked failed if any
549        # step failed, but every step had a chance to run.
550        if has_any_failure:
551            success = False
552            if not error_msg:
553                error_msg = "One or more steps failed"
554
555        if timed_out:
556            success = False
557            error_msg = f"Workflow timed out after {self.config.timeout}s"
558
559        return WorkflowResult(
560            success=success,
561            steps=results,
562            context=context,
563            total_duration=time.time() - start_time,
564            error=error_msg,
565        )
566
567    # ----- per-step execution ------------------------------------------------
568
569    def _execute_step(
570        self,
571        step_obj: Step,
572        context: WorkflowContext,
573    ) -> StepResult:
574        """Dispatch a step to its type-specific executor."""
575        start_time = time.time()
576
577        if step_obj.step_type == StepType.BRANCH:
578            return self._execute_branch(step_obj, context, start_time)
579
580        if step_obj.step_type == StepType.PARALLEL:
581            return self._execute_parallel(step_obj, context, start_time)
582
583        if step_obj.step_type == StepType.LOOP:
584            return self._execute_loop(step_obj, context, start_time)
585
586        return self._execute_simple(step_obj, context, start_time)
587
588    def _execute_simple(
589        self,
590        step_obj: Step,
591        context: WorkflowContext,
592        start_time: float,
593    ) -> StepResult:
594        """Execute a SIMPLE step, honoring ``condition`` and retries."""
595        if step_obj.func is None:
596            return StepResult(
597                name=step_obj.name,
598                status=StepStatus.SKIPPED,
599                duration=time.time() - start_time,
600            )
601
602        # Evaluate the condition first; a failing predicate is a SKIP, not a
603        # failure.
604        if step_obj.condition is not None:
605            try:
606                if not step_obj.condition(context):
607                    return StepResult(
608                        name=step_obj.name,
609                        status=StepStatus.SKIPPED,
610                        duration=time.time() - start_time,
611                    )
612            except Exception as exc:
613                return StepResult(
614                    name=step_obj.name,
615                    status=StepStatus.FAILED,
616                    error=f"Condition check failed: {exc}",
617                    duration=time.time() - start_time,
618                )
619
620        last_error: Optional[str] = None
621        for attempt in range(self.config.retry_count + 1):
622            try:
623                output = step_obj.func(context)
624                return StepResult(
625                    name=step_obj.name,
626                    status=StepStatus.COMPLETED,
627                    output=output,
628                    duration=time.time() - start_time,
629                    retries=attempt,
630                )
631            except Exception as exc:
632                last_error = str(exc)
633                if attempt < self.config.retry_count:
634                    time.sleep(self.config.retry_delay)
635
636        return StepResult(
637            name=step_obj.name,
638            status=StepStatus.FAILED,
639            error=last_error,
640            duration=time.time() - start_time,
641            retries=self.config.retry_count,
642        )
643
644    def _execute_branch(
645        self,
646        step_obj: Step,
647        context: WorkflowContext,
648        start_time: float,
649    ) -> StepResult:
650        """Execute a BRANCH step."""
651        try:
652            # ``func`` stores the condition predicate.
653            condition_result = bool(step_obj.func(context)) if step_obj.func else False
654            branches = step_obj.branch_yes if condition_result else step_obj.branch_no
655
656            if not branches:
657                return StepResult(
658                    name=step_obj.name,
659                    status=StepStatus.COMPLETED,
660                    output={"branch": "yes" if condition_result else "no"},
661                    duration=time.time() - start_time,
662                )
663
664            branch_results: list[StepResult] = []
665            for branch_step in branches:
666                result = self._execute_step(branch_step, context)
667                branch_results.append(result)
668
669                if result.status == StepStatus.FAILED and not self.config.continue_on_error:
670                    return StepResult(
671                        name=step_obj.name,
672                        status=StepStatus.FAILED,
673                        error=f"Branch step '{result.name}' failed: {result.error}",
674                        output=branch_results,
675                        duration=time.time() - start_time,
676                    )
677
678            return StepResult(
679                name=step_obj.name,
680                status=StepStatus.COMPLETED,
681                output=branch_results,
682                duration=time.time() - start_time,
683            )
684
685        except Exception as exc:
686            return StepResult(
687                name=step_obj.name,
688                status=StepStatus.FAILED,
689                error=str(exc),
690                duration=time.time() - start_time,
691            )
692
693    def _execute_parallel(
694        self,
695        step_obj: Step,
696        context: WorkflowContext,
697        start_time: float,
698    ) -> StepResult:
699        """Execute a PARALLEL step with a real per-step timeout.
700
701        Each sub-step runs on the shared thread pool. We wait for the futures
702        with the configured timeout (from ``parallel_config`` or the
703        workflow-level budget); any future that has not resolved in time is
704        cancelled and reported FAILED, and the executor is shut down without
705        blocking so the workflow returns promptly at the deadline. The shared
706        :class:`WorkflowContext` is lock-protected, so concurrent writers do
707        not lose updates.
708
709        Note: Python cannot forcibly kill a running thread, so a sub-step that
710        ignores the deadline may keep running in the background until the
711        interpreter tears it down. The contract we honor is that
712        ``_execute_parallel`` returns at (or very near) the deadline with
713        FAILED results for any sub-step that has not finished.
714        """
715        steps = step_obj.parallel_steps or []
716        if not steps:
717            return StepResult(
718                name=step_obj.name,
719                status=StepStatus.COMPLETED,
720                duration=time.time() - start_time,
721            )
722
723        parallel_config = step_obj.parallel_config
724        max_workers = parallel_config.max_workers if parallel_config else self.config.max_workers
725        timeout = parallel_config.timeout if parallel_config else self.config.timeout
726
727        results: list[StepResult] = []
728        has_error = False
729        error_msg: Optional[str] = None
730
731        # Manage the executor manually so that on timeout we can call
732        # ``shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)`` and return immediately
733        # rather than blocking on the slow worker. ``cancel_futures`` is
734        # Python 3.9+; the project targets 3.10+.
735        executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers)
736        future_to_step: dict[Future[StepResult], Step] = {}
737        for sub_step in steps:
738            future = executor.submit(self._execute_step, sub_step, context)
739            future_to_step[future] = sub_step
740
741        deadline = start_time + timeout
742        pending: set[Future[StepResult]] = set(future_to_step.keys())
743
744        # Poll until every future resolves or the deadline passes.
745        while pending:
746            remaining = deadline - time.time()
747            if remaining <= 0:
748                break
749            done_now, pending = wait(pending, timeout=remaining, return_when=FIRST_COMPLETED)
750            if not done_now:
751                # No future finished within the remaining budget.
752                break
753
754        # Anything still in ``pending`` hit the deadline: cancel + synthesize.
755        if pending:
756            for future in pending:
757                future.cancel()
758            # Stop the pool without joining the still-running workers.
759            executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
760            for future in pending:
761                sub_step = future_to_step[future]
762                has_error = True
763                if error_msg is None:
764                    error_msg = f"Parallel task '{sub_step.name}' timed out after {timeout}s"
765                results.append(
766                    StepResult(
767                        name=sub_step.name,
768                        status=StepStatus.FAILED,
769                        error=f"timed out after {timeout}s",
770                        duration=time.time() - start_time,
771                    )
772                )
773        else:
774            executor.shutdown(wait=True)
775
776        # Materialize results in submission order, skipping ones already added
777        # above (the timed-out pending set).
778        already_added: set[Future[StepResult]] = set(pending)
779        for future, sub_step in future_to_step.items():
780            if future in already_added:
781                continue
782            try:
783                result = future.result()
784                results.append(result)
785                if result.status == StepStatus.FAILED:
786                    has_error = True
787                    if error_msg is None:
788                        error_msg = result.error
789            except Exception as exc:
790                has_error = True
791                if error_msg is None:
792                    error_msg = str(exc)
793                results.append(
794                    StepResult(
795                        name=sub_step.name,
796                        status=StepStatus.FAILED,
797                        error=str(exc),
798                    )
799                )
800
801        return StepResult(
802            name=step_obj.name,
803            status=StepStatus.FAILED if has_error else StepStatus.COMPLETED,
804            output=results,
805            error=error_msg,
806            duration=time.time() - start_time,
807        )
808
809    def _execute_loop(
810        self,
811        step_obj: Step,
812        context: WorkflowContext,
813        start_time: float,
814    ) -> StepResult:
815        """Execute a LOOP step, honoring ``max_iterations`` and ``break_on_error``."""
816        loop_config = step_obj.loop_config or LoopConfig()
817
818        try:
819            if step_obj.loop_items is None:
820                items: list[Any] = []
821            elif callable(step_obj.loop_items):
822                items = step_obj.loop_items(context)
823            else:
824                items = list(step_obj.loop_items)
825
826            if not isinstance(items, list):
827                items = list(items) if items else []
828
829            results: list[StepResult] = []
830            for iteration, item in enumerate(items):
831                if iteration >= loop_config.max_iterations:
832                    break
833
834                if step_obj.step_template is None:
835                    continue
836                template_step = step_obj.step_template(item)
837                result = self._execute_step(template_step, context)
838                results.append(result)
839
840                if result.status == StepStatus.FAILED and loop_config.break_on_error:
841                    return StepResult(
842                        name=step_obj.name,
843                        status=StepStatus.FAILED,
844                        error=f"Loop step failed: {result.error}",
845                        output=results,
846                        duration=time.time() - start_time,
847                    )
848
849            return StepResult(
850                name=step_obj.name,
851                status=StepStatus.COMPLETED,
852                output=results,
853                duration=time.time() - start_time,
854            )
855
856        except Exception as exc:
857            return StepResult(
858                name=step_obj.name,
859                status=StepStatus.FAILED,
860                error=str(exc),
861                duration=time.time() - start_time,
862            )
863
864    # ----- bookkeeping -------------------------------------------------------
865
866    def clear(self) -> None:
867        """Drop every added step."""
868        self._steps.clear()
869        self._step_names.clear()
870        self._last_step = None
871
872    @property
873    def steps(self) -> list[Step]:
874        """Return a defensive copy of all added steps."""
875        return list(self._steps)
876
877    @property
878    def last_step(self) -> Optional[Step]:
879        """The most recently added :class:`Step`, or None."""
880        return self._last_step
881
882    # ----- internals ---------------------------------------------------------
883
884    def _check_unique(self, name: str) -> None:
885        if name in self._step_names:
886            raise ValueError(f"Step name '{name}' already exists")
887
888    def _append(self, step_obj: Step) -> None:
889        self._steps.append(step_obj)
890        self._step_names.add(step_obj.name)
891        self._last_step = step_obj
892
893    @staticmethod
894    def _materialize_steps(specs: Optional[list[StepSpec]]) -> list[Step]:
895        """Convert a list of ``(name, func)`` tuples into SIMPLE steps."""
896        steps: list[Step] = []
897        if not specs:
898            return steps
899        for step_name, step_func in specs:
900            steps.append(
901                Step(
902                    name=step_name,
903                    step_type=StepType.SIMPLE,
904                    func=step_func,
905                )
906            )
907        return steps

Workflow orchestration engine.

Supports sequential steps, conditional branches, loops, and parallel execution. Builders return self so a workflow can be fluently chained and then run in one expression:

>>> result = (
...     Workflow()
...     .step("setup", lambda ctx: ctx.set("ready", True))
...     .parallel([("a", task_a), ("b", task_b)])
...     .run()
... )

For ergonomics, last_step exposes the most recently added Step object so callers that need the underlying definition can still reach it.

Workflow(config: Optional[WorkflowConfig] = None) 
336    def __init__(self, config: Optional[WorkflowConfig] = None) -> None:
337        """Initialize with optional ``config`` (defaults are applied otherwise)."""
338        self.config = config or WorkflowConfig()
339        self._steps: list[Step] = []
340        self._step_names: set[str] = set()
341        self._last_step: Optional[Step] = None

Initialize with optional config (defaults are applied otherwise).

config
def step( self, name: str, func: Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any], condition: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool]] = None) -> Workflow: 
345    def step(
346        self,
347        name: str,
348        func: Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any],
349        condition: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool]] = None,
350    ) -> "Workflow":
351        """Append a sequential step.
352
353        Args:
354            name: Unique step name.
355            func: Step body taking the shared context.
356            condition: Optional predicate; when it returns False the step is
357                skipped.
358
359        Returns:
360            ``self`` (for chaining).
361
362        Raises:
363            ValueError: Invalid or duplicate name.
364        """
365        validated_name = _validate_step_name(name)
366        self._check_unique(validated_name)
367
368        step_obj = Step(
369            name=validated_name,
370            step_type=StepType.SIMPLE,
371            func=func,
372            condition=condition,
373        )
374        self._append(step_obj)
375        return self

Append a sequential step.

Args: name: Unique step name. func: Step body taking the shared context. condition: Optional predicate; when it returns False the step is skipped.

Returns: self (for chaining).

Raises: ValueError: Invalid or duplicate name.

def branch( self, condition: Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool], yes_steps: Optional[list[tuple[str, Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]]]] = None, no_steps: Optional[list[tuple[str, Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]]]] = None, name: Optional[str] = None) -> Workflow: 
377    def branch(
378        self,
379        condition: Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool],
380        yes_steps: Optional[list[StepSpec]] = None,
381        no_steps: Optional[list[StepSpec]] = None,
382        name: Optional[str] = None,
383    ) -> "Workflow":
384        """Append a conditional branch.
385
386        Args:
387            condition: Predicate over the context. ``True`` selects
388                ``yes_steps``; ``False`` selects ``no_steps``.
389            yes_steps: Steps to run when the predicate is True.
390            no_steps: Steps to run when the predicate is False.
391            name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.
392
393        Returns:
394            ``self`` (for chaining).
395
396        Raises:
397            ValueError: Condition is None or name is invalid/duplicate.
398        """
399        if condition is None:
400            raise ValueError("Branch condition must not be None")
401
402        branch_name = name or f"branch_{len(self._step_names)}"
403        validated_name = _validate_step_name(branch_name)
404        self._check_unique(validated_name)
405
406        step_obj = Step(
407            name=validated_name,
408            step_type=StepType.BRANCH,
409            func=condition,
410            branch_yes=self._materialize_steps(yes_steps),
411            branch_no=self._materialize_steps(no_steps),
412        )
413        self._append(step_obj)
414        return self

Append a conditional branch.

Args: condition: Predicate over the context. True selects yes_steps; False selects no_steps. yes_steps: Steps to run when the predicate is True. no_steps: Steps to run when the predicate is False. name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.

Returns: self (for chaining).

Raises: ValueError: Condition is None or name is invalid/duplicate.

def parallel( self, steps: list[tuple[str, typing.Callable[[WorkflowContext], typing.Any]]], config: Optional[ParallelConfig] = None, name: Optional[str] = None) -> Workflow: 
416    def parallel(
417        self,
418        steps: list[StepSpec],
419        config: Optional[ParallelConfig] = None,
420        name: Optional[str] = None,
421    ) -> "Workflow":
422        """Append a parallel step.
423
424        Args:
425            steps: Non-empty list of ``(name, func)`` tuples run concurrently.
426            config: Optional :class:`ParallelConfig` (workers + timeout).
427            name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.
428
429        Returns:
430            ``self`` (for chaining).
431
432        Raises:
433            ValueError: Empty step list, or invalid/duplicate name.
434        """
435        if not steps:
436            raise ValueError("Parallel step list must not be empty")
437
438        parallel_name = name or f"parallel_{len(self._step_names)}"
439        validated_name = _validate_step_name(parallel_name)
440        self._check_unique(validated_name)
441
442        step_obj = Step(
443            name=validated_name,
444            step_type=StepType.PARALLEL,
445            parallel_steps=self._materialize_steps(steps),
446            parallel_config=config,
447        )
448        self._append(step_obj)
449        return self

Append a parallel step.

Args: steps: Non-empty list of (name, func) tuples run concurrently. config: Optional ParallelConfig (workers + timeout). name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.

Returns: self (for chaining).

Raises: ValueError: Empty step list, or invalid/duplicate name.

def loop( self, items: Union[list[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], list[Any]]], step_name_template: Callable[[Any], str], step_func_template: Callable[[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]], config: Optional[LoopConfig] = None, name: Optional[str] = None) -> Workflow: 
451    def loop(
452        self,
453        items: Union[list[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], list[Any]]],
454        step_name_template: Callable[[Any], str],
455        step_func_template: Callable[[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]],
456        config: Optional[LoopConfig] = None,
457        name: Optional[str] = None,
458    ) -> "Workflow":
459        """Append a loop step.
460
461        Args:
462            items: Either a literal list or a callable that resolves the list
463                against the context at execution time.
464            step_name_template: Maps an item to a step name.
465            step_func_template: Maps an item to the step body.
466            config: Optional :class:`LoopConfig`.
467            name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.
468
469        Returns:
470            ``self`` (for chaining).
471
472        Raises:
473            ValueError: A template is None or name is invalid/duplicate.
474        """
475        if step_name_template is None or step_func_template is None:
476            raise ValueError("Loop name/func templates must not be None")
477
478        loop_name = name or f"loop_{len(self._step_names)}"
479        validated_name = _validate_step_name(loop_name)
480        self._check_unique(validated_name)
481
482        def _make_step(item: Any) -> Step:
483            return Step(
484                name=step_name_template(item),
485                step_type=StepType.SIMPLE,
486                func=step_func_template(item),
487            )
488
489        step_obj = Step(
490            name=validated_name,
491            step_type=StepType.LOOP,
492            loop_items=items,
493            step_template=_make_step,
494            loop_config=config or LoopConfig(),
495        )
496        self._append(step_obj)
497        return self

Append a loop step.

Args: items: Either a literal list or a callable that resolves the list against the context at execution time. step_name_template: Maps an item to a step name. step_func_template: Maps an item to the step body. config: Optional LoopConfig. name: Optional step name; auto-generated when omitted.

Returns: self (for chaining).

Raises: ValueError: A template is None or name is invalid/duplicate.

def run( self, initial_data: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None) -> WorkflowResult: 
501    def run(
502        self,
503        initial_data: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
504    ) -> WorkflowResult:
505        """Execute the workflow.
506
507        Args:
508            initial_data: Optional seed for the shared context.
509
510        Returns:
511            The :class:`WorkflowResult`.
512
513        Timeout semantics:
514            ``config.timeout`` is a real wall-clock budget checked between
515            steps. If the deadline has already passed before dispatching the
516            next step, the run stops, is reported with ``success=False`` and
517            ``error="Workflow timed out after <N>s"``, and contains the results
518            gathered so far. Steps themselves are bounded by their own
519            mechanisms (parallel steps by ``ParallelConfig.timeout``); the
520            workflow-level timeout prevents a long chain of sequential steps
521            from running indefinitely.
522        """
523        start_time = time.time()
524        context = WorkflowContext(data=initial_data or {})
525        deadline = start_time + self.config.timeout
526
527        results: list[StepResult] = []
528        success = True
529        error_msg: Optional[str] = None
530        has_any_failure = False
531        timed_out = False
532
533        for step_obj in self._steps:
534            if time.time() >= deadline:
535                timed_out = True
536                break
537
538            result = self._execute_step(step_obj, context)
539            results.append(result)
540
541            if result.status == StepStatus.FAILED:
542                has_any_failure = True
543                if not self.config.continue_on_error:
544                    success = False
545                    error_msg = result.error
546                    break
547
548        # With continue_on_error=True, the run is still marked failed if any
549        # step failed, but every step had a chance to run.
550        if has_any_failure:
551            success = False
552            if not error_msg:
553                error_msg = "One or more steps failed"
554
555        if timed_out:
556            success = False
557            error_msg = f"Workflow timed out after {self.config.timeout}s"
558
559        return WorkflowResult(
560            success=success,
561            steps=results,
562            context=context,
563            total_duration=time.time() - start_time,
564            error=error_msg,
565        )

Execute the workflow.

Args: initial_data: Optional seed for the shared context.

Returns: The WorkflowResult.

Timeout semantics: config.timeout is a real wall-clock budget checked between steps. If the deadline has already passed before dispatching the next step, the run stops, is reported with success=False and error="Workflow timed out after <N>s", and contains the results gathered so far. Steps themselves are bounded by their own mechanisms (parallel steps by ParallelConfig.timeout); the workflow-level timeout prevents a long chain of sequential steps from running indefinitely.

def clear(self) -> None: 
866    def clear(self) -> None:
867        """Drop every added step."""
868        self._steps.clear()
869        self._step_names.clear()
870        self._last_step = None

Drop every added step.

steps: list[Step] 
872    @property
873    def steps(self) -> list[Step]:
874        """Return a defensive copy of all added steps."""
875        return list(self._steps)

Return a defensive copy of all added steps.

last_step: Optional[Step] 
877    @property
878    def last_step(self) -> Optional[Step]:
879        """The most recently added :class:`Step`, or None."""
880        return self._last_step

The most recently added Step, or None.

class WorkflowConfig(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
 84class WorkflowConfig(BaseModel):
 85    """Top-level workflow configuration.
 86
 87    Example:
 88        >>> WorkflowConfig()  # defaults
 89        >>> WorkflowConfig(max_workers=8, timeout=600.0, retry_count=2)
 90    """
 91
 92    max_workers: int = Field(
 93        default=DEFAULT_MAX_WORKERS, ge=1, le=32, description="Max threads for parallel steps"
 94    )
 95    timeout: float = Field(
 96        default=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT, ge=1.0, description="Whole-workflow timeout in seconds"
 97    )
 98    retry_count: int = Field(default=DEFAULT_RETRY_COUNT, ge=0, le=10, description="Retries on failure")
 99    retry_delay: float = Field(default=DEFAULT_RETRY_DELAY, ge=0.0, description="Delay between retries (s)")
100    continue_on_error: bool = Field(
101        default=False, description="Whether to keep running after a step fails"
102    )
103
104    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", validate_default=True)

Top-level workflow configuration.

Example:

WorkflowConfig() # defaults WorkflowConfig(max_workers=8, timeout=600.0, retry_count=2)

max_workers: int = 4

Max threads for parallel steps

timeout: float = 300.0

Whole-workflow timeout in seconds

retry_count: int = 0

Retries on failure

retry_delay: float = 1.0

Delay between retries (s)

continue_on_error: bool = False

Whether to keep running after a step fails

class WorkflowContext(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
146class WorkflowContext(BaseModel):
147    """Execution context shared across steps.
148
149    The ``data`` and ``metadata`` dicts are mutated by step functions, including
150    from concurrent threads during a parallel step. All accessors take a lock so
151    concurrent writers cannot lose updates.
152
153    Example:
154        >>> ctx = WorkflowContext()
155        >>> ctx.set("key", "value")
156        >>> ctx.get("key")
157        'value'
158    """
159
160    data: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Context data")
161    metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Metadata")
162
163    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
164
165    # Private attribute (not validated/serialized). RLock allows nested locking
166    # from the same thread: ``get``/``set``/``update``/``clear`` all acquire it
167    # and may be called from inside a ``with ctx.lock:`` block the user holds.
168    _lock: threading.RLock = PrivateAttr(default_factory=threading.RLock)
169
170    @property
171    def lock(self) -> threading.RLock:
172        """Return the context's lock.
173
174        Each individual ``get`` / ``set`` / ``update`` / ``clear`` is atomic,
175        so concurrent writers cannot corrupt the dict. A *compound*
176        read-modify-write (``ctx.set(k, ctx.get(k) + 1)``) is NOT atomic by
177        itself; callers that need that should hold this lock explicitly::
178
179            with ctx.lock:
180                ctx.set("counter", ctx.get("counter") + 1)
181
182        The lock is reentrant, so ``get``/``set`` work correctly inside that
183        ``with`` block.
184        """
185        return self._lock
186
187    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
188        """Return ``data[key]`` if present, else ``default``."""
189        with self._lock:
190            return self.data.get(key, default)
191
192    def set(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None:
193        """Store ``value`` under ``key``."""
194        with self._lock:
195            self.data[key] = value
196
197    def update(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
198        """Merge ``data`` into the context atomically."""
199        with self._lock:
200            self.data.update(data)
201
202    def clear(self) -> None:
203        """Drop all data and metadata."""
204        with self._lock:
205            self.data.clear()
206            self.metadata.clear()

Execution context shared across steps.

The data and metadata dicts are mutated by step functions, including from concurrent threads during a parallel step. All accessors take a lock so concurrent writers cannot lose updates.

Example:

ctx = WorkflowContext() ctx.set("key", "value") ctx.get("key") 'value'

data: dict[str, typing.Any] = PydanticUndefined

Context data

metadata: dict[str, typing.Any] = PydanticUndefined

Metadata

lock: <function RLock at 0x0000015FB3ACFC40> 
170    @property
171    def lock(self) -> threading.RLock:
172        """Return the context's lock.
173
174        Each individual ``get`` / ``set`` / ``update`` / ``clear`` is atomic,
175        so concurrent writers cannot corrupt the dict. A *compound*
176        read-modify-write (``ctx.set(k, ctx.get(k) + 1)``) is NOT atomic by
177        itself; callers that need that should hold this lock explicitly::
178
179            with ctx.lock:
180                ctx.set("counter", ctx.get("counter") + 1)
181
182        The lock is reentrant, so ``get``/``set`` work correctly inside that
183        ``with`` block.
184        """
185        return self._lock

Return the context's lock.

Each individual get / set / update / clear is atomic, so concurrent writers cannot corrupt the dict. A compound read-modify-write (ctx.set(k, ctx.get(k) + 1)) is NOT atomic by itself; callers that need that should hold this lock explicitly::

with ctx.lock:
    ctx.set("counter", ctx.get("counter") + 1)

The lock is reentrant, so get/set work correctly inside that with block.

def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any: 
187    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
188        """Return ``data[key]`` if present, else ``default``."""
189        with self._lock:
190            return self.data.get(key, default)

Return data[key] if present, else default.

def set(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None: 
192    def set(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None:
193        """Store ``value`` under ``key``."""
194        with self._lock:
195            self.data[key] = value

Store value under key.

def update(self, data: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> None: 
197    def update(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
198        """Merge ``data`` into the context atomically."""
199        with self._lock:
200            self.data.update(data)

Merge data into the context atomically.

def clear(self) -> None: 
202    def clear(self) -> None:
203        """Drop all data and metadata."""
204        with self._lock:
205            self.data.clear()
206            self.metadata.clear()

Drop all data and metadata.

@dataclass
class WorkflowResult: 
234@dataclass
235class WorkflowResult:
236    """Result of executing the whole workflow.
237
238    Attributes:
239        success: True iff no step failed (or failures were tolerated).
240        steps: Per-step results, in execution order.
241        context: Final shared context.
242        total_duration: Wall-clock duration in seconds.
243        error: Optional aggregated error message.
244    """
245
246    success: bool
247    steps: list[StepResult] = field(default_factory=list)
248    context: Optional[WorkflowContext] = None
249    total_duration: float = 0.0
250    error: Optional[str] = None

Result of executing the whole workflow.

Attributes: success: True iff no step failed (or failures were tolerated). steps: Per-step results, in execution order. context: Final shared context. total_duration: Wall-clock duration in seconds. error: Optional aggregated error message.

WorkflowResult( success: bool, steps: list[StepResult] = <factory>, context: Optional[WorkflowContext] = None, total_duration: float = 0.0, error: Optional[str] = None)
success: bool
steps: list[StepResult]
context: Optional[WorkflowContext] = None
total_duration: float = 0.0
error: Optional[str] = None
@dataclass
class StepResult: 
213@dataclass
214class StepResult:
215    """Result of executing one step.
216
217    Attributes:
218        name: Step name.
219        status: Outcome status.
220        output: Optional payload produced by the step.
221        error: Optional human-readable error message.
222        duration: Wall-clock duration in seconds.
223        retries: Number of retries that were performed.
224    """
225
226    name: str
227    status: StepStatus
228    output: Any = None
229    error: Optional[str] = None
230    duration: float = 0.0
231    retries: int = 0

Result of executing one step.

Attributes: name: Step name. status: Outcome status. output: Optional payload produced by the step. error: Optional human-readable error message. duration: Wall-clock duration in seconds. retries: Number of retries that were performed.

StepResult( name: str, status: StepStatus, output: Any = None, error: Optional[str] = None, duration: float = 0.0, retries: int = 0)
name: str
status: StepStatus
output: Any = None
error: Optional[str] = None
duration: float = 0.0
retries: int = 0
class StepStatus(builtins.str, enum.Enum): 
54class StepStatus(str, Enum):
55    """Status of a single step execution."""
56
57    PENDING = "pending"
58    RUNNING = "running"
59    COMPLETED = "completed"
60    FAILED = "failed"
61    SKIPPED = "skipped"

Status of a single step execution.

PENDING = <StepStatus.PENDING: 'pending'>
RUNNING = <StepStatus.RUNNING: 'running'>
COMPLETED = <StepStatus.COMPLETED: 'completed'>
FAILED = <StepStatus.FAILED: 'failed'>
SKIPPED = <StepStatus.SKIPPED: 'skipped'>
@dataclass
class Step: 
257@dataclass
258class Step:
259    """A single workflow step.
260
261    The meaning of the fields depends on ``step_type``:
262
263    - ``SIMPLE``: ``func`` runs against the context (``condition`` optional).
264    - ``BRANCH``: ``func`` is the predicate; ``branch_yes``/``branch_no`` are
265      the two sequential sub-lists.
266    - ``PARALLEL``: ``parallel_steps`` runs concurrently.
267    - ``LOOP``: ``loop_items`` is a list or callable returning a list;
268      ``step_template`` produces a :class:`Step` per item.
269    """
270
271    name: str
272    step_type: StepType = StepType.SIMPLE
273    func: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]] = None
274    condition: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool]] = None
275    branch_yes: Optional[list[Step]] = None
276    branch_no: Optional[list[Step]] = None
277    parallel_steps: Optional[list[Step]] = None
278    loop_items: Optional[Union[list[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], list[Any]]]] = None
279    step_template: Optional[Callable[[Any], Step]] = None
280    loop_config: Optional[LoopConfig] = None
281    parallel_config: Optional[ParallelConfig] = None

A single workflow step.

The meaning of the fields depends on step_type:

  • SIMPLE: func runs against the context (condition optional).
  • BRANCH: func is the predicate; branch_yes/branch_no are the two sequential sub-lists.
  • PARALLEL: parallel_steps runs concurrently.
  • LOOP: loop_items is a list or callable returning a list; step_template produces a Step per item.
Step( name: str, step_type: StepType = <StepType.SIMPLE: 'simple'>, func: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]] = None, condition: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool]] = None, branch_yes: Optional[list[Step]] = None, branch_no: Optional[list[Step]] = None, parallel_steps: Optional[list[Step]] = None, loop_items: Union[list[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], list[Any]], NoneType] = None, step_template: Optional[Callable[[Any], Step]] = None, loop_config: Optional[LoopConfig] = None, parallel_config: Optional[ParallelConfig] = None)
name: str
step_type: StepType = <StepType.SIMPLE: 'simple'>
func: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], Any]] = None
condition: Optional[Callable[[WorkflowContext], bool]] = None
branch_yes: Optional[list[Step]] = None
branch_no: Optional[list[Step]] = None
parallel_steps: Optional[list[Step]] = None
loop_items: Union[list[Any], Callable[[WorkflowContext], list[Any]], NoneType] = None
step_template: Optional[Callable[[Any], Step]] = None
loop_config: Optional[LoopConfig] = None
parallel_config: Optional[ParallelConfig] = None
class StepType(builtins.str, enum.Enum): 
71class StepType(str, Enum):
72    """Discriminator for :class:`Step`."""
73
74    SIMPLE = "simple"
75    BRANCH = "branch"
76    PARALLEL = "parallel"
77    LOOP = "loop"

Discriminator for Step.

SIMPLE = <StepType.SIMPLE: 'simple'>
BRANCH = <StepType.BRANCH: 'branch'>
PARALLEL = <StepType.PARALLEL: 'parallel'>
LOOP = <StepType.LOOP: 'loop'>
class ParallelConfig(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
107class ParallelConfig(BaseModel):
108    """Configuration for a parallel step.
109
110    ``timeout`` is real: each parallel step's futures are awaited with this
111    budget; any future that has not resolved in time is cancelled and the step
112    is reported FAILED.
113
114    Example:
115        >>> ParallelConfig(max_workers=4, timeout=60.0)
116    """
117
118    max_workers: int = Field(
119        default=DEFAULT_MAX_WORKERS, ge=1, le=32, description="Max parallel threads"
120    )
121    timeout: float = Field(
122        default=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT, ge=1.0, description="Per-step parallel timeout in seconds"
123    )
124
125    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", validate_default=True)

Configuration for a parallel step.

timeout is real: each parallel step's futures are awaited with this budget; any future that has not resolved in time is cancelled and the step is reported FAILED.

Example:

ParallelConfig(max_workers=4, timeout=60.0)

max_workers: int = 4

Max parallel threads

timeout: float = 300.0

Per-step parallel timeout in seconds

class LoopConfig(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
128class LoopConfig(BaseModel):
129    """Configuration for a loop step.
130
131    Example:
132        >>> LoopConfig(loop_type=LoopType.WHILE, max_iterations=100)
133    """
134
135    loop_type: LoopType = Field(default=LoopType.FOR, description="Loop strategy")
136    max_iterations: int = Field(default=100, ge=1, le=10000, description="Hard iteration cap")
137    break_on_error: bool = Field(default=True, description="Stop the loop on the first failure")
138
139    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", validate_default=True)

Configuration for a loop step.

Example:

LoopConfig(loop_type=LoopType.WHILE, max_iterations=100)

loop_type: LoopType = <LoopType.FOR: 'for'>

Loop strategy

max_iterations: int = 100

Hard iteration cap

break_on_error: bool = True

Stop the loop on the first failure

class LoopType(builtins.str, enum.Enum): 
64class LoopType(str, Enum):
65    """Loop strategy."""
66
67    FOR = "for"
68    WHILE = "while"

Loop strategy.

FOR = <LoopType.FOR: 'for'>
WHILE = <LoopType.WHILE: 'while'>
class LocalModelManager: 
370class LocalModelManager:
371    """Local model manager.
372
373    Downloads, loads, caches and unloads local models. The cache directory is
374    created lazily on first download; the constructor performs no filesystem
375    I/O so merely instantiating the manager (e.g. to inspect metadata) is
376    side-effect free.
377
378    Example:
379        >>> manager = LocalModelManager()
380        >>> manager.list_models(ModelCategory.SPEECH_TO_TEXT)
381        >>> manager.download("whisper-small")
382        >>> model = manager.load("whisper-small")
383        >>> result = model.transcribe("audio.mp3")
384    """
385
386    def __init__(self, config: Optional[LocalModelConfig] = None) -> None:
387        self.config = config or LocalModelConfig(model_name="")
388        self._loaded_models: dict[str, Any] = {}
389        self._cache_dir = (
390            Path(self.config.cache_dir)
391            if self.config.cache_dir is not None
392            else DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR
393        )
394        # NOTE: deliberately no mkdir here — construction is side-effect free.
395
396    def list_models(
397        self,
398        category: Optional[ModelCategory] = None,
399        language: Optional[str] = None,
400        tag: Optional[str] = None,
401    ) -> list[LocalModelInfo]:
402        """List available models, optionally filtered.
403
404        Args:
405            category: Filter by model category.
406            language: Filter by supported language.
407            tag: Filter by tag.
408
409        Returns:
410            List of matching model metadata.
411        """
412        models = list(AVAILABLE_MODELS.values())
413
414        if category:
415            models = [m for m in models if m.category == category]
416
417        if language:
418            models = [m for m in models if language in m.languages or "auto" in m.languages]
419
420        if tag:
421            models = [m for m in models if tag in m.tags]
422
423        return models
424
425    def get_model_info(self, model_name: str) -> Optional[LocalModelInfo]:
426        """Get metadata for a named model, or ``None`` if unknown."""
427        return AVAILABLE_MODELS.get(model_name)
428
429    def is_downloaded(self, model_name: str) -> bool:
430        """Return True if a model appears to be present in the cache."""
431        model_info = self.get_model_info(model_name)
432        if not model_info:
433            return False
434
435        model_dir = self._cache_dir / model_name
436        return model_dir.exists() and any(model_dir.iterdir())
437
438    def download(
439        self,
440        model_name: str,
441        progress_callback: Optional[Callable[[float], None]] = None,
442    ) -> bool:
443        """Download a model into the cache.
444
445        Args:
446            model_name: Model name (must be in :data:`AVAILABLE_MODELS`).
447            progress_callback: Called with progress in ``[0.0, 1.0]``.
448
449        Returns:
450            True on success, False on failure.
451        """
452        model_info = self.get_model_info(model_name)
453        if not model_info:
454            raise ValueError(f"Unknown model: {model_name}")
455
456        # Lazily create the cache directory on first download only.
457        model_dir = self._cache_dir / model_name
458        model_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
459
460        logger.info("Downloading model %s...", model_name)
461        logger.info("  size: %.0f MB", model_info.network.download_size_mb)
462        logger.info("  category: %s", model_info.category.value)
463        logger.info("  languages: %s", ", ".join(model_info.languages))
464
465        # Dispatch to the category-specific downloader. Each downloader is
466        # responsible for its own transient-failure handling (returns False on
467        # runtime errors, re-raises ImportError for missing optional deps so
468        # the caller gets an actionable configuration error instead of a
469        # misleading "download failed" message).
470        if model_info.category == ModelCategory.SPEECH_TO_TEXT:
471            return self._download_whisper(model_name, progress_callback)
472        elif model_info.category == ModelCategory.EMBEDDING:
473            return self._download_embedding(model_name, model_info, progress_callback)
474        else:
475            logger.warning(
476                "Download for category %s is not supported.",
477                model_info.category.value,
478            )
479            return False
480
481    def _download_whisper(
482        self,
483        model_name: str,
484        progress_callback: Optional[Callable[[float], None]] = None,
485    ) -> bool:
486        """Download a Whisper model via ``openai-whisper``'s own loader."""
487        try:
488            import whisper
489        except ImportError as exc:
490            raise ImportError(
491                "openai-whisper is required: pip install openai-whisper "
492                "(or faster-whisper: pip install faster-whisper)"
493            ) from exc
494
495        whisper_name = model_name.replace("whisper-", "")
496
497        try:
498            # whisper.load_model performs the download using its own URL
499            # resolution; the bytes are cached under download_root.
500            whisper.load_model(
501                whisper_name,
502                download_root=str(self._cache_dir),
503            )
504
505            if progress_callback:
506                progress_callback(1.0)
507
508            logger.info("Model %s downloaded.", model_name)
509            return True
510        except Exception as e:
511            logger.error("Whisper download failed: %s", e)
512            return False
513
514    def _download_embedding(
515        self,
516        model_name: str,
517        model_info: LocalModelInfo,
518        progress_callback: Optional[Callable[[float], None]] = None,
519    ) -> bool:
520        """Download an embedding model via ``sentence-transformers``.
521
522        When ``use_mirror`` is enabled and the model declares mirrors, the
523        first mirror is exported as ``HF_ENDPOINT`` so ``huggingface_hub``
524        (used internally by sentence-transformers) actually fetches from the
525        mirror instead of the default endpoint.
526        """
527        try:
528            from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
529        except ImportError as exc:
530            raise ImportError(
531                "sentence-transformers is required: pip install sentence-transformers"
532            ) from exc
533
534        mirror_endpoint = self._select_mirror(model_info)
535
536        try:
537            with _export_hf_endpoint(mirror_endpoint):
538                SentenceTransformer(
539                    model_name,
540                    cache_folder=str(self._cache_dir),
541                )
542
543            if progress_callback:
544                progress_callback(1.0)
545
546            logger.info("Model %s downloaded.", model_name)
547            return True
548        except Exception as e:
549            logger.error("Embedding download failed: %s", e)
550            return False
551
552    def _select_mirror(self, model_info: LocalModelInfo) -> Optional[str]:
553        """Pick the HF mirror endpoint to use, or ``None`` for the default."""
554        if not self.config.use_mirror:
555            return None
556        if not model_info.network.mirrors:
557            return None
558        return model_info.network.mirrors[0]
559
560    def load(self, model_name: str) -> Any:
561        """Load (downloading first if needed) and cache a model instance."""
562        if model_name in self._loaded_models:
563            return self._loaded_models[model_name]
564
565        model_info = self.get_model_info(model_name)
566        if not model_info:
567            raise ValueError(f"Unknown model: {model_name}")
568
569        if not self.is_downloaded(model_name):
570            logger.info("Model %s not cached, downloading...", model_name)
571            self.download(model_name)
572
573        logger.info("Loading model %s...", model_name)
574
575        if model_info.category == ModelCategory.SPEECH_TO_TEXT:
576            model: Any = self._load_whisper(model_name)
577        elif model_info.category == ModelCategory.EMBEDDING:
578            model = self._load_embedding(model_name, model_info)
579        else:
580            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported model category: {model_info.category}")
581
582        self._loaded_models[model_name] = model
583        return model
584
585    def _load_whisper(self, model_name: str) -> WhisperModel:
586        """Load a Whisper model."""
587        try:
588            import whisper
589        except ImportError as exc:
590            raise ImportError(
591                "openai-whisper is required: pip install openai-whisper"
592            ) from exc
593
594        whisper_name = model_name.replace("whisper-", "")
595        device = self._get_device()
596
597        model = whisper.load_model(
598            whisper_name,
599            download_root=str(self._cache_dir),
600            device=device,
601        )
602
603        return WhisperModel(model, model_name, device)
604
605    def _load_embedding(self, model_name: str, model_info: LocalModelInfo) -> EmbeddingModel:
606        """Load an embedding model (applying the configured mirror)."""
607        try:
608            from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
609        except ImportError as exc:
610            raise ImportError(
611                "sentence-transformers is required: pip install sentence-transformers"
612            ) from exc
613
614        device = self._get_device()
615        mirror_endpoint = self._select_mirror(model_info)
616
617        with _export_hf_endpoint(mirror_endpoint):
618            model = SentenceTransformer(
619                model_name,
620                cache_folder=str(self._cache_dir),
621                device=device,
622            )
623
624        return EmbeddingModel(model, model_name, device)
625
626    def _get_device(self) -> str:
627        """Resolve the compute device. Falls back to ``cpu`` when torch is absent."""
628        if self.config.device != "auto":
629            return self.config.device
630
631        try:
632            import torch
633
634            if torch.cuda.is_available():
635                return "cuda"
636            if hasattr(torch.backends, "mps") and torch.backends.mps.is_available():
637                return "mps"
638        except ImportError:
639            pass
640
641        return "cpu"
642
643    def unload(self, model_name: str) -> None:
644        """Unload a model from the in-memory cache."""
645        if model_name in self._loaded_models:
646            del self._loaded_models[model_name]
647            logger.info("Unloaded model %s.", model_name)
648
649    def clear_cache(self, model_name: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
650        """Clear the on-disk cache for one model, or all models if ``None``."""
651        import shutil
652
653        if model_name:
654            model_dir = self._cache_dir / model_name
655            if model_dir.exists():
656                shutil.rmtree(model_dir)
657                logger.info("Cleared cache for model %s.", model_name)
658        else:
659            if self._cache_dir.exists():
660                shutil.rmtree(self._cache_dir)
661                self._cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
662                logger.info("Cleared all model caches.")
663
664    def get_cache_size(self) -> float:
665        """Return the cache size in MB (0 if the cache does not exist)."""
666        total_size = 0
667        if self._cache_dir.exists():
668            for path in self._cache_dir.rglob("*"):
669                if path.is_file():
670                    total_size += path.stat().st_size
671        return total_size / (1024 * 1024)

Local model manager.

Downloads, loads, caches and unloads local models. The cache directory is created lazily on first download; the constructor performs no filesystem I/O so merely instantiating the manager (e.g. to inspect metadata) is side-effect free.

Example:

manager = LocalModelManager() manager.list_models(ModelCategory.SPEECH_TO_TEXT) manager.download("whisper-small") model = manager.load("whisper-small") result = model.transcribe("audio.mp3")

LocalModelManager(config: Optional[LocalModelConfig] = None) 
386    def __init__(self, config: Optional[LocalModelConfig] = None) -> None:
387        self.config = config or LocalModelConfig(model_name="")
388        self._loaded_models: dict[str, Any] = {}
389        self._cache_dir = (
390            Path(self.config.cache_dir)
391            if self.config.cache_dir is not None
392            else DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR
393        )
394        # NOTE: deliberately no mkdir here — construction is side-effect free.
config
def list_models( self, category: Optional[ModelCategory] = None, language: Optional[str] = None, tag: Optional[str] = None) -> list[LocalModelInfo]: 
396    def list_models(
397        self,
398        category: Optional[ModelCategory] = None,
399        language: Optional[str] = None,
400        tag: Optional[str] = None,
401    ) -> list[LocalModelInfo]:
402        """List available models, optionally filtered.
403
404        Args:
405            category: Filter by model category.
406            language: Filter by supported language.
407            tag: Filter by tag.
408
409        Returns:
410            List of matching model metadata.
411        """
412        models = list(AVAILABLE_MODELS.values())
413
414        if category:
415            models = [m for m in models if m.category == category]
416
417        if language:
418            models = [m for m in models if language in m.languages or "auto" in m.languages]
419
420        if tag:
421            models = [m for m in models if tag in m.tags]
422
423        return models

List available models, optionally filtered.

Args: category: Filter by model category. language: Filter by supported language. tag: Filter by tag.

Returns: List of matching model metadata.

def get_model_info(self, model_name: str) -> Optional[LocalModelInfo]: 
425    def get_model_info(self, model_name: str) -> Optional[LocalModelInfo]:
426        """Get metadata for a named model, or ``None`` if unknown."""
427        return AVAILABLE_MODELS.get(model_name)

Get metadata for a named model, or None if unknown.

def is_downloaded(self, model_name: str) -> bool: 
429    def is_downloaded(self, model_name: str) -> bool:
430        """Return True if a model appears to be present in the cache."""
431        model_info = self.get_model_info(model_name)
432        if not model_info:
433            return False
434
435        model_dir = self._cache_dir / model_name
436        return model_dir.exists() and any(model_dir.iterdir())

def download( self, model_name: str, progress_callback: Optional[Callable[[float], NoneType]] = None) -> bool: 
438    def download(
439        self,
440        model_name: str,
441        progress_callback: Optional[Callable[[float], None]] = None,
442    ) -> bool:
443        """Download a model into the cache.
444
445        Args:
446            model_name: Model name (must be in :data:`AVAILABLE_MODELS`).
447            progress_callback: Called with progress in ``[0.0, 1.0]``.
448
449        Returns:
450            True on success, False on failure.
451        """
452        model_info = self.get_model_info(model_name)
453        if not model_info:
454            raise ValueError(f"Unknown model: {model_name}")
455
456        # Lazily create the cache directory on first download only.
457        model_dir = self._cache_dir / model_name
458        model_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
459
460        logger.info("Downloading model %s...", model_name)
461        logger.info("  size: %.0f MB", model_info.network.download_size_mb)
462        logger.info("  category: %s", model_info.category.value)
463        logger.info("  languages: %s", ", ".join(model_info.languages))
464
465        # Dispatch to the category-specific downloader. Each downloader is
466        # responsible for its own transient-failure handling (returns False on
467        # runtime errors, re-raises ImportError for missing optional deps so
468        # the caller gets an actionable configuration error instead of a
469        # misleading "download failed" message).
470        if model_info.category == ModelCategory.SPEECH_TO_TEXT:
471            return self._download_whisper(model_name, progress_callback)
472        elif model_info.category == ModelCategory.EMBEDDING:
473            return self._download_embedding(model_name, model_info, progress_callback)
474        else:
475            logger.warning(
476                "Download for category %s is not supported.",
477                model_info.category.value,
478            )
479            return False

Download a model into the cache.

Args: model_name: Model name (must be in AVAILABLE_MODELS). progress_callback: Called with progress in [0.0, 1.0].

Returns: True on success, False on failure.

def load(self, model_name: str) -> Any: 
560    def load(self, model_name: str) -> Any:
561        """Load (downloading first if needed) and cache a model instance."""
562        if model_name in self._loaded_models:
563            return self._loaded_models[model_name]
564
565        model_info = self.get_model_info(model_name)
566        if not model_info:
567            raise ValueError(f"Unknown model: {model_name}")
568
569        if not self.is_downloaded(model_name):
570            logger.info("Model %s not cached, downloading...", model_name)
571            self.download(model_name)
572
573        logger.info("Loading model %s...", model_name)
574
575        if model_info.category == ModelCategory.SPEECH_TO_TEXT:
576            model: Any = self._load_whisper(model_name)
577        elif model_info.category == ModelCategory.EMBEDDING:
578            model = self._load_embedding(model_name, model_info)
579        else:
580            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported model category: {model_info.category}")
581
582        self._loaded_models[model_name] = model
583        return model

Load (downloading first if needed) and cache a model instance.

def unload(self, model_name: str) -> None: 
643    def unload(self, model_name: str) -> None:
644        """Unload a model from the in-memory cache."""
645        if model_name in self._loaded_models:
646            del self._loaded_models[model_name]
647            logger.info("Unloaded model %s.", model_name)

Unload a model from the in-memory cache.

def clear_cache(self, model_name: Optional[str] = None) -> None: 
649    def clear_cache(self, model_name: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
650        """Clear the on-disk cache for one model, or all models if ``None``."""
651        import shutil
652
653        if model_name:
654            model_dir = self._cache_dir / model_name
655            if model_dir.exists():
656                shutil.rmtree(model_dir)
657                logger.info("Cleared cache for model %s.", model_name)
658        else:
659            if self._cache_dir.exists():
660                shutil.rmtree(self._cache_dir)
661                self._cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
662                logger.info("Cleared all model caches.")

Clear the on-disk cache for one model, or all models if None.

def get_cache_size(self) -> float: 
664    def get_cache_size(self) -> float:
665        """Return the cache size in MB (0 if the cache does not exist)."""
666        total_size = 0
667        if self._cache_dir.exists():
668            for path in self._cache_dir.rglob("*"):
669                if path.is_file():
670                    total_size += path.stat().st_size
671        return total_size / (1024 * 1024)

Return the cache size in MB (0 if the cache does not exist).

@dataclass
class LocalModelConfig: 
355@dataclass
356class LocalModelConfig:
357    """Local model manager configuration."""
358
359    model_name: str
360    cache_dir: Optional[str] = None
361    device: str = "auto"  # auto, cpu, cuda, mps
362    download_timeout: int = 3600  # seconds
363    use_mirror: bool = True  # export HF_ENDPOINT for the configured mirror
364
365    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
366        if self.cache_dir is None:
367            self.cache_dir = str(DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR)

Local model manager configuration.

LocalModelConfig( model_name: str, cache_dir: Optional[str] = None, device: str = 'auto', download_timeout: int = 3600, use_mirror: bool = True)
model_name: str
cache_dir: Optional[str] = None
device: str = 'auto'
download_timeout: int = 3600
use_mirror: bool = True
@dataclass
class LocalModelInfo: 
 96@dataclass
 97class LocalModelInfo:
 98    """Metadata for a locally-downloadable model.
 99
100    Renamed from ``ModelInfo`` (kept in :mod:`vertai.local` as a deprecated
101    alias) to distinguish it from :class:`vertai.core.llm.LLMModelInfo` which
102    describes cloud LLM models.
103    """
104
105    name: str
106    category: ModelCategory
107    description: str
108    version: str
109    hardware: HardwareRequirements
110    network: NetworkRequirements
111    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
112    tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
113    license: str = "MIT"
114
115    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
116        return {
117            "name": self.name,
118            "category": self.category.value,
119            "description": self.description,
120            "version": self.version,
121            "hardware": self.hardware.to_dict(),
122            "network": self.network.to_dict(),
123            "languages": self.languages,
124            "tags": self.tags,
125            "license": self.license,
126        }

Metadata for a locally-downloadable model.

Renamed from ModelInfo (kept in vertai.local as a deprecated alias) to distinguish it from vertai.core.llm.LLMModelInfo which describes cloud LLM models.

LocalModelInfo( name: str, category: ModelCategory, description: str, version: str, hardware: vertai.local.models.HardwareRequirements, network: vertai.local.models.NetworkRequirements, languages: list[str] = <factory>, tags: list[str] = <factory>, license: str = 'MIT')
name: str
category: ModelCategory
description: str
version: str
hardware: vertai.local.models.HardwareRequirements
network: vertai.local.models.NetworkRequirements
languages: list[str]
tags: list[str]
license: str = 'MIT'
def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
115    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
116        return {
117            "name": self.name,
118            "category": self.category.value,
119            "description": self.description,
120            "version": self.version,
121            "hardware": self.hardware.to_dict(),
122            "network": self.network.to_dict(),
123            "languages": self.languages,
124            "tags": self.tags,
125            "license": self.license,
126        }
class ModelCategory(enum.Enum): 
41class ModelCategory(Enum):
42    """Model category."""
43
44    SPEECH_TO_TEXT = "speech_to_text"
45    EMBEDDING = "embedding"
46    IMAGE = "image"
47    TEXT_GENERATION = "text_generation"

Model category.

SPEECH_TO_TEXT = <ModelCategory.SPEECH_TO_TEXT: 'speech_to_text'>
EMBEDDING = <ModelCategory.EMBEDDING: 'embedding'>
IMAGE = <ModelCategory.IMAGE: 'image'>
TEXT_GENERATION = <ModelCategory.TEXT_GENERATION: 'text_generation'>
ModelInfo = <class 'LocalModelInfo'>
class WhisperModel: 
705class WhisperModel:
706    """Whisper speech-to-text model wrapper."""
707
708    def __init__(self, model: Any, model_name: str, device: str) -> None:
709        self._model = model
710        self._model_name = model_name
711        self._device = device
712
713    def transcribe(
714        self,
715        audio_path: str,
716        language: Optional[str] = None,
717        task: str = "transcribe",  # transcribe, translate
718        verbose: bool = False,
719    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
720        """Transcribe an audio file.
721
722        Args:
723            audio_path: Path to an audio file (mp3, wav, m4a, ...).
724            language: Language code (zh, en, ja, ...). ``None`` auto-detects.
725            task: ``transcribe`` keeps the source language; ``translate``
726                translates to English.
727            verbose: If True, the underlying library prints progress.
728
729        Returns:
730            Dict with ``text``, ``segments`` and ``language``.
731        """
732        logger.info("Transcribing: %s", audio_path)
733
734        result = self._model.transcribe(
735            audio_path,
736            language=language,
737            task=task,
738            verbose=verbose,
739        )
740
741        return {
742            "text": result.get("text", ""),
743            "segments": result.get("segments", []),
744            "language": result.get("language", "unknown"),
745        }
746
747    def detect_language(self, audio_path: str) -> str:
748        """Detect the dominant language of an audio file."""
749        import whisper
750
751        audio = whisper.load_audio(audio_path)
752        audio = whisper.pad_or_trim(audio)
753        mel = whisper.log_mel_spectrogram(audio).to(self._model.device)
754        _, probs = self._model.detect_language(mel)
755        detected = max(probs, key=probs.get)
756        return str(detected)

Whisper speech-to-text model wrapper.

WhisperModel(model: Any, model_name: str, device: str)
