709        self._model = model
710        self._model_name = model_name
711        self._device = device
def transcribe( self, audio_path: str, language: Optional[str] = None, task: str = 'transcribe', verbose: bool = False) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
713    def transcribe(
714        self,
715        audio_path: str,
716        language: Optional[str] = None,
717        task: str = "transcribe",  # transcribe, translate
718        verbose: bool = False,
719    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
720        """Transcribe an audio file.
721
722        Args:
723            audio_path: Path to an audio file (mp3, wav, m4a, ...).
724            language: Language code (zh, en, ja, ...). ``None`` auto-detects.
725            task: ``transcribe`` keeps the source language; ``translate``
726                translates to English.
727            verbose: If True, the underlying library prints progress.
728
729        Returns:
730            Dict with ``text``, ``segments`` and ``language``.
731        """
732        logger.info("Transcribing: %s", audio_path)
733
734        result = self._model.transcribe(
735            audio_path,
736            language=language,
737            task=task,
738            verbose=verbose,
739        )
740
741        return {
742            "text": result.get("text", ""),
743            "segments": result.get("segments", []),
744            "language": result.get("language", "unknown"),
745        }

Transcribe an audio file.

Args: audio_path: Path to an audio file (mp3, wav, m4a, ...). language: Language code (zh, en, ja, ...). None auto-detects. task: transcribe keeps the source language; translate translates to English. verbose: If True, the underlying library prints progress.

Returns: Dict with text, segments and language.

def detect_language(self, audio_path: str) -> str: 
747    def detect_language(self, audio_path: str) -> str:
748        """Detect the dominant language of an audio file."""
749        import whisper
750
751        audio = whisper.load_audio(audio_path)
752        audio = whisper.pad_or_trim(audio)
753        mel = whisper.log_mel_spectrogram(audio).to(self._model.device)
754        _, probs = self._model.detect_language(mel)
755        detected = max(probs, key=probs.get)
756        return str(detected)

Detect the dominant language of an audio file.

class EmbeddingModel: 
759class EmbeddingModel:
760    """Sentence embedding model wrapper."""
761
762    def __init__(self, model: Any, model_name: str, device: str) -> None:
763        self._model = model
764        self._model_name = model_name
765        self._device = device
766
767    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
768        """Embed a single text into a vector."""
769        embedding = self._model.encode(text)
770        return embedding.tolist()  # type: ignore[no-any-return]
771
772    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
773        """Embed a batch of texts."""
774        embeddings = self._model.encode(texts)
775        return [e.tolist() for e in embeddings]
776
777    def similarity(self, text1: str, text2: str) -> float:
778        """Cosine similarity between two texts."""
779        import numpy as np
780
781        embeddings = self._model.encode([text1, text2])
782        similarity = np.dot(embeddings[0], embeddings[1]) / (
783            np.linalg.norm(embeddings[0]) * np.linalg.norm(embeddings[1])
784        )
785        return float(similarity)
786
787    def search(
788        self,
789        query: str,
790        documents: list[str],
791        top_k: int = 5,
792    ) -> list[tuple[int, float]]:
793        """Return ``(doc_index, score)`` pairs for the top-k most similar docs."""
794        import numpy as np
795
796        query_embedding = self._model.encode(query)
797        doc_embeddings = self._model.encode(documents)
798
799        similarities = np.dot(doc_embeddings, query_embedding) / (
800            np.linalg.norm(doc_embeddings, axis=1) * np.linalg.norm(query_embedding)
801        )
802
803        top_indices = np.argsort(similarities)[::-1][:top_k]
804
805        return [(int(i), float(similarities[i])) for i in top_indices]

Sentence embedding model wrapper.

EmbeddingModel(model: Any, model_name: str, device: str) 
762    def __init__(self, model: Any, model_name: str, device: str) -> None:
763        self._model = model
764        self._model_name = model_name
765        self._device = device
def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]: 
767    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
768        """Embed a single text into a vector."""
769        embedding = self._model.encode(text)
770        return embedding.tolist()  # type: ignore[no-any-return]

Embed a single text into a vector.

def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: 
772    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
773        """Embed a batch of texts."""
774        embeddings = self._model.encode(texts)
775        return [e.tolist() for e in embeddings]

Embed a batch of texts.

def similarity(self, text1: str, text2: str) -> float: 
777    def similarity(self, text1: str, text2: str) -> float:
778        """Cosine similarity between two texts."""
779        import numpy as np
780
781        embeddings = self._model.encode([text1, text2])
782        similarity = np.dot(embeddings[0], embeddings[1]) / (
783            np.linalg.norm(embeddings[0]) * np.linalg.norm(embeddings[1])
784        )
785        return float(similarity)

Cosine similarity between two texts.

def search( self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_k: int = 5) -> list[tuple[int, float]]: 
787    def search(
788        self,
789        query: str,
790        documents: list[str],
791        top_k: int = 5,
792    ) -> list[tuple[int, float]]:
793        """Return ``(doc_index, score)`` pairs for the top-k most similar docs."""
794        import numpy as np
795
796        query_embedding = self._model.encode(query)
797        doc_embeddings = self._model.encode(documents)
798
799        similarities = np.dot(doc_embeddings, query_embedding) / (
800            np.linalg.norm(doc_embeddings, axis=1) * np.linalg.norm(query_embedding)
801        )
802
803        top_indices = np.argsort(similarities)[::-1][:top_k]
804
805        return [(int(i), float(similarities[i])) for i in top_indices]

Return (doc_index, score) pairs for the top-k most similar docs.

def check_hardware_requirements(model_name: str) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
808def check_hardware_requirements(model_name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
809    """Check whether the host hardware satisfies a model's requirements.
810
811    Returns a dict describing current vs required RAM and GPU state. Without
812    ``psutil`` installed the function returns a best-effort dict (``satisfied``
813    set to ``None``) rather than raising, and without ``torch`` the GPU branch
814    is skipped gracefully (no ``None >= float`` TypeError).
815    """
816    model_info = AVAILABLE_MODELS.get(model_name)
817    if not model_info:
818        return {"satisfied": False, "error": f"Unknown model: {model_name}"}
819
820    # Current RAM.
821    try:
822        import psutil
823
824        current_ram_gb = psutil.virtual_memory().total / (1024**3)
825    except ImportError:
826        return {
827            "satisfied": None,
828            "error": "psutil is required: pip install psutil",
829            "required_ram_gb": model_info.hardware.min_ram_gb,
830            "supports_cpu": model_info.hardware.supports_cpu,
831        }
832
833    # GPU detection (optional).
834    gpu_available = False
835    gpu_name: Optional[str] = None
836    gpu_vram_gb: Optional[float] = None
837
838    try:
839        import torch
840
841        if torch.cuda.is_available():
842            gpu_available = True
843            gpu_name = torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)
844            gpu_vram_gb = torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).total_memory / (1024**3)
845    except ImportError:
846        pass
847
848    ram_satisfied = current_ram_gb >= model_info.hardware.min_ram_gb
849
850    # GPU check only applies when the model requires GPU and cannot run on CPU.
851    # Guard against gpu_vram_gb being None (no torch / no CUDA) to avoid
852    # ``None >= float`` TypeError.
853    gpu_satisfied = True
854    if (
855        model_info.hardware.min_gpu_vram_gb is not None
856        and not model_info.hardware.supports_cpu
857    ):
858        if not gpu_available or gpu_vram_gb is None:
859            gpu_satisfied = False
860        else:
861            gpu_satisfied = gpu_vram_gb >= model_info.hardware.min_gpu_vram_gb
862
863    return {
864        "satisfied": ram_satisfied and gpu_satisfied,
865        "current_ram_gb": round(current_ram_gb, 1),
866        "required_ram_gb": model_info.hardware.min_ram_gb,
867        "recommended_ram_gb": model_info.hardware.recommended_ram_gb,
868        "gpu_available": gpu_available,
869        "gpu_name": gpu_name,
870        "gpu_vram_gb": round(gpu_vram_gb, 1) if gpu_vram_gb is not None else None,
871        "required_gpu_vram_gb": model_info.hardware.min_gpu_vram_gb,
872        "supports_cpu": model_info.hardware.supports_cpu,
873    }

Check whether the host hardware satisfies a model's requirements.

Returns a dict describing current vs required RAM and GPU state. Without psutil installed the function returns a best-effort dict (satisfied set to None) rather than raising, and without torch the GPU branch is skipped gracefully (no None >= float TypeError).
